quotes.quote: This can be compared with the amount of energy absorbed in the same line from a Seyfert 1 galaxy; for NGC 3783 this quantity is 5 10^ 4 Kasp02 . Correcting for the difference in distance, this would imply that, if the same emission line gas seen in NGC 1068 is also present in NGC 3783, then the ratio of line flux absorbed from the observed spectrum to emission is 8. That is, the apparent flux absorbed in the O VIII L line, and likely other lines as well, is actually greater than observed by 10 -- 15 . The O VIII L line in NGC 3783 shows signs of this with an apparent weak P-Cygni emission on the red edge of the trough, though this is not apparent in most other lines in that spectrum.

sources.summary: NGC 3783 is a barred ring spiral with a compact Seyfert 1 nucleus, strong coronal-line emission, and an extensive low-surface-brightness ENLR.

quotes.quote: NGC 3783: This barred ring spiral has been imaged with HST in the continuum by Malkan98 , and the Seyfert1 nucleus in O 3 ] by Schmitt03a . However, the [ O 3 ] is almost point-like in the HST image. The measured [ O 3 ] luminosity L = 41.08 agrees closely with our measurement agrees closely with our measurement - L = 41.12 . We find this compact nucleus, strong in coronal line emission, to be surrounded by an extensive low-surface brightness ENLR 18 arcsec, or 3.8 kpc in diameter, effectively filling the central region out to the star forming ring, marked by \ regions.

sources.summary: NGC 3783 has dust-consistent Fe K constraints and is among the best-constrained sources, with high-S/N data resolving its narrow line even at 99% confidence.

quotes.quote: For 9 of the 13 AGN, the measurements or limits on \ are fully consistent with . These are NGC 3783, NGC 4151, NGC 4593, NGC 5548, Mrk 509, 3C 273, IRAS 13349+2438, NGC 4051 and IC 4329A.
Fig. fig:comparison shows that in many of the sources with the best constraints on \ (e.g. NGC 4151, NGC 5548, NGC 3783), \ is well matched to .

Whereas much narrower lines can be deconvolved given enough S/N in the data (e.g. see the case of NGC 3783 which has the best S/N and a resolved line even at 99 confidence), this may not be possible if the S/N is weaker.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3783 as a Seyfert with short-lived recurrent X-ray obscuration events, UV broad absorption troughs, and an inclination around 40 degrees.

quotes.quote: Blueshifted broad absorption troughs in the UV spectra were also found for the short-lived X-ray obscuration events in December 2016 for NGC 3783 meh17 . Note that X-ray obscuration events in NGC 3783 are also recurrent kaa18 .
These column densities are large enough to produce blueshifted absorption troughs in the HST/COS spectra for NGC 3227, similar to those in NGC 5548 kaa14 and NGC 3783 meh17, kri19a .
For comparison, the inclination angles for both NGC 3783 and NGC 5548 is 40^ lyr13,fis13,pan14 .

sources.summary: The paper cites GRAVITY mapping of NGC 3783, reporting a rotating disk-like BLR, radial cloud distribution, black-hole mass, and consistency with reverberation mapping.

quotes.quote: Very high resolution interferometry observations of 3C 273, IRAS 09149-6206, and NGC 3783 have been obtained with the GRAVITY instrument using the European Southern Observatory Very Large Telescope Interferometer (VLTI). These data suggest that all 3 AGN have a rotating, disk-like BLR. From the broad Paschen- line of the quasar 3C 273, Sturm2018SpatiallyScale inferred R_ BLR =0.12 0.03 pc and M_ = 2.6 1.1 10^8 M_ . Amorim2020The091496206 spatially resolved the broad Br line in IRAS 09149-6206 and inferred R_ BLR =0.075 pc and M_ 10^8 M _ . More recently, Amorim2021TheRegion used VLTI data to map broad Br in NGC 3783 and found a BLR with a radial distribution of clouds within R_ BLR 0.014 pc and then in Amorim2021A3783 reported M_ =2.54^ +0.90 _ 0.72 10^7 M _ . For all 3 objects, the BLR measurements obtained from GRAVITY are consistent with the R-L relation and reverberation mapping studies of those same AGN.

sources.summary: The paper cannot distinguish cold from warm absorption for NGC 3783, but cites previous obscuring-event studies and confirmed two-phase warm absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 3783 According to the AIC, we are unable to determine whether the warm or cold absorber yield a better fit for this source. There are many previous publications on this AGN including reports on obscuring events. Based on RGS data, Bl2002 , reported the detection of a two-phase warm absorber. This result is confirmed by Mao2019 .

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra grating spectroscopy of NGC 3783, describing it as a bright Seyfert 1 galaxy with strong warm-absorber features and a variable X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 3783 is one of the best-studied and brightest Seyfert 1 galaxies ( V 13.5 mag). It has some of the strongest X-ray absorption features around 0.7--1.5 keV known for a Seyfert 1; these have been typically attributed to O 7 (739 eV) and O 8 (871 eV) edges indicating the presence of a ``warm absorber.'' NGC 3783 has been studied in detail in the X-ray band with ROSAT (Turner et al. 1993) and ASCA (e.g., George et al. 1998) as well as with the new generation of X-ray observatories, Chandra (Kaspi et al. 2000a, 2001) and XMM-Newton (J. Blustin et al., in preparation). Its 2--10 keV spectrum is fitted by a power law with photon index 1.7 -1.8, the 2--10 keV flux varies in the range (4 - 9) 10^ 11 , and its mean X-ray luminosity is 1.5 10^ 43 \ (for H_0=70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and q_0=0.5 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3783 to illustrate photoionized soft-X-ray plasma, inner-shell absorption features, and an unusually large warm-absorber column density among Seyfert comparison objects.

quotes.quote: In at least one source for which an extremely high signal-to-noise spectrum is available (NGC 3783 -- kaspi02 ), however, there are clear detections of a number of \ from different ions, thereby demonstrating the dominance of photoionization over collisional ionization.
These features have been clearly detected in at least two objects -- an infrared quasar IRAS 13349+2348 sako01 and a \ galaxy NGC 3783 kaspi01 - as well as in the laboratory chenais00 .

NGC 3783 harbors, by far, the largest column density warm absorber observed to date. From several \ \ observations, the total equivalent hydrogen column density in this object is estimated to be N_H 4 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 kaspi02 .

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 3783 as the best high-resolution warm-absorber case, describing its column density, outflow velocity, absorber distance, and absorber-structure interpretations.

quotes.quote: The 900 ks Chandra HETGS spectrum of NGC 3783 is the high resolution spectrum with the highest quality currently available, and it has been extensively used to investigate the properties of the warm absorber kaspi02,netzer03,krongold03 . It was found that, at least in the case of NGC 3783, the warm absorber has a column density of the order of a few times 10^ 22 cm^ 2 , it is outflowing with velocities ranging from a few to several 100 km s^ 1 and it is located between 0.2 pc and 3 pc from the ionizing, nuclear source. According to and matching the several absorption lines observed in the spectrum of NGC 3783 requires the presence of two or three different phases of the absorbing medium, at different temperatures and different ionization stage, but in pressure equilibrium. However, pointed out that multi-temperature components arise naturally in a single medium as a consequence of the stratification of the ionization structure of each cloud exposed to the nuclear source. In particular, could fit the various absorption lines observed in NGC 3783 with a single medium, without the need for different, separate components of the absorbing medium.

sources.summary: The review says NGC 3783 had been tentatively interpreted as having a second low-frequency PSD bend, but later observations indicate it is a soft-state AGN.

quotes.quote: There had been a tentative suggestion that there might be a second bend, at low frequencies, in the PSD of NGC 3783 NGC 3783 and hence that it might be a hard state system markowitz03a . As NGC 3783 does not have a particularly low accretion rate ( 0.07 m _ E ), this suggestion was a little puzzling. However our further observations summons07 show that, in fact, NGC 3783 is another soft state AGN.

sources.summary: NGC 3783 is described as having broad iron-line variability whose rms excess is similar to the time-averaged broad line, consistent with line and continuum varying together.

quotes.quote: However, a further three observations, NGC 3783, MCG-6-30-15(1) and NGC 5548(2) almost certainly have broad line variability as well, based on moderately significant excesses adjacent to the more significant bins.
MCG-6-30-15(1) has a variable, broad iron line that is as strong as the time-averaged line profile, as does NGC 3783 (although its iron line red wing is not as strong as in MCG-6-30-15).
The excess in the rms spectrum of NGC 3783 is similar in strength to the broad iron line in the time-averaged spectrum, so in the accretion disk model, the line and continuum vary together.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3783's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3783 is a barred face-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0097 ( D 48.4 Mpc) with a well-studied variable Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which also belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. The nucleus is unresolved at radio wavelengths morganti_radio_1999,kinney_jet_2000 , embedded within a halo-like extended NLR with 1.9 ( 0.4 kpc) size schmitt_hubble_2003 . The first MIR observations of NGC 3873 were carried out by , followed by , , , and .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3783 analyses, noting prior use of an additional dataset and reporting two warm absorber components detected in this work.

quotes.quote: NGC 3783 - studied the same dataset as ours but also added another 138 dataset taken almost simultaneously. The slope =1.7 and the WA 30 . We detected two WA components.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3783 among compact, variable radio-quiet AGN targets, noting X-ray and near-infrared variability plus a large 95 GHz excess relative to low-frequency spectra.

quotes.quote: Six of the targets, namely NGC 3783, NGC 5548, NGC 7469, NGC 3227, Ark 564, and Mrk 766, are highly variable in the X-rays according to the extensive monitoring of markowitz04 .
MR 2251-178 as well as NGC 3783 show evidence for variable emission in the near-IR lira11 .
The excess is large ( 4 - 7 ) for NGC 3783, ARK 564, and NGC 3227, and smaller (but still > 1 ) for MR 2251-178, NGC 5506, and NGC 7469.

sources.summary: NGC 3783 is included among the bright, well-studied AGNs whose published parsec-scale elongated mid-infrared emission is recovered by the authors' analysis.

quotes.quote: Elongated mid-IR emission on the parsec-scale was found previously in four of the sources in this sample: NGC 424 2012ApJ...755..149H , NGC 3783 2013ApJ...771...87H , NGC 1068 2014A&A...565A..71L and the Circinus galaxy 2014A&A...563A..82T . These are among the brightest and very well studied sources ( 30 (u,v) points) where elongation and position angle can be constrained well. Our results (Tab. table:Tparelong ) are in excellent agreement with the published ones, confirming the reliability of the detected elongations.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3783 as a Seyfert 1.2, reports line ratios consistent with other unreddened Seyfert 1s, and finds its BLR and NLR extinction consistent with zero.

quotes.quote: NGC 3783 : [ O III ] /H = 0.7 in this object and thus we classify it as a Seyfert 1.2 (noting that based on this object would be a Seyfert 1.5).

The line ratios we observe for the unreddened Seyfert 1s MGC-05--14--012, NGC 3783 and NGC 4593 are in good agreement with these other observational studies; as is the predicted line ratio for NGC 6814, MCG--06--30--015 and MCG-05--23--16, the latter based on the best fit to the H and Paschen lines, and for which we derived a substantial extinction of A_V(BLR) = 7.7 mag.
NGC 3783 : barr83 estimated A _V = 0.56 ^ +0.37 _ 0.50 from the UV continuum, which, like our estimates of A_V(BLR) and A_V(NLR) , is consistent with zero extinction.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC3783 as an AGN with strong polar mid-infrared emission, successful disk-plus-polar modeling, and a warm absorber viewed along the outflow-cone edge.

quotes.quote: The extended emission accounts for a significant part of the point-like source seen in most single-telescope mid-IR imaging data, reaching 60-80 in the interferometrically best-covered AGN in NGC1068, the Circinus galaxy, and NGC3783.
They successfully and simultaneously reproduced the total and resolved emission in NGC3783 Hon17 and the Circinus galaxy Sta17,Sta19 .
Tur93,Ric10 , with NGC 3783 being such a candidate object with a warm absorber where modelling of the mid-IR interferometry implies a viewing angle along the edges of the outflow cone Hon17 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3783 has asymmetric hot-dust structure and reports Gaussian size measurements that agree with earlier VLTI/AMBER thin-ring results.

quotes.quote: The closure phases measured for NGC 3783 are shown as a separate histogram, are always < 0^ , and clearly indicative of asymmetry. For consistency with the other sources, we report size measurements for NGC 3783 from 1D Gaussian model fits, even though the model is inconsistent with the observed non-zero closure phases.
VLTI/AMBER observations found a thin ring radius of 0.74 0.23 mas for NGC 3783 (Gaussian HWHM of 0.56 0.17 mas), consistent with our result of 0.42 0.06 mas.

sources.summary: The paper uses warm-absorber data for NGC 3783 in a Seyfert sample to infer ambient circumnuclear medium density profiles of roughly n ^ 1.7 to n ^ 2.15.

quotes.quote: We study the correlation between the outflow velocity and ionization parameter of WAs in each of the five Seyfert 1 galaxies (NGC 3227, NGC 3783, NGC 4051, NGC 4593, and NGC 5548), inferring that the density profile of the ACM is between n ^ 1.7 and n ^ 2.15 ( n is number density and is distance) from 0.01 pc to pc scales in these five AGNs.

The density profile indexes k of the ACM in the five Seyfert 1 galaxies of our sample (NGC 3227, NGC 3783, NGC 4051, NGC 4593, and NGC 5548) are within the range of k for the ACM in TDEs between 1 and 2.5 ; Alexander2020 (see Figure fig:kdis ).
According to the fitting results of the v_ out relation, we infer that the density profile of the ACM is between n ^ 1.7 and n ^ 2.15 from 0.01 pc to pc scales in these five AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3783 as a Seyfert 1 case where UV absorbers coexist with X-ray obscuration and match the modeled spectral features.

quotes.quote: Another similar observational signature showing a coexistence of UV absorbers and X-ray obscurers has been reported in NGC 3783 ( z=0.00973 ) with the observed absorption, emission and extinction effects being simultaneously analyzed, resulting in nine distinct X-ray warm absorber components e.g. Mao19 . Multi-epoch synergistic observations of NGC 3783 have further revealed the presence of X-ray-obscuring column ( N_H 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) in \ spectra simultaneous with the UV absorbers (e.g. \ line up to 6,000 km s ^ 1 ) in the observed /COS data Kriss19 .
These spectral features, X-ray obscuration and UV absorbers, thus appear to be consistent with multi-wavelength observations of NGC 5548 and NGC 3783 (e.g. Kaastra14 ; Kriss19 ; M22).

gmw_00000198.name: NGC 3982
gmw_00000198.overview: NGC 3982 is a face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:0709.3505, arXiv:1310.2770] hosting a low-luminosity Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1303.3511, arXiv:2403.06972] that is heavily obscured [arXiv:1508.05065, arXiv:2403.06972]. The galaxy features ongoing star formation [arXiv:2104.05736] that is suppressed in its central regions due to AGN feedback [arXiv:2209.04914], alongside abundant ionized extraplanar gas [arXiv:2104.05736].

sources.summary: NGC 3982 is described as a Seyfert 1.9 with a weak AGN; its CO modeling favors lower-temperature gas than the rest of the analyzed sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 3982 NGC 3982 is a Seyfert 1.9 Ho1997 located at 20.6 Mpc. The hard X-ray luminosity of this galaxy is < 2.5 10 ^ 39 erg s ^ 1 and the Fe K feature at 6.4 keV is not detected Guainazzi2005 . This suggests that NGC 3982 hosts a relatively low luminosity AGN. Moreover, the luminosities of the high-ionization mid-IR [ O 4 ] and [ Ne 5 ] emission lines are about 2 10 ^ 39 erg s ^ 1 Tommasin2010 , low compared to those of other Seyfert galaxies (see Figure 2 of Pereira2010c ) and confirming that the AGN in NGC 3982 is not very luminous.

The best-fit model for NGC 3982 has a higher density and lower temperature than the rest of the galaxies (see Table tab:radexfit and Figure fig:radex_model ). No CO lines with J_ up >7 are detected, so our fit could be biased toward low kinetic temperatures. However, the CO J=4-3 CO J=7-6 ratio in NGC 3982 is more than a factor of two higher than in the other galaxies in the sample. This implies a lower kinetic temperature for a fixed density and, therefore, the estimated lower temperature is likely real and not a consequence of the limited sensitivity of the \ spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3982 as the sample's most complicated profile: an II.o ring break plus outer Type III disk light traced by spiral arms.

quotes.quote: This is perhaps the most complicated profile in our sample. The profile out to 50 is a good example of the II.o form, with a ring right at the break. This is one of three galaxies with ``extreme outer-ring'' profiles; in this case, the profile between the end of the bar and the outer ring is not exponential.

(Due to the small size of this ring, rc1 described it as an ``inner ring;'' but since it lies at almost exactly twice the bar radius, it is far more likely to be an OLR-associated outer ring.) Beyond 50 , the profile changes to a shallower exponential, forming a Type III profile. Although the galaxy is face-on, making determination of the outer geometry difficult, there is a clear pair of spiral arms at > 50 , indicating that the outermost light is still from the disk (Figure fig:type3d-spirals ).

sources.summary: The paper models ionised extraplanar gas in NGC 3982, finding a late-type Seyfert 2 system with abundant lagging, inflowing, high-dispersion extraplanar gas.

quotes.quote: In this work we focus on two galaxies from the DMS sample, NGC 3982 and NGC 4152. We select these two galaxies because they have sufficient S/N to reveal the relatively weak EPG emission, and their structures are well-resolved under the SparsePak spatial resolution. Besides, their position--velocity (pv) diagrams along their major axes have emission features called `beard' patterns Schaap00,Fraternali01 that deviate from the bulk rotation and extended towards the systemic velocity.

NGC3982 is a late-type Seyfert 2 galaxy Cardamone07 with a relatively low inclination angle ( 26 2 ) and a distance of 21.6 Mpc Tully13 . It is a high surface brightness member of the Ursa Major cluster, with on-going star-forming activity Martinsson13 , making it an ideal target for the kinematic study of EPG.

Our results indicate that there is abundant ionised EPG in NGC 3982, with lagging rotation, vertical and radial inflow, and large velocity dispersion We note that the flux-weighted velocity dispersion should be lowered by a factor 0.7 with respect to the mass-weighted values quoted in Table tab:result , due to the difference between H i and H line profiles discussed in Sec. sec:modifications . .

sources.summary: The study presents NGC 3982 as a nearby Seyfert where multiwavelength data show central star formation suppression, outer/ring enhancement, and ionized-gas disturbances consistent with AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: A pilot study on NGC 3982 shows star formation suppression in the central regions of the galaxy, likely due to negative AGN feedback, and enhanced star formation in the outer regions. The case of NGC 3982 could be observational evidence of AGN feedback operating in a Seyfert galaxy.

Based on a multi-wavelength analysis, we present evidence for star formation suppression in the central region of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 3982. As revealed from line diagnostic analysis, the galaxy's central region with reduced star formation is dominated by AGN+composite emission. This is further supported by an AGN jet or outflow revealed from radio and gas velocity dispersion map analysis. The most plausible explanation for the observed scenario presented here is suppression of star formation in the central regions due to feedback from recent AGN activity.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3982 as a nearby heavily obscured low-luminosity AGN, using XMM-Newton and NuSTAR fits to compare model-dependent column densities and luminosities.

quotes.quote: To assess the impact of model-specific parameter dependencies, we present a case study of the nearby heavily obscured low-luminosity AGN NGC 3982, which has a variety of column density estimations reported in the literature. We fit the same broadband XMM-Newton + NuSTAR spectra of the source with five unique obscuration models and generate posterior parameter distributions for each. By using global parameter exploration, we traverse the full prior-defined parameter space to accurately reproduce complex posterior shapes and inter-parameter degeneracies. The unique model posteriors for the line-of-sight column density are broadly consistent, predicting Compton-thick N_ H >1.5 10^ 24 cm^ 2 at the 3 confidence level. The posterior median intrinsic X-ray luminosity in the 2--10 keV band however was found to differ substantially, with values in the range log L_ 2-10 keV / erg s ^ 1 = 40.9--42.1 for the individual models.

In this paper, we test the effect of non-unique spectral prescriptions for the obscurer with a case study of the Low-Luminosity ( L_ 2-10 keV 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 ) Compton-thick AGN candidate NGC 3982, a Seyfert 2 AGN located at a Hubble distance 18.91 1.33 Mpc https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu (z = 0.00371; Martinsson2013 ).

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 3982 from the reliable sample because its few-count hard X-ray detection is host-contaminated, though MIR-X-ray predictions remain consistent with heavy obscuration.

quotes.quote: NGC 3982 The nucleus of NGC 3982 was detected only with a few counts above 2 keV guainazzi_x-ray_2005,lamassa_uncovering_2011 . It is embedded in extended host emission, which heavily contaminates lower resolution X-ray data from panessa_x-ray_2006 and brightman_xmm-newton_2011-1 . Still, the nucleus might be heavily obscured ghosh_chandra_2007 . Therefore, is highly uncertain, and we have to exclude NGC 3982 from the reliable sample. The predictions from the MIR--X-ray correlation are consistent with heavy obscuration.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 3982 with XMM-Newton and NuSTAR spectra, with some models indicating Compton-thin absorption and others supporting a Compton-thick column density.

quotes.quote: NGC 3982: We fitted the \ EPIC-pn and the \ FPMA/FPMB spectra of this source using the four models that are described in Section sec:spectralfit . We tied all parameters between the spectra of the two observatories. The Pexmon and MYTC models suggest a Cth source with N_ H = 6 10^ 23 cm^ 2 . However, MYTD-1NH and MYTD-1NH suggest a CT column density with N_ H = (5.3 3.3) 10^ 24 cm^ 2 and (4.5 3.1 ) 10^ 24 cm^ 2 , respectively. We note that found that the source is a CT candidate based on the F_ X /F_ OIII vs F_ OIII /F_ IR diagnostic.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC3982 as the spiral-galaxy source profile in mock strong-lensing images, scaling archival HST/ACS observations and describing its brightness covariance with a Gaussian-kernel case.

quotes.quote: We use three distinctly different brightness profiles for the lensed source, representing the broad range of galaxies that can be lensed: an analytic profile and archival high resolution observations of two real galaxies, NGC3982 (a spiral galaxy) and NGC2623 (a merger).

For NGC3982 and NGC2623, we use high resolution archival observations taken with the ACS instrument onboard the Hubble Space Telescope (HST), scaled to roughly much the 1 arcsec extent of the analytical profile and having the same peak brightness. The high resolution image that we use for each source is shown in the first column of Fig. fig:src_img_res . These sources have distinct covariance properties described accurately by Mat e rn kernels Stein1999 : C(d|l, ) = 2^ 1- ( ) 2 d l ^ K_ 2 d l , where d is the distance between any two points of the source, K_ is the modified Bessel function of the second kind, and l is a characteristic coherence scale. Special cases occur for =1/2 and , i.e. an exponential and Gaussian kernel, which is the case for NGC2623 and NGC3982 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 3982, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 3982 As noted in the main text, the parameters, flux and Fe K EW values listed are from the model fits to Chandra spectrum only as extended emission is present in the XMM-Newton field of view. The spectrum was best-fit by an absorbed power law model with a thermal component, using the C-statistic on data grouped by 3 counts. We used ZGAUSS to test for the presence of an Fe K line, but the EW was unconstrained in both the global and local fit, where in the latter, it was necessary to group by 1 count per bin to fit the continuum.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3982's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3982 is a face-on barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 21.4 2.2 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an AGN optically classified either as Sy 1.9 or Sy 2 trippe_multi-wavelength_2010 . It features an unresolved NLR gonzalez_delgado_h_1997 and unresolved radio core ho_radio_2001 . The first ground-based MIR observations of NGC 3982 were carried out after but the nucleus was not detected edelson_broad-band_1987,devereux_spatial_1987 .

sources.summary: The paper describes WR 214 as a sub-L* blue galaxy near the absorber in velocity and virial impact parameter, with extended emission-line morphology but a low star formation rate.

quotes.quote: This galaxy (WR 214 ) is at = 230 kpc ( 1.2R_ vir ) and v = +75 from the absorber. The galaxy has an extended morphology with an emission line dominated spectrum and g - = 0.656 color, consistent with it being a blue galaxy Blanton2003 . However, the integrated luminosity in H only suggests a star-formation rate of SFR = 0.03 M _ yr ^ 1 Kennicutt1998 , which is much less compared to starburst galaxies in the local universe such as M82 10 M _ yr ^ 1 , Mangano1978 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3982 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3982 : J115628.3+550729 Close to Ursa Major Cluster but membership questionable . Galaxy exhibits a detached, narrow, starforming spiral arm embedded into the very outer, symmetric disk towards south-west. The final profile beyond the central part is smooth and straight down to 55 where it clearly starts upbending, classified as , which is consistent with the profile by .

gmw_00000199.name: NGC 3998
gmw_00000199.overview: NGC 3998 is a nearby lenticular [arXiv:1605.04795, arXiv:1907.05879] fast-rotator galaxy [arXiv:1605.04795] hosting a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1907.05879] classified as a Type I LINER [arXiv:0905.1266]. The galaxy is gas-rich but exhibits a highly disturbed neutral hydrogen morphology [arXiv:1907.08632], featuring extended, S-shaped radio lobes [arXiv:1912.04812] and a polar warp that indicates past tidal interactions [arXiv:1907.08632].

sources.summary: NGC 3998 has a variable UV point excluded from modeling; its SED is fit with an ADAF/truncated-disk model constrained by IR limits, X-rays, and Fe K absence.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 The shortest wavelength (1750 ) UV data point in the SED of NGC 3998 presented in EHF10 is anomalously high because of variability Devereux11n3998 . As discussed by Devereux this data point was obtained many years before all the other observations, when the source was much brighter. For this reason, we exclude this UV measurement from our analysis and plots. In our models we adopted a large outer radius for the ADAF, but smaller radii are not ruled out by the data. For instance, we are also able to obtain a reasonable fit to the SED with r_ tr =500 and m _ o =10^ 3 .

This model is consistent with the IR upper limits and accounts for the X-ray data. The transition radius cannot be much smaller than this value, otherwise the emission of the truncated thin disk would exceed the IR upper limits. Furthermore, as noted by Ptak04 , the lack of Fe K line emission also suggests that the value of r_ tr is not so small.

sources.summary: The paper uses RXTE data for faint NGC 3998, fitting a Galactic-absorbed power law and finding photon index anticorrelated with intrinsic luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 As there were few Swift data available for NGC 3998, we instead used the RXTE data from SkipperThesis , for which a large number of flux-binned spectra could be produced. The RXTE data are of quite low S/N, as NGC 3998 is a very faint source. The spectrum can be fitted reasonably well by a power law absorbed by the galactic absorbing column of 1.05 10^ 21 cm^ 2 , and shows no excesses which imply that this is not a good model. The fit is not improved by the addition of a further absorber, possibly because the RXTE does not extend below 3 keV, so is only sensitive to high absorbing columns. If it is assumed that the spectrum is absorbed and that the source of the spectral variability is changes in absorption instead of a changing , a significantly worse fit is obtained.

This is consistent with previous spectral studies of this source Ptak2004,Awaki1991, Younes2011 . The values obtained for the photon index in previous studies are all within the range found in this model ( 1.87 - 2.50 ). In the best-fitting model, the photon index shows a clear anticorrelation with the intrinsic luminosity of the system.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3998 as a low-luminosity AGN example where uncorrected host-galaxy UV contamination lowers the measured hardness, even after an empirical correction.

quotes.quote: However, the only source from his study that fulfils our strict filtering criteria for creating the final sample is NGC 3998. Its hardness from the analysis by Maoz2007 is H_ Maoz, NGC 3998 = 0.86 . The hardness from our main sample without any host-galaxy correction is H_ NGC 3998 = 0.21 0.01 , which is significantly lower. This clearly indicates that the disc luminosity is largely overestimated in such sources.

Figure dfld_HG shows the hardness-luminosity diagram after the applied host-galaxy correction using eqs. eq_uvsfr and eq_xsfr . The plot is shown in the linear scale for hardness to focus on changes in the hard part of the diagram, and can therefore be directly compared to the right panel of Fig. dfld_rmpos . The radio-loud sources moved to the right in the diagram, towards higher hardness as expected. For NGC 3998, we get a `corrected' value of the hardness H_ NGC 3998 = 0.48 0.01 , which is, however, still insufficient.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3998 as a nearby S0 galaxy with a low-power radio AGN and flat-spectrum core, finding a thermal ADAF fit resolvable by EHT and partly by GMVA.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 is a nearby (Distance =13.1 Mpc) S0 galaxy located at the outskirts of the Ursa Major group. The galaxy hosts a low power radio AGN with a flat spectrum radio core. The central black hole has a mass M_ BH =7.9 10^8 M_ . This galaxy has been studied well with various probes in the X-ray using Swift, XMM-Newton and NuSTAR shown with various markers in the left panel of Fig. [ fig:NGC3998spec ] . These observations were taken between October 25 to October 27, 2016.

With the given data points, we obtain a good fit to the data when the emission is from thermal electrons in the ADAF. The model of the jet emission here predicts a weaker contribution to the emission from the jet than from the ADAF. With this model, the ADAF appears to be observable both by the EHT but partially by the GMVA as the right panel of Fig. [ fig:NGC3998spec ] shows.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3998 as a RIAF comparison, rescaling its nuclear SED and noting its optical-to-X-ray similarity to NGC 4235 and NGC 4639.

quotes.quote: To illustrate this point, the crosses in all panels of Fig. fig:seds show the nuclear SED of NGC 3998. Data were taken from Table 4 of Ptak \ (2004). In the case there were multiple measurements at a certain wavelength, we chose the ones derived from the smallest aperture size. The NGC 3998 SED is rescaled to the optical luminosity of the objects in our sample.

Figure fig:seds shows that the optical to X--ray SEDs of NGC 3998, NGC 4235 and NGC 4639 (at least) are almost identical. The NGC 3998 SED is consistent with RIAF models. Ptak \ (2004) found that, although a ``pure" thin-disc model does not account the mid-IR emission in NGC 3998, a truncated thin-disc (at around 300 Schwarzschild radii), surrounding an inner RIAF, fitted its mid-IR to X--ray spectrum well (when the accretion rate is 10^ 3 of the Eddington limit).

sources.summary: NGC 3998 is a non-reverberating dwarf Seyfert with a giant BLR far from the usual size-luminosity relation; the paper calls its BLR the largest in the six-object sample.

quotes.quote: In the present contribution, high quality spectra obtained with the Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) are used to constrain the size of the BLR in the dwarf Seyfert nuclei of M81, NGC 3998, NGC 4203, NGC 3227, NGC 4051, and NGC 3516.

There are presently six LLAGNs whose BLR size has been deduced by modeling the shape of the H Balmer lines including three non-reverberating dwarf Seyferts (M81, NGC 3998 and NGC 4203), and three reverberating ones (NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 4051). Some basic observables are listed in Table 1, and their BLR size-luminosity relationship is illustrated in Figure 1. The three non-reverberating dwarf Seyferts lie far from the correlation defined by reverberating AGNs. The other three LLAGN are reverberating, and their inner radii consistently lie on the correlation defined by other reverberating AGN. This latter result is important as it shows that the line profile fitting method does yield a size that agrees with the reverberation size.

Figure 2 graphically illustrates that the BLR in the non-reverberating dwarf Seyfert NGC 3998 is by far the largest of the six objects included in this study. It is also the first AGN to be identified with broad forbidden lines including [ O iii ] 4959,5007 and [ S ii ] 6718,6732. That the forbidden [ S ii ] lines have the same width as the permitted H Balmer lines allows a useful limit to be placed on the average electron density of the BLR gas corresponding to 7 10 ^3 cm ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The LOFAR study describes NGC 3998 as a nearby, gas-rich, low-power radio AGN with rare extended S-shaped lobes that may still be fed by the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We address this by studying one of these objects, the radio source hosted by NGC 3998 In this paper, we will refer to it by the name of its optical identification , which provides a unique case-study. NGC 3998 represents one of the rare examples of a low-power radio AGN ( P _ 1400 MHz 10^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 ) with extended radio emission ( 20 kpc) In this paper, we assume a distance of of 13.7 Mpc ( z=0.0035 ) for NGC 3998 Cappellari11 .

As described in , the radio source in NGC 3998 shows two S-shaped lobes (see Fig. fig:franketal ) of low surface-brightness ( 5 mJy/arcmin ^2 at 1400 MHz). In addition, a bright central component is also observed. Because of this, the radio source is characterised by an extremely high core dominance (S _ core /S _ ext = 8 or S _ core /S _ tot = 0.9 , derived at 1400 MHz, see frank2016 ), much higher than typically found for FRIs morganti1997,laing2014 and in the radio sources of lower power studied by . From a morphological point of view, the lobes of NGC 3998 have characteristics which may suggest that they are still actively being fed by the nucleus (see frank2016 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3998 as a LINER with an unobscured nucleus, ionized gas disk, radio lobes, weak reflection signatures, and a Compton-thin torus fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 This object is spectroscopically classified as a LINER 1980Heckman, 1997Ho , and no significant broad-line polarization 1999barth . HST /WFPC2 optical images revealed an unobscured nucleus and the presence of a bright circumnuclear ionized gas disk 2000Pogge,2018cazzoli . In radio, it contains a nucleus 1984hummel displaying a weak jet-like northern structure 2002filho and according to 2016frank , the radio source in NGC 3998 shows two S-shaped lobes.

They found that the reflection strength is very weak with an upper-limit of R _ f <0.10 and no significant iron K line emission is detected, either. They suggest that there is little surrounding matter around the nucleus.

In the present work, the data were fitted with PEXMON and the result is consistent with a reflection fraction R _ f <0.15. Using borus02 we found that the data is consistent with a Compton thin torus (with (N_ H,refl ) =22.50 _ 22.28 ^ 23.11 ) covering more than 20 of the sky.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3998 as a Type I LINER newly observed by Swift and reports first-time significant X-ray variability on rapid, inter-orbit, and day timescales.

quotes.quote: Our sample includes the brightest LINERs in the UV sample, M81, NGC 3998, NGC 4203, and NGC 4579, that are all Type I LINERs (i.e., with detected broad H components) according to . Data for two of these sources, M81 and NCG 4203 were already available in the Swift archive: M81 (net exposure of 33.7 ks) has an X-ray Telescope spectrum of excellent quality and even the suggestion of an Fe K line; NGC 4203 (5.3 ks) has a good XRT spectrum, although slightly under exposed with respect to M81. NGC 3998 and NGC 4579 were observed with Swift for the first time (27.4 and 20.8 ks, respectively).

NGC 3998 also exhibits rapid intra-orbit variability as seen in M81. Inter-orbit variability is clearly seen both at hard and soft X-rays and this is nearly a factor of 4 (Fig. liners:fig:xlc_all d). The day time scale variability is also remarkable (Fig. liners:fig:xlc_all e). This is unprecedented for this source, that has always been reported not to be variable in X-rays

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3998 as a nearly face-on lenticular fast rotator with low central dark matter, I-band M/L 4.7, bulge and disc components, and counter-rotation.

quotes.quote: We observed the lenticular galaxy NGC 3998 with the Mitchell Integral-Field Spectrograph and extracted line-of-sight velocity distributions out to 3 half-light radii. We constructed collisionless orbit models in order to constrain NGC 3998's dark and visible structure, using kinematics from both the Mitchell and SAURON instruments. We find NGC 3998 to be almost axisymmetric, seen nearly face on with a flattened intrinsic shape - i.e., a face-on fast-rotator.

We find an I-band mass-to-light ratio of 4.7_ 0.45 ^ +0.32 in good agreement with previous spectral fitting results for this galaxy. Our best-fit orbit model shows a both a bulge and a disc component, with a non-negligible counter-rotating component also evident. We find that relatively little dark matter is needed to model this galaxy, with an inferred dark mass fraction of just (7.1^ +8.1 _ 7.1 ) within one half-light radius.

sources.summary: The paper excludes a variable anomalous UV point from NGC 3998's SED and models the remaining data with ADAF-dominated IR-to-X-ray emission plus radio emission from the jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 The shortest wavelength (1750 ) UV data point in the SED of NGC 3998 presented in EHF10 is anomalously high because of variability devereux11 . As discussed by Devereux this data point was obtained many years before all the other observations, when the source was much brighter. For this reason, we exclude this UV measurement from our analysis and plots. 0.3truein The left panel of Figure fig:n3998 shows an AD model in which the ADAF dominates the emission from the IR to the X-ray bands and explains well the available optical-UV and X-ray data. As usual, the jet dominates the radio emission.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3998's low-luminosity AGN infrared SED with dusty torus and optically thin dust shells, finding dust can match the IR luminosity and silicate emission features.

quotes.quote: In Figure f4 we show two dusty model fits to the IR SED of NGC 3998: optically thin dust emission produced with the DUSTY radiative transfer code Ivezic99 , and a clumpy torus spectrum obtained using the models of Nenkova08a and the Bayesclumpy fitting tool AsensioRamos09,AsensioRamos13 . Clumpy torus models have been successful in fitting the SEDs of numerous Seyfert galaxies e.g. Mason06,Honig10b,Alonso-Herrero11 , while the presence of the strong silicate emission features in this object M12 Sturm05 suggests the use of the optically thin dust model.

In the torus model in Figure f4 , the bolometric luminosity required to account for the IR emission of NGC 3998, L_ bol = 1 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 , is comparable to that estimated from the X-rays L_ bol 3 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 , assuming the bolometric correction factor of Ho09 . The N_H 8 10^ 22 cm^ 2 implied by the visual extinction through the clumpy torus, A_ V = 41 4 mag, is quite close to the observed N_H 2.3^ +3.2 _ 1.6 10^ 22 cm^ 2 Gonzalez-Martin09b , and the escape probability of an AGN-produced optical photon, P_ esc = 0.19 , is within the range spanned by the type 1 objects in Alonso-Herrero11 .

The best-fitting model (Figure f4 ) provides a reasonable description of the SED of NGC 3998, and the strength of the silicate emission features is comparable to those observed in the Spitzer spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3998's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998: The SDSS i- band decomposed bulge profile comes from Beifiori2012 , is calibrated to the I- band using the equation of Jordi2006 and gives R_e=5.2'' . This roughly agrees with the R_e=3.4'' of Laurikainen2010 . We prefer this solution to the multi-component 'best-fit' of Laesker2014 , which delivers R_e=1.8'' , since the size of the fitted bar seems implausible. Since only the (B-V) color was available in Hyperleda, we derived the (V-I) and the (R-I) colors by linearly fitting the (V-I) vs. (B-V) and (R-I) vs.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 3998 needs no scattering component, has a weak reflection strength, lacks significant iron-K emission, and likely has little nuclear surrounding matter.

quotes.quote: The base-line model without a scattering component well reproduces the spectra with ^2/d.o.f = 950.8/982. The obtained photon index, = 1.84 0.01 , is consistent with a previous study Ptak04 . We find that the reflection strength is very weak with an upper limit of R_ torus < 0.10 . No significant iron-K line emission is detected, either. These suggest that there is little surrounding matter around the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3998 as a non-barred S0 LINER with diffuse, lopsided, misaligned HI and ionized gas sharing the same orientation as the neutral gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 NGC 3998 is a non-barred S0 and well-studied low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) galaxy in a relatively populated group. In addition to NGC 3998, four other galaxies are detected in the new HI observations from the VLA including NGC 3972, NGC 3982, UGC 06919, and UGC 06988. Table  tab:n3998comps presents properties of the known companions to NGC 3998. The neutral gas in NGC 3998 is diffuse and not well organized. Fig.  fig:n3998sum shows that it lies in a roughly disc-like distribution with an apparent position angle that is perpendicular to the position angle of the stellar distribution.

sources.summary: The survey finds NGC 3998 has compact non-thermal synchrotron radio emission and describes it as an H I-rich early-type galaxy with disturbed H I morphology and a polar warp indicating tidal interactions.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3998 and Arp 102B, we measured the brightness temperature ( T_ b ) of the radio emission to investigate the power source. In general, T_ b of non-thermal emission is known to be higher ( T_ b > 10 ^ 7 K) than thermal emission ( T_ b 10 ^ 4 10 ^ 5 K) e.g., Middelberg2004, Bontempi2012 . Using the extent of the nuclear radio component and its flux density, the brightness temperature can be estimated by the following equation: T_ b (K) = 1.222 10 ^ 3 I ^ 2 _ maj _ min where I is the flux density in mJy, is the observing frequency in GHz, and is the size of the radio component in arcsec. For the unresolved compact sources, we measured the lower limit of brightness temperature using the synthesized beam size, yielding 10 ^ 8.4 K and 10 ^ 8.6 K for NGC 3998 and Arp 102B, respectively, i.e. sufficiently high to expect a non-thermal origin. Therefore we conclude that the radio emission detected in our VLBI image for these two cases also originates from non-thermal synchrotron radiation.

NGC 3998 is one of the H i richest systems among 166 early-type galaxies studied in a volume-limited H i survey, ATLAS 3D Cappellari2011 . Its H i morphology is highly disturbed Serra2012 , and a polar H i /H warp in the core region has been seen Frank2016 , indicating tidal interactions.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3998 as a LLAGN hosting a roughly 10^9 solar-mass SMBH with low accretion; its reported X-ray cutoff and softening are described as consistent with the RIAF model.

quotes.quote: The four LLAGNs differ in M_ BH and m . NGC 3516, NGC 4203, and NGC 5866 have M_ BH close to 10^8 , while NGC 3998 hosts a SMBH of M_ BH 10^9 . m is close to the critical accretion rate for NGC 3516, m 0.1 m_ crit for NGC 4203 and NGC 3998, and m 0.01 m_ crit for NGC 5866.

Recently, Ref. estimated the cutoff energy in X-ray spectrum in NGC 3998 to be around 100 keV using the NuSTAR and XMM-Newton data. However, they just measured a slight softening of the spectrum, which can be reconciled by our RIAF model. The Compton scattering makes a few bumps in the broad-band spectrum, which causes a softening in the X-ray band for NGC 3998 as seen in Figure fig:soft .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3998 to test its RIAF model, reproducing the hard-X-ray softening and arguing hard spectra below longer-wavelength data require components such as jets or outer disks.

quotes.quote: Ref. reported a softening feature in the hard X-ray band in NGC 3998, from which they claimed that the electron temperature is 30-40 keV. Our RIAF model can reproduce the softening feature in the NuSTAR band, as well as the Swift BAT data shown in Fig. fig:detail , despite a higher electron temperature. Ref. also provided the X-ray spectrum for NGC 4579, which has a higher m and does not show any softening feature. Our model also produces a hard power-law spectrum consistent with the NuSTAR data (see Fig. fig:detail ). In our RIAF model, the resulting spectra for NGC 3998 and NGC 4579 are relatively hard, and well below the longer wavelength data (radio, infrared/optical/ultraviolet, and soft X-rays). These should be attributed to other emission components, such as compact jets or outer accretion disks . Indeed, radio jets are observed in both objects .

sources.summary: The paper builds NGC 3998's AGN SED from literature data, excluding large-aperture host-contaminated measurements and using smaller apertures as AGN constraints.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998. -- To construct the \ of NGC 3998 we began from the extensive data tabulation of ptak04 . We excluded many of the measurements presented therein because they were obtained through extremely large apertures that encompass a substantial fraction of the host galaxy (e.g., from IRAS observations). Since the AGN in NGC 3998 is rather bright compared to other objects in our collection, we adopted measurements through apertures as large as 3 \ as fair measurements of the AGN luminosity. Measurements through apertures between 3 \ and 15 \ were taken as upper limits to the AGN luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper compares prior and new NGC 3998 X-ray spectra, finding a mildly absorbed power law whose photon index varies while flux declines between observations.

quotes.quote: ptak04apj:ngc3998 studied the 10 ks \ observation and fit the spectrum with a slightly absorbed ( N_ H 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) power-law ( 1.9 ). Same results were found for observations made with BeppoSAX pellegriniaa00:mgc3998 and ASCA ptak99apjs:TCM . gonzalezmartin09aa fit the \ spectrum with a combination of two absorbed power-laws and a thermal component. We fit the \ and the \ spectra simultaneously with a mildly absorbed ( N_ H 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) power-law and found a varying from 1.8 to 2.1 respectively, occurring with a flux decrease from 1.1 10^ 11 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 to 6.5 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3998's outer profile as III-d(?) and describes bar evidence, rings, antitruncation shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 (III-d(?); SA()0 ^0 ?) : In this object we see a small bar ( 7.8 ; 0.6 kpc) and a ring between 30 (2.1 kpc) and 50 (3.5 kpc) which produces a slight bump on the brightness profile, and coincides with a peak in the ellipticity at 36 (2.4 kpc). sanchez04 claimed that this galaxy has a Freeman Type II disk, with a cut-off at 30 (2 kpc), but this is probably just the effect of the bump associated with the ring. Our profile extends out to 350 (23 kpc) and shows an antitruncation with its break point at 120 (8 kpc). As this galaxy is only slightly inclined, so that its ellipticity is low over the whole of the disk, both inner and outer, it is not easy to say that there is no surrounding stellar halo. However, the fact that isophotes outside the break show no significant decline in ellipticity leads us to classify this as Type III-d(?), albeit with some uncertainty.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3998 as having a variable flat-spectrum radio core, a weak northern radio extension or inner outflow, and strong UV variability across five F250W epochs.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 hummel84AAradagn reported a variable radio core at the center of NGC 3998 with a flat spectrum and a very high surface brightness temperature ( >10^8 K). At milliarcsecond resolution, filho02AA:radliner reported a weak northern radio extension suspected to be the innermost kpc-scale outflow seen at arcsecond resolution hummel80AA:radagn . At UV wavelength, maoz05apj:linervarUV reported a strong variability between the 5 epoch observations of NGC 3998 with the /F250W filter. The flux of the first observation was found the highest and decreasing all along the 4 other observations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3998 as the faintest core-classified galaxy, notes its high velocity dispersion and AGN-contaminated central light profile, and tests relabelling it as Sersic.

quotes.quote: The `core' galaxy with the faintest magnitude is the lenticular galaxy NGC 3998. With a velocity dispersion in excess of 300 km s ^ 1 , this galaxy is expected to be a `core' galaxy. Figure 8 from Gonzalez Delgado et al. \ (2008) reveals that this galaxy hosts an AGN that swamps the central light profile and any core which may be there, hence the cautious `?' on its classification in Table 1. Re-labelling this galaxy to be a ``Sersic'' galaxy slightly steepens the Sersic M_ bh - L relation, and hence our case for a deviation from the previous linear M-L relation, but not significantly.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC3998, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC3998 (Fig. fig:NGC3998 ) appears to be a lenticular galaxy, but the radial profile indicates deviations from a simple bulge+disk composition. The surface brightness profile exhibits an inflection between 10 and 40 (best seen when scaling the radial coordinate logarithmically), corresponding to the location of a ring seen in the image. This inflection is located in between the bulge- and disk-dominated regions, and its surface brightness is lower than the inward extrapolation of the outer, exponential part of the profile.

sources.summary: The paper uses the LLAGN NGC3998 as an observational check on Compton temperature, deriving T_C about 10^9 K from its multiwavelength spectrum despite X-ray cutoff uncertainty.

quotes.quote: Taking an LLAGN -- NGC3998 --- as an example, we have calculated the corresponding T_C following eq. (4). The multiwaveband spectrum of this source is combined in Yu2011 . The result is T_C 10^9 K, although there are some uncertainties in the value of the high-energy cutoff in its X-ray spectrum. So this seems to indicate that the ``theoretical spectrum'' adopted in YXO2009 is not a bad approximation at least for some sources, although it is still necessary to check the other sources with various luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 3998 as the only non-barred galaxy with a notable negative residual and links this to its steep velocity-dispersion profile.

quotes.quote: The only non-barred galaxy with a notable negative M_ bh residual in Figure Fig4 and Fig5 is the LINER galaxy NGC 3998 (De Francesco et al. \ 2006). As remarked by Fisher (1997), this galaxy has a very steep central velocity dispersion profile, dropping from 320 km s ^ 1 at =0 to 160 km s ^ 1 at =4 arcseconds (270 pc) A velocity dispersion of 210 (or 250) km s ^ 1 for this galaxy would result in a zero (or 1 ) residual about the M_ bh relation.

However, it is noted that removal of just two galaxies (NGC 3998 and Cygnus A) from the sample of 29 non-barred galaxies leaves the coefficient in front of the (R_ e /3) term inconsistent with a value of zero at a significance of less than 2 .

sources.summary: The paper reports the first Swift detection of X-ray variability in NGC 3998, including rapid and multi-day flux changes, spectral softening, and a possible weak jet.

quotes.quote: Among our targets, we detect X-ray variability in NGC 3998 for the first time.
The 1--10 keV flux of NGC 3998 decreases by 30 in the nine days separating our two observations, while the 0.2--1 keV flux decreases more slowly, implying a softening of the spectrum.

We note, finally, that we cannot exclude that, in these sources, the emission at X-ray and/or UV bands is partially due to a weak jet, some evidence of which has been reported for all of our four sources, either directly (for M 81, based on resolved radio structure detection and radio polarization) or indirectly (for NGC 3998, NGC 4203, NGC 4579, based on a flat radio spectrum, a compact and variable radio core, and high brightness temperature) from high resolution radio imaging (Bietenholz et al.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC3998 as a pseudobulge host, consistent with one classical-bulge correlation but displaced from later inferred regressions, showing mixed behavior among pseudobulges.

quotes.quote: By means of the S e rsic index, we identify nine disk galaxies as hosting pseudobulges: Circinus, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368, NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594.

Figure fg:mbh illustrates that 4 out of 9 pseudobulges (NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594) are consistent with the correlation for classical bulges, while the others (CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368) are outliers, at more than 4 below the LINMIX ERR linear regression.

These are CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079 and NGC3998; therefore there is a good overlap (4/6 where the exceptions are NGC3368 and NGC3998) with the deviant pseudobulges found in the previous sections.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3998's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 is an early-type spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 14.1 1.3 Mpc tonry_sbf_2001 with a variable radio-loud broad-line LINER nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It features a compact flat-spectrum radio core with a jet-like northern extension (e.g., filho_light-year_2002 ), and a disc-like NLR with a major axis of 3 200 pc and a PA 90 pogge_narrow-line_2000 . The first ground-based MIR observations of NGC 3998 were carried out with IRTF and UKIRT in 1983 and 1984 sparks_infrared_1986, willner_infrared_1985 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a new CO(1-0) detection in UGC 6946, describes its double-horn profile, and notes its low H2 mass but high multiwavelength luminosity.

quotes.quote: We report the new detection of CO(1-0) emission in four galaxies (UGC 0968, UGC 5617, UGC 6946, and UGC 8355) and CO(2-1) emission in two of them (UGC 0968 and UGC 5617).
Among the 4 sources showing CO(1-0) detection, UGC 0968 and UGC 6946 exhibit double-horn profiles, while UGC 5617 and UGC 8355 have single-peak profiles.

However, it is interesting to point out that UGC 6946 which has the lowest H_ 2 mass among the new CO detection is one of the most luminous sources in [ O III ] , X-ray, optical, and radio of the sample.

sources.summary: NGC 3998 is cited as a nearby flat-spectrum FR 0 radio source with faint 10 kpc emission, unresolved FIRST structure, and no SDSS spectrum.

quotes.quote: Nonetheless, very low surface brightness emission ( 0.15 mJy beam ^ 1 ) extending over 10 kpc have been detected in NGC 3998 frank16 . NGC 3998 is a nearby (z=0.0035) flat-spectrum radio source, unresolved in the FIRST images, fulfilling all requirements for an FR 0 classification. NGC 3998 is not included in the , \ because the SDSS did not obtain its optical spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper compares its NGC 3998 dark-matter fraction with earlier modeling, finding consistency and arguing stellar mass dominates within one effective radius.

quotes.quote: 2016MNRAS.460.3029B modelled NGC 3998 with the same stellar kinematics data sets but a constant mass-to-light ratio and an NFW dark matter profile and reported f_ DM (R_ e ) = 0.07_ 0.07 ^ 0.08 . In this work, we measure f_ DM (R_ e ) = 0.05 0.06 for NGC 3998 taking into account the mass-to-light gradient, consistent with the previous result. Since the stellar mass dominates the enclosed mass within 1 R_ e in NGC 3998, the measurements of f_ DM (R_ e ) are expected to be close to 0, regardless of the assumptions made.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3998 as a LINER with a variable compact radio core, declining HST UV flux, and a measured central black hole mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 3998 - In this nucleus, Filho et al. (2002) have measured a variable radio core that is unresolved at 5 mas resolution with VLBI, with a mean 6 cm flux of 83 mJy. In the UV, M05 measured in 2002--2003 a monotonic 20 per cent decline in UV flux in the F250W and F330W bands (means of 199 10^ 17 and 153 10^ 17 , respectively), over the 11 months they observed it. These variations provide firm lower limits on the AGN flux.

gmw_00000200.name: NGC 4051
gmw_00000200.overview: NGC 4051 is a nearby narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galaxy [arXiv:1801.03519, arXiv:2303.11799] hosting a low-mass supermassive black hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0104380, arXiv:1605.02631]. The galaxy exhibits rapid, multi-band X-ray and optical variability [arXiv:2311.02722], complex warm absorbers [arXiv:1106.2135, arXiv:2208.08457], and star-forming regions within its spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0508281, arXiv:2308.09002].

sources.summary: The study monitors NGC 4051 for 12 years, finding optical variability lagging X-rays by about 1.2 days from disc reprocessing and a second lag consistent with torus emission.

quotes.quote: We discuss the origin of the optical variations in the Narrow line Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4051 and present the results of a cross-correlation study using X-ray and optical light curves spanning more than 12 years. The emission is highly variable in all wavebands, and the amplitude of the optical variations is found to be smaller than that of the X-rays, even after correcting for the contaminating host galaxy flux falling inside the photometric aperture. The optical power spectrum is best described by an unbroken power law model with slope =1.4^ +0.6 _ 0.2 and displays lower variability power than the 2--10 keV X-rays on all time-scales probed. We find the light curves to be significantly correlated at an optical delay of 1.2^ +1.0 _ 0.3  days behind the X-rays. This time-scale is consistent with the light travel time to the optical emitting region of the accretion disc, suggesting that the optical variations are driven by X-ray reprocessing.

We show, however, that a model whereby the optical variations arise from reprocessing by a flat accretion disc cannot account for all the optical variability. There is also a second significant peak in the cross-correlation function, at an optical delay of 39^ +2.7 _ 8.4  days. The lag is consistent with the dust sublimation radius in this source, suggesting that there is a measurable amount of optical flux coming from the dust torus.

sources.summary: The paper monitors Seyfert-1 AGN NGC 4051 in X-ray and radio bands, linking its nearby high-Eddington accretion, variability, radio lobes, and inverse X-ray-radio trend to an unusual disk-jet coupling mode.

quotes.quote: To address these issues, and in order to examine disk-jet coupling at high mass accretion rates, we undertook a simultaneous X-ray and radio monitoring campaign of the Seyfert-1 AGN NGC 4051. This galaxy is relatively nearby (z=0.002336), and the central black hole mass has been determined through reverberation mapping techniques (1.91 0.78) 10^6 M_ (1.73 0.55) 10^6 M_ Peterson04,Denney09 . NGC 4051 is typically observed to accrete at approximately 5 of its Eddington luminosity Peterson04 . The innermost orbital timescale of NGC 4051 is on the order of a few minutes to hours, defined as t_ dyn R/v_ , where R is the radius assumed to be only a few gravitational radii from the black hole and v_ is the orbital velocity.

In this paper, we present eight Chandra X-ray observations and six VLA/EVLA radio observations of Seyfert-1 NGC 4051 taken over a seven month period. By simultaneously measuring in X-ray and radio bands every two to four weeks apart, we are able to probe variations in the accretion rate that occur on the viscous timescale of a few days to weeks in NGC 4051. The observations reveal significant variability in both X-ray and radio bands. The first 8.4 GHz observation on MJD 54831.3 also shows evidence of jet production in the form of two distinct radio lobes, contrary to the idea that radio quiet galaxies like NGC 4051 lack jet production.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NuSTAR observations of NGC 4051, a variable narrow-line Seyfert 1 AGN, measuring X-ray lags and linking reprocessing to circumnuclear material.

quotes.quote: Five archived X-ray observations of NGC 4051, taken using the NuSTAR observatory, have been analysed, revealing lags between flux variations in bands covering a wide range of X-ray photon energy. In all pairs of bands compared, the harder band consistently lags the softer band by at least 1000 s, at temporal frequencies 5 10^ 5 Hz. In addition, soft-band lags up to 400 s are measured at frequencies 2 10^ 4 Hz. Light echos from an excess of soft band emission in the inner accretion disk cannot explain the lags in these data, as they are seen in cross-correlations with energy bands where the softer band is expected to have no contribution from reflection.

NGC 4051 is a bright, nearby narrow-line Seyfert 1 AGN with low black hole mass, (M 1.7 10^6 M_ ; Denney10a ) and high accretion rate ( 10 of Eddington), making it one of the most variable Seyfert galaxies in the X-ray band, with factors up to 15 observed on timescales of tens of ks ponti06a . The X-ray spectrum has been well studied and shows a multi-phase outflow, with zone velocities ranging from a few hundred to few thousand km/s, pounds04c, pounds11a . The ionization of some of the absorbing zones has been observed to change in response to the varying continuum lobban11a, krongold07a . The strong and persistent reprocessing signatures show that there is a large amount of circumnuclear material in NGC 4051, making this an ideal target for a detailed reverberation study.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4051 has low-sigma patches, high h3 values, h3-velocity anti-correlation, and the lowest deprojected velocity amplitude in the sample.

quotes.quote: Patches of low- are seen for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899, while three objects (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388) show a centrally peaked distribution. These rings and partial rings are located at distances from the nucleus ranging between 150 and 250 pc. Nuclear values for each galaxy are presented in the last column of Table tab_mod , obtained by fitting the nuclear spectrum integrated within a circular aperture with radius of 75 pc. Most galaxies show low h_ 3 values ( 0.10 <h_ 3 < 0.10), suggesting that their LOSVDs are well represented by a Gaussian velocity distribution. Exceptions are Mrk 607, Mrk1066 and NGC 4051 that show h_ 3 values larger than 0.15. For 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066), the h_ 3 map is anti-correlated with the velocity field, with positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where rotation velocities are observed in blueshifts and negative h_ 3 values associated to regions where the rotation velocities are observed in redshifts.

The velocity dispersion maps show low- ( 50-80 ) rings for 4 galaxies (Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788) or ``patches" of low- structures (for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899) at typical distances of 200 pc, interpreted as being originated in young/intermediate age stellar populations. Centrally peaked maps are observed for three galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388). The h_ 3 moment is anti-correlated with the velocity field for 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066) -- positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where the velocity field shows blueshifts and h_ 3 <0 for locations where the velocity field shows redshifts. The presence of these wings are attributed to the contribution of stars from the galaxy bulge that present lower rotation velocities. The observed velocity fields are well reproduced by a rotating disk model, with deprojected velocity amplitudes in the range 60--340 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4051 as a low-mass Seyfert 1 AGN, using XMM-Newton data to show timescale-dependent X-ray spectral variability beyond a simple softer-when-brighter trend.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051, one of the X-ray brightest AGNs, is a low mass Seyfert 1 galaxy. Its X-ray variabilities have been extensively studied in literature e.g. Lamer2003,McHardy2004,Miniutti2004,Uttley2004,Pounds2004,Ponti2006 . In this work we scrutinize its hard X-ray spectral variabilities from two new aspects, using archival XMM-Newton observations.

Using XMM-Newton exposures on NGC 4051, we show that its powerlaw (2--10 keV) spectral variability is more than what an empirical ``softer-when-brighter" trend could describe. We find intervals which clearly deviate from the empirical relation, both between and within XMM-Newton exposures. The deviations can be seen at timescales as short as 2 ks.

We further find that the 4 -- 10 keV structure function is flatter than that of 2 -- 4 keV, indicating the ``softer-when-brighter" trend is gradually weaker at shorter timescales (from 100 ks to 0.5 ks). These discoveries directly demonstrate that the powerlaw spectral slope is not solely determined by its brightness.

sources.summary: For NGC 4051, the paper finds behavior unlike the previous three sources: higher flux is associated with larger relative variability and a different PSD normalization-spectrum link.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051 For NGC 4051, only two observations met the requirements that we set in Section sec:2 . The spectra and PSDs are shown in the bottom row of panels in Figure fig:spec&PSD_s and fig:spec&PSD_h . The spectrum in 2001 is steeper than that in 2010, and the flux in 2001 is also higher. But in the 0.3-1 keV band, the break frequency in 2001 is much lower than that in 2018, which is opposite to the behavior of break frequency in other objects.

Meanwhile, the 2-10 keV PSD does not show a high-frequency break in the observation of 2001, which has also been confirmed by taking into account the influence of Poisson noise power (see Table tab:LRT2 ). Interestingly, the PSD normalization in the observation of 2001 is higher than 2018, except the lower Poisson noise in 2001 due to the higher count rate. This suggests that as the flux of NGC 4051 increases, its relative variability amplitude also increases. Therefore, the link between the PSD normalization and the energy spectrum of NGC 4051 is different from the previous three sources.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes AstroSat UVIT far-UV observations of Seyfert AGN host NGC 4051, identifying 193 star-forming regions and deriving the lowest median SFR in the two-galaxy sample.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the ultra-violet (UV) observations of two Seyfert type active galactic nuclei (AGN), namely NGC 4051 and NGC 4151. These observations aimed at studying the star formation in the hosts of these AGN were carried out with the ultra-violet imaging telescope on board AstroSat in far-UV. A total of 193 and 328 star-forming regions (SF) were identified using SExtractor in NGC 4051 and NGC 4151, respectively. Using aperture photometry of the identified SF regions, we estimated the star formation rates (SFRs). We found NGC 4051 to have the lowest SFR with a median value of 3.16 10 ^ 5 M _ yr ^ 1 while for NGC 4151, we found a median SFR of 0.012 M _ yr ^ 1 .

We observed NGC 4051 and NGC 4151 using UVIT. The final FUV image of NGC 4051 and NGC 4151 is shown in Fig. figure-1 and Fig. figure-2 , respectively. We adopted the Galactic extinction of A(V) = 0.036 mag and 0.074 mag in the V-band for NGC 4051 and NGC 4151, respectively, from NED https://ned.ipac. caltech.edu .

sources.summary: The paper presents simultaneous Chandra and HST spectroscopy of NGC 4051, describing its Seyfert nature, rich X-ray history, intrinsic absorption systems, soft X-ray excess, and strong variability.

quotes.quote: We present the results from simultaneous observations of the Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4051 with the Chandra High Energy Transmission Grating Spectrometer and the HST Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph. The X-ray grating spectrum reveals absorption and emission lines from hydrogen-like and helium-like ions of O, Ne, Mg and Si. We resolve two distinct X-ray absorption systems: a high-velocity blueshifted system at 2340 130  km s ^ 1 and a low-velocity blueshifted system at 600 130  km s ^ 1 . In the UV spectrum we detect strong absorption, mainly from C 4 , N 5 and Si 4 , that is resolved into as many as nine different intrinsic absorption systems with velocities between 650  km s ^ 1 and 30 km s ^ 1 .

NGC 4051 is a Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy with a rich observational history. Its optical emission-line spectrum was first noted by Hubble (1926), and it is one of the archetypical Seyfert galaxies (Seyfert 1943). It has been studied with every X-ray mission since Einstein , as well as with EUVE , HST and IUE , because it is bright [ V 13.5 ; z=0.002295 0.000043 based on optical emission lines (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991) ] and has relatively little Galactic absorption [ N_ H =(1.3 0.1) 10^ 20  cm ^ 2 ; Elvis, Lockman, Wilkes 1989 ] . NGC 4051 is highly variable in X-rays and has even been seen virtually to shut off (decreasing in flux by a factor of 20 from the average; e.g., Guainazzi et al. 1998; Uttley et al. 1999).

sources.summary: The paper finds no extended X-ray emission in NGC 4051, but reports ionized absorption, photoionized plasma, a narrow Fe K line, and one near-nuclear point source.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051 Figure 6 compares the optical DSS image of NGC 4051 with the zero order Chandra HETG soft and hard X-ray images. There is no evidence for extended emission (see also Collinge et al. \ 2001). NGC 4051 possesses an ionized absorber with N_ H =10^ 20-21 cm ^ 2 . The Chandra spectrum shows emission lines from various hydrogen-like and helium-like ions of O, Ne, Mg and Si as well as blueshifted X-ray absorption systems at 2340 130 km s ^ 1 and 600 130 km s ^ 1 . The high ratio of forbidden to resonance lines implies that the plasma is photoionized with little collisional ionization (Collinge et al. \ 2001).

The Fe K emission line at 6.4 keV has a FWHM of less than 2,800 km s ^ 1 and an EW of 158 eV. This line is significantly narrower than an accretion disk line and, like MCG 5-23-16, indicates emission from farther out in the galaxy core. Within 1 kpc of the nucleus there is a single X-ray point source, which shows up in the soft image to the SE of the nucleus. It has a 0.5--8 keV X-ray luminosity of 1.4 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 , consistent with an X-ray binary accreting at the Eddington limit.

sources.summary: The paper reports newly discovered water maser emission toward the NLS1 galaxy NGC 4051, with components near systemic velocity and VLA evidence placing the masers near the radio continuum peak.

quotes.quote: Water vapor maser emission in the 6 _ 16 -5 _ 23 transition towards the narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy NGC 4051 has been discovered during an ongoing single-dish extragalactic water maser survey. The Doppler-shifted maser components appear to bracket maser components lying near the systemic velocity of the galaxy symmetrically. The tentative result of a Very Large Array (VLA) snapshot observation is that the masers are confined within 0.1 arcsec (5 pc at a distance D = 9.7 Mpc) of the radio continuum peak seen at 8.4 GHz.

The discovery spectrum also displays a high-velocity component at = 936 ( 3 level), but this component does not have any blue-shifted counterparts. The maser components are observed from 659 to the weaker component at 773 , which accounts for a velocity extreme of 70 relative to the systemic velocity. While these extrema are not as large as the maximum rotation velocity of 1100 for NGC 4258 (e.g., miyo95 ), such symmetrically or quasi-symmetrically distributed emission has not been observed except in the cases of NGC 4258, NGC 1068 and NGC 2960 linc96,henk02 . We searched for additional components within 1000 of the systemic velocity, but none were detected at the 3 sensitivity levels of 15 -- 45 mJy, depending on the velocity range (Table 1). No acceleration of the maser components was detected over the six months interval, yielding an upper limit for the drift rate of 2.2 year ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4051 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy, finding variable optical Fe ii emission that tracks continuum changes and positive correlations with Fe ii strength measures.

quotes.quote: The variability of optical Fe ii blends in NGC 4051 is examined from spectra extracted from the AGN Watch program. In our analysis, the Fe ii complex are subtracted and measured with the following results. Firstly, the Fe ii variations were detected in NGC 4051 during a 3-year period. The identified Fe ii variations followed the variations in continuum closely. Secondly, the EW of Fe ii is reported to increase with the rising continuum flux, which is consistent with previous claims that there is no convincing Baldwin Effect in optical Fe ii .

This analysis allows us to make the following conclusions: In NGC 4051, we find that the optical Fe ii complex was variable during the three years period. The Fe ii variations closely follow the continuum variations, and the intensity of Fe ii evidently increases with the continuum flux. A positive correlation between the EW of Fe ii and the continuum flux is identified in NGC 4051. This result agrees with the previous claims that no convincing BE of Fe ii has been detected untill now. By comparing the variations of H and Fe ii , a positive correlation between R _ Fe and continuum flux is obtained in NGC 4051.

sources.summary: The paper uses Chandra LETGS spectroscopy of NGC 4051 to constrain its AGN outflow, ionization response, emission components, and known Seyfert-galaxy properties.

quotes.quote: We study the absorption and emission properties of the photoionised gas near one of the brightest and most variable AGN, the Seyfert galaxy NGC 4051, in order to constrain the geometry, dynamics and ionisation structure of the outflow. We analyse two observations taken with the Low Energy Transmission Grating Spectrometer (LETGS) of Chandra. We study the spectra of both observations and investigate the spectral response to a sudden, long-lasting flux decrease of a factor of 5 that occurred during the second observation. We confirm the preliminary detection of a highly ionised component with an outflow velocity of 4500 km s ^ 1 , one of the highest velocity outflow components seen in a Seyfert 1 galaxy. The sudden drop in intensity by a factor of five during the second observation causes a drop in ionisation parameter of a similar magnitude in the strongest and main ionisation component ( v = -610 km s ^ 1 ), allowing us for the first time to determine the recombination time of this component and thereby its distance in a robust way.

In this paper we describe the X-ray spectra of the Active Galactic Nucleus of NGC 4051, obtained with the Low-Energy Transmission Grating on board of the Chandra satellite. Emission from the nucleus of NGC 4051 was already found by hubble , and the galaxy is one of the 6 observed by seyfert , and discussed in his seminal paper as one of the first 12 known Seyfert galaxies. Being a member of the Ursa Major cluster tullyetal it has a distance of 18.6 Mpc tullypierce and a redshift of 700 km s ^ 1 verheijen .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4051 as an SAB Seyfert with a large stellar bar, radio-aligned emission, blue-wing narrow lines, a conical outflow, and regular stellar rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051 is an SAB galaxy classified as S1.2 by Ho3 . The stellar bar extends up to 50 along a PA 135 . Radio-observations show a triple source at a PA  73 4051_outflow , and the \ emission line distribution in the inner 3 \ is aligned with this radio component. The narrow-line profiles of NGC 4051 present strong blue wings.
