sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4051 as one of five poorly fit Seyfert/BLRG spectra with high signal-to-noise Suzaku data and strong O 7/O 8 absorption requiring more detailed warm-absorber modeling.

quotes.quote: The majority of sources have spectra that are well-fit with this model. Five sources, however, are not well-fit, with reduced ^2 > 2.0 . These include NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, and MR 2251-178. Each of these sources have high signal-to-noise Suzaku spectra with exposures from 79--275 ks in length and have strong detections of O 7 and O 8 absorption. The complexity in the soft X-ray emission is not fully accounted for with the simple addition of the absorption edges. This is clear in the bottom panels of Figure fig-basemodel , where the ratio of data/model for three of these sources clearly shows structure that is not accounted for with our model.

sources.summary: NGC 4051 has significant absorption near the neutral Fe K edge, manifested by two velocity-linked lines, and is included among radio-quiet Fe K outflows confirmed by the analysis.

quotes.quote: Of the sources included in the sample CBS 126, Mrk 766 (OBSIDs 701035010, 701035020), NGC 3227 (OBSIDs 703022010, 703022020, 703022030), NGC 4051 (OBSIDs 700004010, 703023010) and NGC 4151 all have at least one significantly detected absorption line at a rest-frame energy which is consistent at the 90 level with the neutral Fe K-shell edge from the reflection component. The absorption in Mrk 766, NGC 3227, and NGC 4051 is manifested by two lines with a common velocity shift equal to that expected for and and are therefore unlikely to be affected by the presence of an edge.
Detailed sample comparison Radio-quiet sources In this work we have detected Fe K outflows in 17 radio-quiet AGN and 3 in radio-loud.

In the heavily absorbed sources such as NCG 3783, NGC 4051 and MCG 6-30-15 there were some small variations in column density and ionisation parameter for the soft band absorbers when fitting with comptt , presumably to compensate for the fact that the Comptonised emission fitted with comptt can extend to harder X-ray energies (i.e., E 2-3 keV) than typically seen in black-body derived models.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4051 with NGC 4395, noting its larger black hole mass, similar viscous-timescale campaign, high X-ray variability, and radio-quiet behavior.

quotes.quote: In our previous work, we show that NGC 4051 has higher variability in the X-ray as compared to its simultaneous radio observations King11 . NGC 4051 is slightly larger at 1.73 ^ +0.55 _ 0.52 10^6 M_ Denney10 , and the simultaneous X-ray and radio observing campaign of NGC 4051 probed the same viscous times as in NGC 4395 King11 . Shown in Figure fig:fp are both NGC 4395 and NGC 4051, as they move out of the fundamental plane as given for Seyferts by . Both sources are fairly constant in the radio, while the X-ray drives them out of the plane.

sources.summary: The paper notes previous studies of NGC 4051 found four decades of ionization and relativistic O VIII lines, while this analysis detects two warm absorber components.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051- This same dataset has been studied multiple times 2008RMxAC..32..123K,2006MNRAS.368..903P,2004ApJ...606..151O . find that the ions cover nearly 4 decades of ionisation parameter. They also find relativistic O 8 emission lines. We detect two discrete WA components of ionisation parameters 0.28 and 2.87 .

sources.summary: The paper infers a bend timescale for NGC 4051, compares it with black-hole mass and spin constraints, and derives a maximally spinning black-hole mass upper limit.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051 with an inferred break timescale of 1820^ +269 _ 235 (from the current analysis) hosts a BH of mass (1.9^ +0.78 _ 0.78 ) 10^6 M_ and a lower limit on the spin of 0.30. Contours of T_ B = T_ B (,a) are plotted in Figure NGC4051brkfig . The overlap in this case is only between the contours of T_ B and the spin a . An upper limit to the black hole mass of 2.85 10^7 M_ (for a maximally spinning black hole with a = 0.998 ) can be inferred.

sources.summary: NGC 4051 has new observations beyond earlier work and is listed among AGN whose Fe K constraints are consistent in the paper’s radius comparisons.

quotes.quote: In this respect, note that several new observations for NGC 4051 have become available since the work of shu10 .
For 9 of the 13 AGN, the measurements or limits on \ are fully consistent with . These are NGC 3783, NGC 4151, NGC 4593, NGC 5548, Mrk 509, 3C 273, IRAS 13349+2438, NGC 4051 and IC 4329A.

Whereas the median value of \ is 8 times smaller than \ for the full sample, there are 6 objects (NGC 4051, NGC 4593, NGC 7469, Mrk 509, Fairall 9 and 3C 273) for which the \ measurement or limit is entirely consistent with .

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4051 as an NLS1 with RXTE and optical monitoring whose X-ray/optical correlation can be explained by both X-ray reprocessing and inward perturbation propagation.

quotes.quote: studied the X-ray optical correlation of the NLS1 galaxy NGC 4051 based on the data available from 2000 May-July observations. The optical data were retrieved from Wise Observatory and X-ray data from RXTE . They obtained RXTE data from 251 observations with an interval of 6 h. They conclude that the observed X-ray optical correlation in the source can be explained as a combined effect of X-ray reprocessing and the propagation of perturbations from the outer disc to the X-ray emitting region.

sources.summary: The paper says dust lanes make the fit uncertain, but models the host as a pseudo-bulge plus disturbed disk and compares bulge magnitudes.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051 (Fig. 11.37) --- \ \ The fit can be highly uncertain due to the presence of a dust lane. The host is fit with a pseudo-bulge ( n=1.03 ) and a disturbed disk. We find m_ bul 14.3 mag, which is in good agreement with Virani et al. (2000). However, Bentz et al. (2009) obtain a significantly brighter bulge ( m_ bul 12.8 ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses J1203+4431 as an NLS1 source, summarizing its radio and spectral-index properties, flux density, luminosity, and whether extended emission is linked to AGN activity or the host galaxy.

quotes.quote: J1203+4431 J1203+4431 (NGC 4051) is one of the most studied sources in our sample due to its proximity ( z = 0.002), and also one of the six original Seyfert galaxies studied in 1943seyfert1 . It is a radio-quiet NLS1 hosted by a spiral galaxy (Fig. fig:J1203-host ). In our observations, its peak flux density is 0.78 mJy beam ^ 1 , the integrated flux density is 5.47 mJy, and the flux densities of the tapered maps slightly higher. Due to its low redshift this translates to a very low luminosity of log L_ , int = 36.46 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4051 among five AGN whose power spectra flatten with energy, noting it is the one sample object with prior similar reports.

quotes.quote: We apply strict data criteria (both in rejecting observations with an insufficient number of Fourier bins at frequencies below the white noise cutoff, and sources for which there were too few observations to provide error estimates), leaving us with five AGN: IRAS 13224-3809, 1H 0707-495, NGC 4051, ARK 564 and MRK 766. In all five cases, we observe that the power-law index -- which well describes the power spectrum in this frequency range (and is preferred over a broken power-law according to maximum likelihood fitting) -- is steep at low energies and becomes increasingly flat towards higher energies. To-date this has only been reported for a very small number of AGN (with only one such AGN within our sample -- NGC 4051, see 2004McHardyNGC4051Edepend ; 2011VaughanNGC4051 ) and across only a small number of energy bins 2001NandraNGC7469, 2003VaughanMCGvariability, 2005McHardyMCG63015, 2020JinREJ ; our analysis therefore represents a much broader investigation into the energy-dependence of the shape of AGN power spectra. Adopting the framework of 2006ArevaloUttley , we model the power-spectra of our AGN sample in five energy bands, assuming energy-dependent changes are driven by propagation only, and extract the emissivity index as a function of energy, parameterising it as a linear function.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4051 has an accurate velocity dispersion, a small bulge dispersion, and black-hole/bulge properties consistent with a low-mass, high-accretion-rate narrow-line Seyfert 1 interpretation.

quotes.quote: Unfortunately, NW95 warn that only for two of these galaxies, NGC 4051 and 3C120, are the measured dispersions deemed accurate. For NGC 4051, they quote = 88 13 , in good agreement with our result.

Regarding the latter point, we note that NGC 4051, the galaxy with the smallest \ in our sample, is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy. Mrk 110 also approximately meets the traditional narrow-line Seyfert 1 criterion that FWHM ( ) 2000 . The BH masses of the NLS1s are consistent with those of the other AGNs, and all are consistent with the masses inferred from the \ relation defined by the quiescent galaxies. This result is at odds with the recent findings of Mathur et al. (2001), who claim that NLS1s lie systematically below the \ relation defined by Seyfert 1 galaxies. One paradigm for NLS1s is that, compared to AGNs with similar non-thermal luminosity, they are powered by lower mass BHs accreting at a relatively higher accretion rate. A competing explanation is that they differ from other AGNs only in inclination; the small widths of the broad lines and concomitantly low reverberation masses are due to viewing disk-like sources at low inclination (i.e., nearly face-on). The consistency of the BH mass and bulge velocity dispersion of NGC 4051, in particular, with the \ relation for other galaxies strongly favors the low-mass/high accretion rate interpretation for the nature of NLS1s.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4051 has unresolved NLR lines and molecular hydrogen emission whose diagnostic ratios place it on or near the pure thermal excitation curve.

quotes.quote: Note that for Mrk 766 and NGC 4051, the optical lines are half as broad as the NIR ones, but in both cases, the NLR lines are spectroscopically unresolved.

NGC 3227, NGC 4051, NGC 4151 and Mrk 766 (labelled 4, 7, 8 and 9, respectively) fall near the thermal curve model and indicate excitation temperatures for the thermal component of between 1500 K and 2500 K. For these galaxies, the 2 \ emission can be considered purely thermal.

Overall, Figure fig1 b reproduces most of the conclusions already drawn: NGC 4051 and NGC 3227 fall on or near the pure thermal excitation curve, and most objects show a thermal excitation temperature in the range 1000--3000 K indicating a dominance of thermal processes over the non-thermal ones.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4051 as having an uneven blue-side continuum and weak Ca II triplet emission with O I and Fe II emission removed through modeled nuclear subtraction.

quotes.quote: The most important effect of this procedure is that it removes the irregular continuum shape from spectra immediately adjacent to the nucleus. This was particularly helpful in NGC 4051, NGC 4151 and NGC 4593, where the continuum on the blue side of the spectrum is distinctly uneven (compare figures fig:rawspec and fig:fcqspec ). This allowed us to extract spectra for the velocity dispersion analysis much closer to the nucleus than would have been possible otherwise, resulting in higher signal-to-noise and ensuring that as much of the bulge light was included as possible.

NGC 4051 -- Weak Ca II triplet in emission can be seen in this object as well as strong and relatively narrow OI 8446 and [ Fe II ] 8618 emission. All of these were removed by combining model nuclear emission with a seeing star as described in section sec:remnuc .

sources.summary: NGC 4051 is identified as a very low-luminosity AGN outlier, possibly affected by reddening or obscuration, and excluded from the fitted BLR relation.

quotes.quote: Two obvious outliers, which do not fit the general trends in any of the wavelength bands, are NGC 3227 and NGC 4051. These are the two-least luminous objects in all wavelength bands. This behavior could be due to major reddening or obscuration in these two objects, which decreases their measured luminosity (e.g., NGC 3227 - Crenshaw et al. 2001; NGC 4051 - Kaspi et al. 2004).
We will exclude these two objects from our fits to the BLR-size -- luminosity relation, noting the derived relation applies to AGNs with optical luminosity in the range 10^ 43 < L_ (5100 ) < 10^ 46 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4051 as a high-accretion NLS1 whose flat X-ray power spectrum resembles the high/soft state of Cyg X-1.

quotes.quote: Two of these AGN (NGC 4051 and MCG-6-30-15, both NLS1's) clearly show flat noise power extending over more than 4 decades in frequency. These look very like that of the high/soft state in Cyg X-1, taking into account uncertainties in the low frequency slope of the power spectra (McHardy et al. 2004; 2005). The total rms power in both is of order 25 per cent, again similar to that of the Comptonized tail in the high/soft state of Cyg X-1 (McHardy et al. 2004). Thus these AGN can be interpreted as the supermassive counterpart of the high/soft state Cyg X-1 data, which is not like the majority of transient GBH power spectra at high accretion rates.

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 4051 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051 . One of the most well-studied AGN of our sample, NGC 4051 is classified as a NLS1 galaxy. Our NIR spectrum reveals a large variety of spectroscopic features, from low ionization forbidden lines such as, [ C i ] and [ N i ] , to high-ionization lines of [ S ix ] and [ Si x ] . The last two are particularly strong, compared to the other emission lines observed. The NLS1 nature of NGC 4051 is revealed well by the width of permitted lines.

sources.summary: Suzaku observed NGC 4051 as a variable narrow-line Seyfert 1; its spectra require complex absorption, soft excess emission, iron K lines, and strong reflection.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051, a Narrow Line Seyfert 1 at z=0.002336 was observed by \ for a net exposure of 93.2 ks. The AGN shows typical large amplitude fluctuations making it possible to extract high and low flux spectra from the \ observation. These spectra are shown in Figure 8, showing that NGC 4051 is harder at low fluxes, and less variable in the hard band. The source has a flat spectrum in the 2-10 keV band, with =1.55 and =1.15 for the high and low spectra, with fluxes of 1.1 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and 6.3 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4051 has double-Gaussian BLR lags, and that revising the VBGC lag resolves its offset from the radius-luminosity relation.

quotes.quote: Two of the sources with double Gaussian line profiles are NGC 4051 and Mrk 509; the lag times for the two components of both sources satisfy this simple relationship t_ IMLR /t_ VBLR =V^ 2 _ VBLR /V^ 2 _ IMLR , consistent with our two-components BLR assumption. Interestingly the lag time for the VBGC of NGC 4051 is between 1 to 2 days, significantly different from the lag time of about 4 days for the whole line. This revised lag time makes NGC 4051 consistent with the Radius-Luminosity relationship in equation (5) or (6), resolving the outstanding problem on the significant deviation of NGC 4051 from the Radius-Luminosity relationship (Kaspi et al. 2005).

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 4051 as an AGN outflow case, linking its sub-Eddington luminosity, cooling-shock evidence, and velocity-ionization correlation to Eddington-wind feedback.

quotes.quote: This may be the reason why AGN showing other signs of super--Eddington phenomena (e.g. narrow--line Seyfert 2 galaxies) are nevertheless seen to have sub--Eddington luminosities (e.g. NGC 4051: Denney et al., 2009).

There is direct observational evidence for this cooling shock in NGC 4051 (Pounds et al., in prep). Pounds et al (2004) had already noted a correlation of outflow velocity with ionization in this source.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4051 as a marginal blue-excess case whose disk model fits, but whose iron-line-region variability must arise from a variable broad line.

quotes.quote: A further four show lower-confidence (80-95 ) excesses only, but in the cases of MCG-5-23-16(2) and NGC 4051 these might well be real, as they exhibit more than one bin with such an excess.
NGC 4051 is fit well by an accretion disk model but Ponti et al. (2006) have found that the iron line is not variable in the same observation of NGC 4051 when it was in a high flux state.
This includes MCG-5-23-16(2), NGC 3516(2) and NGC 4051(1) in which we found variability across the iron line region that must then be due to a variable broad line, rather than narrow transient lines.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 4051 has Fe-line detections, complex absorption, and variable hard-X-ray behavior within the INTEGRAL NLSy1 sample.

quotes.quote: A fluorescent emission line around 6-7 keV is detected in three out of four NLSy1 observed by XMM- Newton (see Table table=fe ), this fraction increases to six out of seven if we consider the line detections in NGC 4051, Mrk 766 and NGC 5506.
The presence of strong, partial and/or stratified absorption has also been found in NGC 4051, Mrk 766 and NGC 5506 and is one of the competing models applied in objects with complex spectra.
NGC 4051 is the only source in our sample which belongs to the list of variable INTEGRAL sources edited by Telezhinsky et al.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4051 with complex iron-line structure, strong soft excess, broad-line and reflection signatures, and pronounced spectral variability.

quotes.quote: A systematic analysis of such lines in our sample is not presented here, but we identify four sources with data that display hints of complex iron lines by inspection of residuals, but have too few counts ( <4600 ) to fit an iron line successfully (Mrk 417, UGC 06527, NGC 4686 and SWIFT J1309.2+1139), two sources that definitely exhibit structure in the lines beyond what is shown here using our simple zgauss fits (Mrk 766 and NGC 4051), and five sources that show possible signs of broad lines by inspection of residuals (NGC 5252, NGC 5273, UM 614, NGC 4151, NGC 4579).

Mrk 766 is known to be highly variable and has been extensively studied in the literature (e.g., 2007A&A...475..121T , where the pronounced variation in spectral shape between epochs is shown); this conclusion also holds for NGC 4051 which exhibits both a broad Iron line and a high reflection fraction, alongside well-studied variability favouring the reflection scenario 2006MNRAS.368..903P .

We see from Fig. softx_vs_Gamma that this is not the case; for the two objects with the largest soft-excess strengths (NGC 4051 and Mrk 766, which also happen to show pronounced spectral variability) we do indeed see that they have hard ( = 1.5 ) spectra, but for all other objects, the opposite trend seems to be evident, i.e., the strength of the soft excess increases with (as the spectrum gets softer), in line with the trend previously found in .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4051 as a detected NLSy1 with water maser emission and a 40 pc double lobe-like structure resembling a mini radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: Five sources (NGC 3227, NGC 3982, NGC 4051, NGC 4138 and NGC 5033) were detected at both frequencies, three more sources at only one frequency (NGC 4388 and NGC 4501 at 1.7 GHz and NGC 4477 at 5 GHz).

Seven out of 27 ( 26 ) have detected water maser emission, with an unusually large fraction of 22 of type 1 objects detected, including the NLSy1 NGC 4051 (see Tarchi et al. (2011) for a comparable detection rate in NLSy1).
A double ( 40 pc extended) lobe-like structure is observed in NGC 4051, which resembles a mini radio galaxy (GP09).

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4051's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4051 is a low-inclination grand-design spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 12.2 2.4 Mpc tully_extragalactic_2009 and belongs to the original six Seyfert galaxies seyfert_nuclear_1943 . It contains a well-studied variable AGN (see maitra_jet_2011 for a recent analysis if its SED), which is classified optically either as Sy 1n veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 or Sy 1.2 ho_search_1997-1 . It is a member of the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

sources.summary: The review contrasts NGC 4051 with NGC 7213, noting marginal radio variability, no clear X-ray correlation, and VLBI evidence for a weak collimated jet.

quotes.quote: Although NGC 7213 showed significant radio variability and some evidence for an X-ray/radio correlation, the lower mass and higher accretion rate radio-quiet Narrow Line Seyfert 1 AGN, NGC 4051 shows only marginal evidence for radio variability with 25 fractional rms, with no clear evidence for a correlation with X-rays which are highly variable Jones2011 . However, NGC 4051 does show perhaps the clearest evidence yet for a highly-collimated jet in a radio-quiet AGN, in the form of VLBI images showing a pair of unresolved steep-spectrum radio `hotspots' equidistant from and aligned with the weak, central flat-spectrum radio core Giroletti2009,Jones2011 . These findings are important because they strongly suggest that weak jets are present in a type of AGN which is considered to be an analogue of soft state BHXRBs (e.g. see Koerding, this volume).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4051 as an AGN whose X-ray/optical reverberation shows a 2.4-day primary lag and a 38.9-day secondary lag consistent with dust sublimation.

quotes.quote: For example, Breedt10 detect secondary peaks between the X-ray and optical bandpasses in NGC 4051. While the primary peak shows the optical emission lagging behind the X-rays with a delay of = 2.4^ +0.9 _ 1.6 d, the secondary peak is much larger, with = 38.9^ +2.7 _ 8.4 d. Breedt10 find that this secondary time delay is consistent with the dust sublimation radius in NGC 4051 and so may be the optical signature of the next-largest structure; i.e. the inner edge of the torus.

sources.summary: The paper uses warm-absorber data for NGC 4051 in a Seyfert sample to infer ambient circumnuclear medium density profiles of roughly n ^ 1.7 to n ^ 2.15.

quotes.quote: We study the correlation between the outflow velocity and ionization parameter of WAs in each of the five Seyfert 1 galaxies (NGC 3227, NGC 3783, NGC 4051, NGC 4593, and NGC 5548), inferring that the density profile of the ACM is between n ^ 1.7 and n ^ 2.15 ( n is number density and is distance) from 0.01 pc to pc scales in these five AGNs.

The density profile indexes k of the ACM in the five Seyfert 1 galaxies of our sample (NGC 3227, NGC 3783, NGC 4051, NGC 4593, and NGC 5548) are within the range of k for the ACM in TDEs between 1 and 2.5 ; Alexander2020 (see Figure fig:kdis ).
According to the fitting results of the v_ out relation, we infer that the density profile of the ACM is between n ^ 1.7 and n ^ 2.15 from 0.01 pc to pc scales in these five AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4051 as a low-luminosity Seyfert comparison where modeled turnover distances fail, suggesting unresolved or additional NLR outflow acceleration.

quotes.quote: More recently, in Meena2021 (hereafter Meena2021 ), we employed a similar dynamical model of radiative acceleration and gravitational deceleration for the outflows in the Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4051.
This trend does, however, seem to break for the low mass, low luminosity AGN in NGC 4051, which has a much smaller modeled turnover ( 2 pc) than the observed values (380 pc, Meena2021 ).

This leaves us with two scenarios that could be the cause of presented difference in our model: (1) Radiation pressure may not be the only and/or main force behind the driving and acceleration of NLR outflows in NGC 4051; and (2) We severely overestimated the observed turnover distance in Meena2021 , because for this low-luminosity AGN the radiation driving model indicates that the turnover is at 4 pc, which is inside of the central resolution element of 8 pc and is therefore undetectable.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4051 as a narrow-line Seyfert whose low-black-hole-mass point strongly affects the fitted black-hole/bulge relation; excluding it would flatten Laor's slope and increase uncertainty.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, a single point significantly influences the slope - the NLS1 NGC 4051 - which extends the dynamical range by almost an order of magnitude. If it were excluded (being a narrow line object), the slope of the fit would decrease considerably (and the standard deviation in the slope would increase). Excluding NGC 4051 and adding the measurement error would flatten the slope and increase the uncertainty in Laor's fit, which would ecome consistent with linearity.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4051 as a Seyfert example where radio emission comes from both a luminous AGN core and extended star formation in spiral arms.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:gal4051 we can see radio contours overlayed onto an optical image of the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC4051. As well as the core being very luminous in the radio, there is also more extended radio emission from the starformation in the spiral arms. Although in this case the AGN outshines the radio emission from starformation by an order of magnitude, it does not mean that in other galaxies with less active black holes and weaker radio jets that the AGN and starformation will not be radiating more equally.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC4051 as an AGN whose reflection flux saturates with primary emission and whose low-state variability challenges some light-bending parameter choices.

quotes.quote: Moreover in at least two AGN, MGC 6-30-15 (Miniutti et al 2003) and NGC4051 (Ponti et al. 2006) and one X-ray binary (XTE J1650-500, Rossi et al. 2005) the reflection flux is correlated to the primary emission at low fluxes and saturates at higher fluxes.

In particular the curious behaviour of MGC 6-30-15, NGC4051, XTE J1650-500, where the reflection flux is correlated to the primary emission at low fluxes and saturates at higher fluxes, is in qualitative agreement with the predictions of this model.

At small source heights ( h<5 R_g ), the overall trend is that the reflection and primary emission are weakly correlated: the reflected flux changes by at most 50 when the primary flux increases by more than one order of magnitude which might be in qualitative agreement with some observations but is inconsistent with the strong non-linear correlation observed for instance in the low state of NGC4051.

gmw_00000201.name: NGC 4074
gmw_00000201.overview: NGC 4074 is a peculiar early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0224 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits a double power-law X-ray spectrum with strong hard-band absorption [arXiv:1005.0373].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4074 with a double power-law X-ray spectrum, finding strong hard-band absorption, no iron line, and a de-absorbed 2-10 keV luminosity.

quotes.quote: The \ spectrum of NGC 4074 (bottom-left panel in Fig. A.1; exposure time of 2.6 ks) requires a double power-law model, with the component at energies above 3 keV being absorbed by a column density 2.4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 (assuming =1.8 ). No iron line is present, with a 90 upper limit on the EW of a neutral iron line of 190 eV. The measured 0.5--2 keV (2--10 keV) flux is 8.6 10^ 14 \ ( 2.59 10^ 12 ), while the de-absorbed, rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity is 8.0 10^ 42 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4074's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4074 is a peculiar early-type galaxy with a high surface brightness at a redshift of z= 0.0224 ( D 107 Mpc). It contains a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. The NLR and radio emission appears to be mostly compact with weak extended radio emission along a PA 131 mulchaey_emission-line_1996,nagar_radio_1999 .

gmw_00000202.name: NGC 4111
gmw_00000202.overview: NGC 4111 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1604.07624, arXiv:2203.02532] located in the Ursa Major group [arXiv:1604.07624] that hosts a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] and a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0604487, arXiv:2203.02532]. The galaxy features a dusty polar ring [arXiv:2203.02532] and an edge-on dust torus perpendicular to its disk plane [arXiv:1603.00019].

sources.summary: The paper studies 4111 as an edge-on S0-a galaxy in a dense group, finding similar intermediate-age thin and thick disc populations with nearly constant abundance ratios.

quotes.quote: The Sample We chose three edge-on galaxies in different environments: 4111, 4710 and 5422 morphologically classified as S0-a by Hyperleda Makarov2014 . 4111 ( M_J = -22.40 mag) is a member of the Ursa Major galaxy group that is known to contain a common extended envelope Wolfinger2013 . The distances from 4111 to the nearest neighbours are about 30-40 kpc Pak2014,Karachentsev2013 .

4111 In Fig. fig1 (left plots) we show the SSP-modelling results of 4111 stellar population and kinematic data. Comeron2014 have estimated the thin and thick disc masses to be 15 3 10^9 \ and 5 1 10^9 \ respectively, and Comeron2012 have demonstrated that the thick disc of 4111 dominates the luminosity at z>25.4 arcsec ( 1.8 kpc) in the inner region. However, according to their models, already at =40 arcsec the thick disc contribution represents about 80 per cent of the total luminosity fraction in the mid-plane and reaches 90 per cent at z=5 arcsec were we placed the slit. Our data extend out to 6.2 kpc from the centre that corresponds to 10.0 and 2.7 thin and thick disc scale-lengths respectively. Nevertheless, we do not see significant differences of stellar populations in the outer regions of the 4111 discs.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4111's polar-ring gas and stellar kinematics, arguing captured dwarf-galaxy gas is inflowing through a molecular ring and may feed AGN activity and star formation.

quotes.quote: We have used Hubble Space Telescope (HST) images, SAURON Integral Field Spectroscopy (IFS) and adaptative optics assisted Gemini NIFS near-infrared K-band IFS to map the stellar and gas distribution, excitation and kinematics of the inner few kpc of the nearby edge-on S0 galaxy NGC 4111. The HST images map its 450 pc diameter dusty polar ring, with an estimated gas mass 10^7 M _ . The NIFS datacube maps the inner 110 pc radius at 7 pc spatial resolution revealing a 220 pc diameter polar ring in hot (2267 166 K) molecular H _2 1-0 S(1) gas embedded in the polar ring.

The large-scale [ O iii ] 5007 \ velocity field shows a superposition of two disk kinematics: one similar to that of the stars and another along the polar ring, showing non-circular motions that seem to connect with the velocity field of the nuclear H _2 ring, whose kinematics indicate accelerated inflow to the nucleus. The estimated mass inflow rate is enough not only to feed an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) but also to trigger circumnuclear star formation in the near future. We propose a scenario in which gas from the polar ring, which probably originated from the capture of a dwarf galaxy, is moving inwards and triggering an AGN, as supported by the local X-ray emission, which seems to be the source of the H _2 1-0 S(1) excitation.

sources.summary: NGC 4111 is described as the sample’s most disky S0, with a small effective radius and major-axis rotation that remains sustained to 4 effective radii.

quotes.quote: This is the most disky galaxy (S0) in our sample, and has the smallest effective radius (i.e., <1 kpc). As a result, our kinematic data extend out to 4 . In the inner parts, long slit data from Loyer:1998a show a bump in the major axis rotation at a galactocentric radius of roughly 5 arcsec with otherwise constant amplitude farther out around 150 km s ^ 1 . Our map shows that this rotation is sustained up to 4 .

sources.summary: NGC 4111 is presented as a nearly edge-on disk and the flattest galaxy modeled, with Spitzer imaging where the new method yields a cleaner residual image.

quotes.quote: The second galaxy modelled in this section was deliberately chosen from a survey dedicated to GC studies, namely the SLUGGS http://sluggs.swin.edu.au/ survey ( Usher2012 , Brodie2012 ). The galaxy in question, NGC 4111, is another example of a disk viewed at close to 90 inclination, and is in fact the `flattest' galaxy modelled in this paper. The data was again retrieved from S4G archive, and consisted of an infrared (4.5 ) Spitzer observation (IRAC 2 instrument, PI: K. Sheth). The results are displayed in Figure fig:n4111 , where again we notice the contaminated residual image produced with as well as the `cleaner' residual obtained with with .

sources.summary: The paper finds an apparent inner peanut in NGC 4111 but attributes the pinched isophotes to an edge-on dust torus pending higher-resolution near-infrared imaging.

quotes.quote: NGC 4111 -- This galaxy appears to host an inner peanut as well, at 2 , in the SDSS i -band image. However, a closer inspection reveals an edge-on dust torus perpendicular to the disc plane at precisely this spatial scale. The obscuration which this induces causes the isophotes to appear `pinched', just as they would if an inner peanut were there. Therefore, we chose to not classify this as an inner peanut, at least not until higher-resolution NIR imaging is available (NB: The `inner peanut' does not show at all in the B_6 profile extracted from the Spitzer imaging, both 3.6 and 4.5 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies a hard central point source in NGC 4111 with radio emission and high central X-ray contribution, interpreting it as a likely low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 4111 (L2, S0) has only one central point source with a hard X-ray spectrum. There is also a fair amount of diffuse emission. The spectrum extracted from the central source does not have enough counts to justify fitting models to it but the luminosity can be estimated to be L_ 0.5-10 keV = 4.4 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 and L_ 2-10 keV = 3.7 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . There is a detection of a central source in the radio with S_ 5 GHz =2.3 mJy WH91 , which means that R_ X = -3 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4111's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4111 is an edge-on early-type spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 15 1.5 Mpc tonry_sbf_2001 with a LINER nucleus ho_search_1997-1 . The nuclear remained undetected in subarcsecond-resolution radio observations nagar_radio_2000 , while it appears a compact hard source in X-rays gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2006,flohic_central_2006 . The H emission has a compact component and an extended halo with a major axis of 6 0.4 kpc length along a PA coincident with the galaxy major axis ( 150 ; masegosa_nature_2011 ).

gmw_00000203.name: NGC 4138
gmw_00000203.overview: NGC 4138 is classified as either an early-type spiral [arXiv:1310.2770] or a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1605.01638] that hosts a Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:1904.03694]. The galaxy features two counter-rotating stellar disks [arXiv:1409.3086, arXiv:1605.01638], a star-forming ring [arXiv:1409.3086, arXiv:2402.08123], and a surrounding dust lane [arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4138 hosts two counter-rotating stellar disks; the younger, slightly metal-poorer component and star-forming ring favor external gas accretion or a gas-rich dwarf merger.

quotes.quote: The Sa spiral NGC 4138 is known to host two counter-rotating stellar disks, with the ionized gas co-rotating with one of them. We measured the kinematics and properties of the two counter-rotating stellar populations to constrain their formation scenario. The counter-rotating stellar population is younger, marginally more metal poor, and more enhanced than the main stellar component. The younger stellar component is also associated with a star-forming ring.

The different properties of the counter-rotating stellar components of NGC 4138 rule out the idea that they formed because of bar dissolution. Our findings support the results of numerical simulations in which the counter-rotating component assembled from gas accreted on retrograde orbits from the environment or from the retrograde merging with a gas-rich dwarf galaxy. According to the simulations by Thakar1997 , the ring of NGC 4138 was created by counter-rotating gas clouds colliding with co-rotating gas already present in the disk and forming stars in the process.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 4138, modeling PAH emission in its LLAGN, bulge, disk, and star-forming ring, and finding radial PAH-ratio suppression not solely controlled by the AGN.

quotes.quote: We present a focused study of radially-resolved varying PAH emission in the low-luminosity AGN-host NGC 4138 using deep Spitzer /IRS spectral maps. Separately, we model the effects of the varying radiation field within NGC 4138, and we use this model to predict the corresponding changes in the PAH emission spectrum.

NGC 4138 provides a convenient environment for this, with a pronounced star-forming ring embedded in a smooth disk of older stars ngc4138_disk_properties, ngc4138_ages . In our radiation model, NGC 4138 is idealized as four components: an AGN, a bulge, a stellar disk, and a star-forming ring embedded in the disk.

The main conclusions from this work are the following: The observed short-to-long PAH band ratios in NGC 4138 are variable with distance from the nucleus. These ratios are suppressed at small radii, and smoothly increase to values typical for star-forming galaxies near the star-forming ring. Outside of the ring, the ratios typically fall back to values seen at small radii, indicating that the AGN does not dictate the band ratios at kpc scales.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4138's outer profile as III-d and describes spiral structure, dust features, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4138 (III-d; SA()0 ^+ ) : The bulge of this galaxy is surrounded by a dust lane, which subtends almost a semi-circle at the center. The contrast between this dust feature and the bright spiral structure arising from the bulge can explain the ``ring'' classification of this object. In the brightest zone, at 20 (1.3 kpc) radius the ellipticity peaks, and there is slight bump in the brightness profile. The profile as a whole is of Type III, with a sharp change of slope at the break point. Traces of spiral structure can be seen out to approximately the break radius; the ellipticity of the isophotes remains almost perfectly constant out to at least 140 (9 kpc), making this a clear III-d profile.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4138 as an SA0 Ursa Major galaxy with extensively studied HI kinematics, an uncertain warped outer disc, and a counterrotating stellar disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 4138 NGC 4138 is an SA0 galaxy in the Ursa Major cluster. The kinematics and distribution of mass in NGC 4138 have been studied extensively using HI synthesis observations from both the WSRT ( ursamajhi ) and VLA ( n4138jbh ). The HI observations presented in Fig.  fig:n4138sum are taken from . In , they derive two rotation curves: one which rapidly declines (more so than is typically observed) and one which they fix to be flat, which then implies a strong outer warp with a decreasing inclination angle. It's not possible to confirm which model is correct based on the velocity field, but the diffuse minor axis gas appears as if it has face-on orbits, which supports the possibility of a smaller inclination angle at large radii.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4138 as a nearby controversial declining-rotation-curve galaxy, deriving f(R) lensing and rotation properties plus a characteristic halo-length scale.

quotes.quote: For instance, we work with the two nearby controversial galaxies NGC 5533 and NGC 4138 and explore their galactic features by analysing the lensing angle profiles in f(R) background. We study the two controversial galaxies i.e., NGC 5533 and NGC 4138 via the observations of rotational velocity profile beyond the optical disk size and lensing angle profile in f(R) background. In Section rgb 1.00,0.00,0.00 (III) , We explore the galactic dynamics via the f(R) rotational velocity profiles for the nearby galaxies (under controversy due to their declining rotation curve) NGC 5533 and NGC 4138 and obtain their respective galactic length scale free parameter r_0 with O(10^ 6 ) .

The magnitude of effective f(R) lensing angle is _ Net 4.2 10^ 6 and _ Net 1.5 10^ 6 for NGC 5533 and NGC 4138, respectively, with the corresponding approximate closest approach at about 22 kpc and 6 kpc in f(R) background. For the two galaxies (NGC 5533 and NGC 4138), we have the value of such approximate f(R) characteristic length as 446.600 kpc and 177.828 kpc which might represent their halo size.

sources.summary: The paper adds GTC/CanariCam N-band spectra for Seyfert galaxy NGC 4138 and uses pseudo-nuclear spectra, noting the spectrum is noisy but nearly identical to the AGN component.

quotes.quote: New Observations Gran Telescopio CANARIAS/CanariCam We obtained subarcsecond resolution N-band spectra (7.5-13 m) of two Seyfert galaxies (NGC 4138 and UGC 6728) using the low spectral resolution (R 175) grating available in the instrument CanariCam (CC; Telesco03,Packham2005 ), on the 10.4 m Gran Telescopio CANARIAS (GTC). NGC 4138 and UGC 6728 were observed in 2016 March and the slit, of width 0.52 arcsec, was oriented at PA= 145 and 150 degrees, respectively.

Note that we also use the ``pseudo-nuclear'' spectra for NGC 4138, NGC 4945, NGC 7172 and UGC 6728. In the case of NGC 4945 and NGC 7172, their nuclear spectra show a strong contribution from the host galaxy, while those of NGC 4138 and UGC 6728 are very noisy and practically identical in spectral shape to the AGN component.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4138's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4138 is an early-type spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 13.8 1.8 Mpc with a Sy 1.9 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample and is an X-ray ``buried" AGN candidate noguchi_new_2009 . A weak compact radio nucleus was detected in NGC 4138 ho_radio_2001 . This object has not been detected with but with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 4138 with the baseline plus one thermal component, improving the fit and yielding absorption and photon-index parameters consistent with earlier XMM-Newton work.

quotes.quote: The base-line model combined with a single apec model well reproduces the X-ray spectra of NGC 4138 with ^2/d.o.f = 78.1/94 . The addition of the apec model improves the fit by ^2 = 26.9 for two degrees of freedom (temperature and normalization). We obtain N_ H =7.82^ +0.72 _ 0.68 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and = 1.59^ +0.17 _ 0.06 , which are consistent with the result of the XMM-Newton observation in 2001 Cap06 .

gmw_00000204.name: NGC 4235
gmw_00000204.overview: NGC 4235 is a nearly edge-on [arXiv:2008.06039], highly inclined S0 [arXiv:2210.12255] or spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] hosting a Seyfert 1 nucleus [arXiv:0809.1750, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161] and an X-shaped bulge [arXiv:2210.12255]. The active nucleus exhibits X-ray variability [arXiv:0809.1750] and powers extended radio lobes [arXiv:1603.08364], while the galaxy's disk is weakly warped [arXiv:2210.12255] and bisected by a dust lane [arXiv:1503.01836].

sources.summary: The paper selects NGC 4235 as a low-Eddington Seyfert 1 and finds significant longer-baseline X-ray variability after its shorter MOS light curve appeared non-variable.

quotes.quote: We chose four from these sources, namely NGC 4235, NGC 4639, NGC 5033 and NGC 5273, because there exist estimates of their central black hole mass (hence we can estimate their L _ Edd ) and their nuclear luminosity is small: their L _ X /L _ Edd ratio is smaller than 10^ 3 . Therefore, it is possible that accretion in these objects operates in a radiatively inefficient mode. First, we used archival \ and \ data to measure their X-ray variability amplitude and we compared it with the variability amplitude of more luminous Seyferts.

NGC 4235 and NGC 4639 do not show significant variations: ^ 2 _ 4235 = 15.07/21 degrees of freedom (dof), and ^ 2 _ 4639 = 26.1/27 dof, respectively. On the other hand we do observe significant variations in the NGC 5503 and NGC 5273 light curves ( ^ 2 _ 5033 =44.3/22 and ^ 2 _ 5273 =552/30 dof, respectively). We use the normalized excess variance, ^ 2 _ NXS (e.g. Nandra \ 1997) as a measure of the intrinsic variability amplitude of the light curves which show significant variations. This estimator is defined as, ^ 2 _ NXS = S^ 2 < _ err ^ 2 > <x>^2 , where <x> , and S^ 2 =(1/ N_ data ) _ i=1 ^ N_ data (x_ i <x>)^2 are the mean and variance of the light curve, while < ^2_ err >=(1/ N_ data ) _ i=1 ^ N_ data ^ 2 _ err,i is the average contribution of the Poisson noise process to the observed scatter around the mean (N _ data is the number of points in the light curve). Its square root (the so called ``fractional root mean square amplitude", f_ rms ) indicates the average variability amplitude of a source as a fraction of the light curve mean. We find that f_ rms,5033 =4 1 and f_ rms,5273 =18 1 (errors account only for the measurement errors in the light curve points and have been estimated according to the prescription of Vaughan , 2003). In order to investigate further the variability of NGC 4235 and NGC 4639, we considered \ data as well. The main advantage of the \ light curves is that they are are longer than the \ light curves. In the case of NGC 4235, we employed its 0.5-10 keV, 5760 s binned, SIS 0+1 combined light curves from the 58 ksec long, December 1998 observation (sequence number 76005000; the light curves were downloaded from the TARTARUS database). The background subtracted light curve is plotted in the upper panel of Fig. fig:lcs2 . We detect significant variations ( ^ 2 =39.8/10 dof) with f_ rms,0.5-10 keV =6.2 1.8 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies mild mid-plane asymmetry in NGC 4235, a highly inclined S0 galaxy with a prominent X-shaped B/P bulge, weak warping, and asymmetric surface-brightness features.

quotes.quote: We identify a mild mid-plane asymmetry in NGC 4235 and NGC 5170 similar to what we found in model D5. NGC 4235 is a highly-inclined S0 galaxy, hosting a prominent X-shaped structure (visible in the unsharp mask in Fig.  fig:sample ) associated with a B/P bulge Buta2015 . The X-shaped structure has a semi-major axis of 4.5 kpc and a semi-minor axis of 4.2 kpc. The disc is weakly warped in the very outer region resulting in a weak quadrupolar signal at |x| 130 arcsec.

The red contours themselves appear asymmetric with respect to the mid-plane. The mid-plane asymmetry map reveals asymmetries at the edges of the X shape and further out, which extend till 25 x 75 arcsec before they disappear further out. However, these asymmetries are not symmetric with respect to the z axis, being stronger and extending further on the right side compared to the left (the peaks are located at x 5 kpc and at x 10 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4235 has complex star formation across the field, partial young and intermediate-age rings, widespread old stars, nuclear hot dust, and off-nuclear featureless continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4235: This galaxy shows a very complex SFH over the full FoV. A (partial) ring of xy is clearly present, with values reaching 50 percent to northern ( > 130 pc) and western ( 100 pc) locations, where a vertical stripe can also be seen, and which may be attributed to a residual fingerprint. Small fractions ( 20 percent) of the xyo SPC are detected in NW and SE directions ( 130 pc), while a partial ring of intermediate population ( xiy and xio ) is detected towards E-S-W directions surrounding the centre (50 pc << 150 pc). The xo component shows up on the full FoV with values reaching 50 percent.

A clear dominance (75 percent) of hot dust emission is seen in the unresolved nucleus ( < 25pc) with a contribution of FC emission of less than 20 percent. The HD component shows a steep gradient decreasing out to the edge of the FoV ( 200 pc), while the FC seems to reach its maximum (20 percent) for 40 pc towards SE directions.

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT observations of NGC4235 that detect radio lobes, notes unresolved 8.4 GHz VLBI cores, and plans lower-frequency VLBI to probe parsec-scale jets.

quotes.quote: In order to image the typically steep-spectrum radio lobes, we have observed two Seyfert galaxies, viz., NGC4235 and NGC4594 (the Sombrero), with the GMRT at 325 and 610 MHz. Radio lobes that were previously indicated in the 5 GHz VLA observations of Gallimore06 are also detected with the GMRT.

We are currently in the process of analysing the 325 MHz data and creating the spectral index maps for these Seyferts. The 8.4 GHz VLBI images of these galaxies that are available in the literature reveal unresolved cores in both Anderson04,Ojha05 . However, since lower frequency VLBI data exists in the NRAO archive, we plan to obtain greater information on the parsec-scale jets in these sources.

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT and infrared analysis of NGC 4235, finding radio lobes, a possible relic lobe from earlier AGN activity, and insufficient star formation to power the lobes.

quotes.quote: Low frequency observations at 325 and 610 MHz have been carried out for two ``radio-loud'' Seyfert galaxies, NGC 4235 and NGC 4594 (Sombrero galaxy), using the Giant Meterwave Radio Telescope (GMRT). The 610 MHz total intensity and 325-610 MHz spectral index images of NGC 4235 tentatively suggest the presence of a ``relic'' radio lobe, most likely from a previous episode of AGN activity. This makes NGC 4235 only the second known Seyfert galaxy after Mrk 6 to show signatures of episodic activity. Spitzer and Herschel infrared spectral energy distribution (SED) modelling using the clumpyDREAM code predicts star formation rates (SFR) that are an order of magnitude lower than those required to power the radio lobes in these Seyferts ( 0.13-0.23 M _ yr ^ 1 compared to the required SFR of 2.0-2.7 M _ yr ^ 1 in NGC 4594 and NGC 4235, respectively). This finding along with the detection of parsec and sub-kpc radio jets in both Seyfert galaxies, that are roughly along the same position angles as the radio lobes, strongly support the suggestion that Seyfert lobes are AGN-powered.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4235 has low-sigma patches at about 200 pc and a weaker h3-velocity anti-correlation trend.

quotes.quote: Patches of low- are seen for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899, while three objects (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388) show a centrally peaked distribution. These rings and partial rings are located at distances from the nucleus ranging between 150 and 250 pc. Nuclear values for each galaxy are presented in the last column of Table tab_mod , obtained by fitting the nuclear spectrum integrated within a circular aperture with radius of 75 pc. Most galaxies show low h_ 3 values ( 0.10 <h_ 3 < 0.10), suggesting that their LOSVDs are well represented by a Gaussian velocity distribution. Exceptions are Mrk 607, Mrk1066 and NGC 4051 that show h_ 3 values larger than 0.15. For 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066), the h_ 3 map is anti-correlated with the velocity field, with positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where rotation velocities are observed in blueshifts and negative h_ 3 values associated to regions where the rotation velocities are observed in redshifts.
A similar trend (but not so clear) is observed for other four galaxies (NGC 4235, NGC 4388, NGC 5929 and Mrk 1157).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4235 as a nearly edge-on Seyfert with a compact X-ray source, extended optical emission, extraplanar radio structure, variability, and possible relic lobes.

quotes.quote: NGC 4235 NGC 4235 is a nearly edge-on, SAa type galaxy perez1998 with a Seyfert type 1 nucleus weedman1978,jimenez2000 with high [ O III ] to H line ratios at the core. NGC 4235 hosts a bright compact X-ray source fabbiano1992 , whereas find a bright nucleus and an extended emission feature in H images. and also find extended [ O III ] emission along the major axis, whereas no minor axis extension is detected colbert1996a . However, the imaging study carried out by at 6 cm using the C-array configuration of VLA reveals an extraplanar bubble-like structure which extends up to 9 kpcs along with the compact core emission. The radio source appears unresolved in VLBA imaging, while it shows evidence for variability in flux density in the VLA images anderson2005 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4235 as having a bright nuclear region and dust lane, with narrowband emission limited to the nucleus and no detected disk H II regions or extraplanar emission.

quotes.quote: The R band image of NGC 4235 (Figure f:ngc4235 ) 17 reveals a bright nuclear region, with a faint dust lane visible on either side of the nucleus. All of the and emission originates from the nucleus. This is in agreement with previous observations pog89,col96,ros03b . Absorption due to the dust lane is visible to the NW of the nucleus in both narrowband images. The absence of H ii regions in the disk and the absence of extraplanar emission in both maps is in agreement with , although deeper observations by ros03b reveal faint extended emission in .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4235 as a nearly edge-on Seyfert 1.2 galaxy whose maps show central AGN continuum emission and whose line strengths may reflect non-stellar continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4235 is a nearly edge-on Seyfert 1.2 galaxy. In the line strength maps one can see the continuum emission of the central AGN in the very centre. Jim enez-Benito et al. (2000) present Fe, Mg and Ca absorption line strengths and find that the Ca triplet lines are stronger than expected from the other lines. This is likely to be due to the non-stellar continuum.

sources.summary: NGC 4235 is presented as a nearly edge-on Seyfert 1 in a non-interacting pair, with regular stellar rotation, an inner stellar disk, and centrally concentrated emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4235 is a nearly edge-on Seyfert 1 galaxy jdtt00 in a non-interacting pair with NGC 4246 at 12 . The stellar velocity field displays regular rotation and evidence for the presence of a smaller stellar disk in the inner parts. This is supported by a decrease in velocity dispersion and h_3 anti-correlation in the centre of the galaxy. The \ emission is much more extended along the main disc of the galaxy than the \ emission, which is mainly confined near the centre of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4235's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4235 is a nearly edge-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.008 ( D 41.2 Mpc) with a Sy 1.2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which is detected but appears unresolved at radio wavelengths ulvestad_radio_1984,kukula_high-resolution_1995 . The NLR appears compact with a possible extension towards the north-east with a PA 48 and a length of 4.4 0.9 kpc pogge_circumnuclear_1989 . The first N band detection of NGC 4235 is reported in .

sources.summary: NGC 4235 is presented as a nearly edge-on Virgo Seyfert with a dust-lane-bisected center, compact narrow-line region, and broad near-infrared permitted helium emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4235 (S1.2) The center of this nearly edge-on galaxy in the Virgo Cluster is bisected by a dust lane. The nucleus displays an emission line spectrum characteristic of type 1 Seyfert galaxies, although optical spectroscopy shows that the broad H line has a peculiar secondary hump redward of [ N ii ] . The NLR appears compact with a possible extension in [ O iii ] towards the north-east with a PA 48 and a length of 4.4 or 0.9 kpc Pogge88 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4235 as borderline Seyfert 1.2/1.5 and notes its photon flux and density are poorly constrained, with one of the lowest photon fluxes in the sample.

quotes.quote: Except for MCG--05-23-16 and NGC 4235, where n_ H and _ H are poorly constrained because only three line ratios were measured for these objects, the number of valid grid points for each object is small, eight at most.
NGC 4235 : [ O III ] /H = 0.5 in this object, we classify it as a Seyfert 1.2 (noting that based on , this is borderline between Seyfert 1.2 and 1.5).

With the exception of MCG--05-23-16, for which the photon flux is poorly constrained as it was estimated based on only three line ratios, and NGC 4235, which have the lowest photon flux in the sample, the distances are consistent with a significant amount of the H I emitting gas being located at regions near (or even at) the dust sublimation radius.

sources.summary: The paper presents sub-parsec VLBA imaging and radio SED results for NGC 4235, describing its central radio morphology and spectral shape in the FRAMEx AGN sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 4235 [ 12 ^ h 17 ^ m 09 88175, + 07 11 29 670 ] : L, C, and X band images showed a point-like structure, with a little extended feature in the X band image; thus, the integrated flux density in X band is a little higher compared to C band flux density. NGC 4235 is another example of our sample with a GHz peaked spectrum found in four-frequency SED with a turnover frequency of about 4.3 GHz. Higher frequency spectrum, including the 5 nondetection upper limit in the K band, showed a steeper spectral index ( 1.6).

gmw_00000205.name: NGC 4258
gmw_00000205.overview: NGC 4258 is a barred spiral Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1110.1275] hosting an active galactic nucleus with a warped, nearly edge-on water-maser accretion disk [arXiv:0804.1013]. This disk orbits a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1802.05133] and drives a perpendicular radio jet [arXiv:1711.04447].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4258 as a Seyfert II maser galaxy for estimating SMBH magnetic fields from polarization, emphasizing its edge-on accretion disk, pc-scale jet, and maser field limits.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 is a low-luminosity Seyfert II galaxy at the distance of about 7.2 Mpc, which harbors water masers (Modjaz et al. 2005). This object is usually considered as a very good laboratory for successfully measuring the magnetic field in accretion disk even very close to the central black hole. The spatial velocity distribution of water mega-maser sources in NGC 4258 on scales of 0.14-0.28 pc indicates a thin Keplerian disk rotating around a black hole with a mass M_ BH = 3.9 10^7 M_ (Herrnstein et al. 1999). The accretion disk has an almost edge-on orientation with the radiation axis and its inclination angle is i = 83^ 4^ (Pringle et al. 1999). A pc-scale jet closely aligns in projection on the sky with the rotation axis (Herrnstein et al. 1997).

Modjaz et al. (2005) present an analysis of polarimetric observations at 22 GHz of the water vapor masers in NGC 4258 obtained with the VLA and the GBT. They do not detect any circular polarization in the spectrum indicative of Zeeman-induced splitting of the maser lines of water, and obtained only an upper limit on the magnetic field strengths. They obtained the 1- upper limit value of the toroidal component of the magnetic field at a radius of 0.2 pc the value of 90 mG and determined a 1- upper limit of 30 mG on the radial component at a radius of 0.14 pc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as a heavily obscured X-ray nucleus with variable absorption, extensive archival observations, and a fitted average spectral slope used in the AGN sample.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of NGC 4258 is heavily obscured in the X--ray band. Absorption column density estimates of the order of a few 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 have been reported in the past (Yang \ 2007). The absorption also varies on time scales of months and years (Fruscione \ 2005). NGC 4258 has been observed extensively by . There are 707 observations (obtained until the end of 2006) in the archive.

We reduced the data for all of them, and fitted the resulting spectra with a wabs PLG model, keeping N _ H fixed at 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 . The model fitted the \ spectra well. The mean spectral slope is equal to 2.02.

sources.summary: The paper presents IFU spectroscopy of active spiral NGC 4258, detecting blueshifted neutral gas and linking its anomalous arms to shocks, jets, or a hypershell wind.

quotes.quote: We present integral field spectroscopic observations of the central region of the active galaxy NGC 4258 obtained with the fibre IFU system INTEGRAL. We have been able to detect cold neutral gas by means of the interstellar NaD doublet absorption and to trace its distribution and kinematics with respect to the underlying disc. The neutral gas is blue-shifted with projected velocities in the 120--370 km/s range.

The nearby spiral galaxy NGC 4258 (M106) is a particularly interesting galaxy. It presents two peculiarities that have been the cause of many studies of this object: the confirmation of the existence at its centre of a massive black hole, detected in 1995 through measurements of high speed motions in the water masers ( ), and the existence of the mysterious anomalous arms discovered in the optical by and in radio by . As we shall see below, it is indeed not unlikely that both phenomena are closely related.

Our observations are consistent with the jet model presented by in which a cocoon originating from the nuclear jet is shocking the gas in the galaxy disc. Alternatively, our observations are also consistent with the bipolar hypershell model of and .

sources.summary: The paper applies its Keplerian disk model to NGC 4258, concluding the central disk rotation is black-hole dominated while maser observations trace a thin gas disk around the nucleus.

quotes.quote: These results are applied to NGC 4258, the unique AGN with a finely measured Keplerian rotation curve of the central disk, with the conclusion that its rotation curve is, in fact, determined by the central black hole. The mass of the accretion disk exceeds 100 .

The number of known AGN's with masers is increasing in recent years (see also for a review ), but there there is only one case of well resolved Keplerian rotation curve --- in the AGN of NGC 4258. Observations of supermasers near the center of the galaxy NGC 4258 provide evidence for a rotating disk of gas surrounding a black hole with a mass = (3.9 0.3) 10^7 . The motion is Keplerian to high precision, of about a part in a hundred , and this mass estimate assumes that velocities are due solely to the gravitational force of the central body. A similar result has been obtained by Siopis et al.  , who determined the mass of the black hole by constructing axisymmetric dynamical models of the galaxy NGC 4258.

The masers are observed in the annular region that extends from 0.13 pc to 0.26 pc from the center, and the region with detected masers is thin --- the maximal relative height is circa 1/800 (these distances can be expressed in terms of the Schwarzschild radius: r_ g G / ^2 = 5.8 10^ 12 cm and 1 pc = 5.4 10^5 r_ g ). There exists evidence that the disk fills not only the annular region in which masers are detected, but that it extends outwards from a vicinity of the black hole horizon.

sources.summary: The thesis uses NGC 4258 as the main water-maser calibrator, describing its warped disk, maser kinematics, distance estimates, and modified-gravity constraints.

quotes.quote: In this work, we will only make use of the most studied galaxy, NGC 4258 , and so from here on we will concentrate on this galaxy only. The principle is similar for other galaxies and a full account of water masers can be found in . NGC 4258 has a thin, slightly warped accretion disk whose shape has been well-studied (see and references therein). This disk rotates at 1000 kms ^ 1 and contains several masing clouds of water, which are classified into two types. Systemic masers are those on the near-edge of the disk and move with the systemic velocity of the galaxy ( 470 kms ^ 1 ).
They drift in position and LOS velocity of 30 as yr ^ 1 and 9 kms ^ 1 yr ^ 1 respectively with respect to the motion of the disk.

As discussed in section subsec:masers , there is only one galaxy for which we have simultaneous distance estimates from water masers and other methods: the spiral galaxy NGC 4258. The two distance estimates using the positional drift and the Keplarian motion methods agree within errors (see ) and a comparison with the TRGB and Cepheid distance estimates (taken from ) is shown below: NGC 4258 \ Maser : d &=& 7.2 0.2 \ Mpc NGC 4258 \ Maser : d &=& 7.1 0.2 \ Mpc NGC 4258 \ Cepheid : d &=& 7.18 0.07 ( statistical ) \ Mpc NGC 4258 \ TRGB : d &=& 7.18 0.13 0.40 \ Mpc .

sources.summary: The paper tests NGC 4258 as an independent SBF calibrator because of its maser distance, but clumpy dust and recent star formation make its fluctuation magnitude too bright for reliable calibration.

quotes.quote: Another approach is to find other distance indicators that are independent of the Cepheid calibration, such as the geometrical distance to NGC 4258. Water masers orbiting the central black hole in NGC 4258 have now been used to accurately determine the distance to this galaxy using a purely geometrical technique based on the Keplerian orbits of the masers (Humphreys et al. \ 2013). While SBF magnitudes are best measured in early-type galaxies, archival \ images of the central bulge of NGC 4258 (GO-11570) provided us with an opportunity to explore the SBF calibration independent of the Cepheid distance scale. Given that an SBF measurement to NGC 4258 could allow us to bypass the systematic uncertainty in the Cepheid calibration, we felt it was worth an attempt.

Unfortunately, the presence of clumpy dust and recent star formation prevented us from achieving an accurate calibration using this galaxy, even when optical color images were used to identify dusty regions. The maser-calibrated fluctuation magnitude is significantly brighter (by 0.3 mag) than the calibration determined above for the elliptical and S0 galaxies in the Fornax and Virgo clusters with similar \ colors (see Fig. calibgz ). This result was not a surprise; patchy dust adds to the fluctuation signal, as does the presence of younger populations containing bright AGB stars. Unfortunately, the geometrical distance to NGC 4258 does not provide a useful direct calibration of the SBF technique for elliptical galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes M106 as a massive spiral with companions, enhanced outer-disk star formation, a truncation at 22 kpc, and a smooth extended disk shaped gently by satellites.

quotes.quote: M106 (NGC 4258) is the brightest member of the Canes Venatici II Group fouque92 , and can be considered a Milky Way analogue given its similar luminosity, Hubble Type, and local environment e.g. kim11 . While the central regions of M106 have been studied extensively specifically, to determine the origin of a pair of offset ``anomalous'' spiral arms; courtes61, vanderkruit72, pietsch94 , previous studies on the outer disk are relatively scarce. M106 is the most massive galaxy of the three examined in this paper, and is also the only one of the three with clearly visible satellites. The galaxy NGC 4248, located to the northwest of M106 (labeled in Figure fig:m106comp ), has long been suspected to be a satellite vanalbada77 , although previous studies found no clear evidence of interaction e.g. vanderkruit79 .

Despite elevated levels of star formation, M106 still shows a clear star formation truncation radius associated with the end of its outer spiral arms at 600 \ (22kpc). Its stellar disk extends roughly twice this distance beyond this truncation radius, with signs of interaction with its two brightest companion galaxies. We trace M106's stellar disk to 40 kpc, or 6.5 scale lengths. Although M106 possesses a more robust satellite system than M64 or M94, its smooth outer disk and fairly weak tidal structure argues that these satellites are not dramatically reshaping the disk --- instead, they may have helped drive the outward migration of stars in M106's disk without completely disrupting the disk outskirts.

sources.summary: The paper reports RadioAstron detections of the NGC 4258 water megamaser, associating it with a central supermassive-black-hole accretion disk and record angular resolution.

quotes.quote: It was detected in a number of star-forming regions in the Galaxy and megamaser galaxies NGC 4258 and NGC 3079. RadioAstron observations provided the absolute record of the angular resolution in astronomy. Fringes from the NGC 4258 megamaser were detected on baseline exceeding 25 Earth Diameters.

As a result, the signals from extragalactic masers NGC3079 and NGC 4258 were detected along with star-forming regions. The maser of NGC 4258 is associated with the accretion disk around super-massive black hole at the center of this galaxy. Projected baselines in these observing sessions were up to 2.0 ED (for NGC3079) and exceeding 25 ED (for NGC 4258) corresponding to angular resolutions of 115 as and 8 as, respectively. The latter resolution represents a record of angular resolution in astronomy, the previous record of 11 as was reported by us in ( ).
Record resolution better than 9 for any astronomical observation is obtained for NGC 4258 extragalactic water maser. Scintillation does not prevent fringe detection.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4258 as a nearby low-luminosity Seyfert II with water masers, a megamaser disk, variable X-ray absorption, and no strong RIAF-like X-ray signature.

quotes.quote: The presence of water masers orbiting around the active galactic nucleus (AGN) in NGC 4258, one of the most studied extragalactic objects, has been crucial in developing a detailed picture of its nuclear environment. Despite this, its accretion rate and bolometric luminosity are still matter of debate, as there are indications that NGC 4258 may host a genuine radiatively inefficient accretion flow (RIAF). In this respect, we present a detailed broadband X-ray spectrum of NGC 4258, with the goal of precisely measuring the coronal luminosity and accretion flow properties of the AGN, and track any possible variation across two decades of observations.

Our results suggest that NGC 4258 is a genuinely low-luminosity Seyfert II, with no strong indications in its X-ray emission for a hot, RIAF-like accretion flow. galaxies: active The nearby spiral galaxy NGC 4258 hosts one of the closest active galactic nuclei (AGN), at a distance of 7.58 0.11 Mpc reid19 . However, its historical importance goes beyond its mere distance. Since the discovery of nuclear megamaser emission at 22 GHz from water vapour molecules claussen84 , NGC 4258 soon became the cleanest evidence for the existence of extragalactic supermassive black holes (SMBHs). The mapping and temporal monitoring of the masers through Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) revealed a sub-pc molecular, dusty disk in Keplerian rotation around a central mass M = 4 10^7 M_ nakai93, miyoshi95 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as a spiral Seyfert/LINER with obscured X-rays, anomalous arms, megamaser disk, radio jet, and a borderline Compton-thin/thick torus fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258* NGC 4258* is a SABbc spiral galaxy, spectroscopically classified as a 1.9 Seyfert 1997Ho and as LINER 2014balmaverde . This source has a highly obscured central X-ray source and is well known for its anomalous arms, discovered on the basis of H imaging 2001wilson and for its nuclear H _ 2 O megamaser, which traces a dense, edge-on disk on sub-parcsec scales 1994watson, 1995greenhill . The nucleus of NGC 4258* also contains a relativistic radio jet 2013doi .

2009reynolds combined Suzaku , XMM-Newton and Swift , and detected robust flux variability of the 6.4 keV iron line and suggested a model in which the line originates from the surface of a warped accretion disk. Also, during their Suzaku observation, they detected high amplitude intraday variability, with fluctuations on timescales as short as 5 ksec.
Using PEXMON we found R _ f =0.78 _ 0.20 ^ 3.744 . Using borus02 we found (N_ H,refl ) =23.29 _ 22.77 ^ 24.39 , so borderline Compton-thin/thick torus. The torus covers less than 81 of the sky.

sources.summary: The paper applies the kerrflow hot-flow model to NGC 4258 X-ray observations, discussing its low Eddington ratio, absorption complexity, flare behavior, and model constraints.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 ( z=0.00149 ), radiating at L/L_ Edd 10^ 4 2009ApJ...691.1159R , is an excellent source for testing the accretion models, owing to the precise measurement of its supermassive black hole mass and distance 1995Natur.373..127M,2013ApJ...775...13H,2019ApJ...886L..27R and was among objects whose observed properties supported the development of the advection-dominated solution 1996ApJ...462..142L .

NGC 4258 The nuclear emission of NGC 4258 is strongly absorbed and the observed radiation is fully dominated by the diffuse emission at 1 keV. Therefore, in all detectors, we considered only the data above 0.9 keV. Guided by previous studies 2009ApJ...691.1159R,2020ApJ...897...66L , in our description of the X-ray spectrum of NGC 4258 we included the diffuse emission described by two apec components (which gives a significantly better description, with ^2 200 , than a version with a single apec component); the optically thin reprocessor described by borus02 ; the internal absorber described by zTbabs ; the intrinsic emission described by kerrflow and the additional component described by the unabsorbed kerrflow (with parameters linked to the main component representing the intrinsic emission), attenuated by a factor K 0.1 .

NGC 4258 displayed a significant flare during the S1 observation and 2009ApJ...691.1159R and 2009PASJ...61..309Y noted some hints for spectral variability associated with this flare. We extracted the spectra of the high and low flux states following the 2009PASJ...61..309Y criterion, i.e. \ we divided the S1 observation into subsets with the 2--10 keV count rates higher and lower than 0.5 counts s ^ 1 , respectively. The resulting spectra can be described, with no apparent residuals, using kerrflow with all parameters fixed at the values found for S1 (as given in Table tab:fit_4258 for the model with unlinked ), except for which is fitted at 0.13 and 0.16 for the higher and lower flux spectrum, respectively.

sources.summary: The review describes KNIFE observations of NGC 4258 masers and notes later VLBA evidence for a Keplerian molecular disk around a 3.6e7 solar-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: What is the origin of the high-velocity mega masers of NGC 4258? In order to identify the origin, observations with a high spatial resolution are required. Prof. Morimoto suggested that we should investigate with KNIFE as soon as possible. The KNIFE observations were conducted in early June 1993, less than a month after the discovery.

In 1995, VLBA observations revealed that the water mega maser in NGC 4258 was from a molecular gas disk with Keplerian motion around a supermassive black hole. From the maser velocity and structure, the mass of the black hole is estimated to be 3.6 10^7 M_ . The Figure Fig-5 shows the mega maser distribution obtained from reanalyzing the KNIFE data and that from the result of VLBA. As you can see from the Figure, it is possible to capture the structure of the water mega maser in NGC 4258 even with the single 200 km baseline of KNIFE.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4258 as a water-maser distance anchor with hundreds of Cepheids, testing background effects by comparing inner and outer Cepheid intercepts.

quotes.quote: The third anchor is the water-maser host NGC 4258, with a 1.5 geometric distance Reid:2019 . By collecting Cepheids in six fields, the SH0ES Team has collected 669 Cepheids in NGC 4258 Yuan:2022 . The Cepheids in NGC 4258 also provide a strong test of the effect of the blotchy background on Cepheid photometry, because we can compare the Cepheid intercepts measured in the inner region (where crowding is high) versus in the outer region, where it is low.

As shown in Figure fg:ngc4258 , the agreement is good, at the 0.05 mag level. Here we clarify some terminology. Freedman:2023 , at this conference, referred to crowding as a bias in photometry. To be clear, this term (as shown in that contribution) is the measured difference in the background level between the true sky and the mean level of the (non-Cepheid) stars (measured with artificial stars). Thus, the true (Cepheid) background is both components added together.

sources.summary: The paper presents ASCA X-ray spectroscopy of NGC 4258, finding soft thermal emission, an absorbed hard AGN power law, an extra continuum, and a narrow iron K line without a broad component.

quotes.quote: We present a deep X-ray observation of the low-luminosity active galactic nucleus in NGC 4258 (M 106) using the Advanced Satellite for Cosmology and Astrophysics ( ASCA ). Confirming previous results, we find that the X-ray spectrum of this source possesses several components. The soft X-ray spectrum ( <2 ) is dominated by thermal emission from optically-thin plasma with kT 0.5 . The hard X-ray emission is clearly due to a power-law component with photon index 1.8 absorbed by a column density of N_ H 8 10^ 22 . The power-law is readily identified with primary X-ray emission from the AGN central engine.

Underlying both of these spectral components is an additional continuum which is possibly due to thermal emission of a very hot gaseous component in the anomalous arms and/or the integrated hard emission of X-ray binaries in the host galaxy. We also clearly detect a narrow iron K emission line at 6.4 . No broad component is detected.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as a barred spiral Seyfert/LLAGN with anomalous arms, nuclear jets, and a warped sub-parsec water-maser accretion disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 (M106) is a prominent barred spiral galaxy (SABbc) located at a distance of 7.2 Mpc (Herrnstein \ 1998b). It is one of 12 galaxies included in Seyfert's initial catalog of active galaxies (Seyfert 1943) and it has been studied extensively over a broad range of frequencies and resolutions. On large scales, NGC 4258 is dominated by a pair of anomalous spiral arms positioned between the stellar arms and symmetric with respect to the nucleus.
