This discontinuity can be located using edge detection methods such as a Sobel filter and references therein Lee_1993ApJ...417..553L, Sakai_1996ApJ...461..713S, Hatt_2017ApJ...845..146H or with a multi-parameter model fit to the luminosity function using least-square minimization Wu_2014AJ....148....7W or maximum likelihood estimation and references therein Mendez_2002AJ....124..213M, Makarov_2006AJ....132.2729M, Mcquinn_2016ApJ...826...21M, Kim_2020ApJ...905..104K, Li_2022arXiv220211110L . For the edge detection approach, a filter, most often of the Sobel type, is evaluated across the luminosity function to measure its weighted first derivative and identify the TRGB discontinuity peak in the derivative function; Lee_1993ApJ...417..553L . However, this approach can result in several local maxima in the first derivative that are comparable in height or which are outside an `expected' range of tip values based on prior inferences. These effects can lead to a somewhat subjective differentiation between the true tip and spurious features. Previous measurements of the tip in NGC 4258 such as Madore_2009ApJ...690..389M involved supervision to decide which tip to accept and subjective definition of what area constitutes ``the halo'' Jang_2021ApJ...906..125J . To address these issues, developed an unsupervised, Sobel filter-based tip measurement algorithm as part of the Comparative Analysis of tips (CATs) program and optimized its parameters to minimize the dispersion among multiple fields in Galaxy Halos, Outer disks, Star clusters, Thick disks, and Substructure (GHOSTS) survey Radburn-Smith_2011ApJS..195...18R galaxies. The CATs algorithm from differs from past tip measurement algorithms by using quantifiable, objective procedures that remove some of the subjective steps used in other work such as choice of color band slopes and widths. It uses an entirely unsupervised Sobel filter-based approach and accepts multiple tip measurements per population; this obviates the need to select a specific peak in the Sobel response, and accounts for variations in stellar properties that can produce tips of slightly different luminosities. The detections undergo a series of quality cuts that are applied in an unsupervised and consistent manner. found that the measured tip magnitude becomes brighter with increasing contrast ratio, where the contrast ratio is defined as the quotient between the number of stars 0.5 mag fainter and brighter than the measured tip and may be related to properties of different stellar populations, luminosity function characteristics, and measurement parameter choices that cause variations in the measured tip. We note that the exact origin of this relationship is inconsequential to the validity of its application as a calibrating tool in general (though the slope may change with smoothing and noise, see Sections sec:ArtpopTCR and sec:Discussion ). The tip-contrast ratio relationship can be used to standardize the tip so that distances to galaxies are calibrated in a more similar manner. When measuring the distance to a galaxy, the measured apparent magnitude tip should be tied to an absolute magnitude tip or zero-point that is calculated using a population of similar stellar properties, luminosity function characteristics, and measurement parameter choices, otherwise the zero-point may not accurately reflect the true absolute magnitude of the tip of a population with those stellar properties and characteristics measured with a different setup. It will be important to investigate whether this relationship exists in other galaxies, especially those used to anchor the extragalactic distance ladder to measure H_0 . NGC 4258 acts as an anchor galaxy for the TRGB-based distance ladder due to the availability of a geometric, maser-based distance Pesce_2020ApJ...891L...1P, Reid_2019ApJ...886L..27R and is an ideal testing ground for this hypothesis as multiple fields have been observed in the past (see Section sec:Data_Selection ). In this study, we use the CATs algorithm to calibrate the tip-contrast ratio relationship in NGC 4258.

sources.summary: The paper presents M106 as a nearby disk galaxy with radio/X-ray bipolar bubbles, hot plasma and a corona, arguing the structure was powered by recent low-luminosity AGN jets or winds.

quotes.quote: In this study, we shed light on this complex phenomenon by presenting evidence for a large-scale bipolar radio/X-ray-emitting bubble-like structure emanating from the central region of the nearby disk galaxy M106 (NGC 4258).
Similar to the eROSITA/Fermi bubbles in our own Galaxy, the M106 bubbles enclose diffuse hot plasma and are partially bounded by prominent radio/X-ray-emitting edges.

The analysis of the X-ray data gives an estimate of the thermal energy of the bubbles as 8 10^ 56 erg. This energy can be supplied by the jets and perhaps by the wind from the accretion flow of the galaxy's low-luminosity AGN, which most likely has been much more powerful in the recent past, with an average mechanical energy release rate of 4 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 over the last 8 10^6 yr -- the estimated age of the structure. We also show evidence for diffuse X-ray emission on larger scales, indicating the presence of a hot galactic corona.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 4258 as a compelling VLBI maser case for a central dark mass, with a warped Keplerian H2O disk orbiting an unresolved 40-million-solar-mass object.

quotes.quote: In external galaxies a compelling case that such a dark mass concentration cannot just be a dense nuclear cluster of white dwarfs, neutron stars and perhaps stellar BHs, already emerged in the mid-1990s from spectacular VLBI observations of the nucleus of NGC 4258. This is a mildly active galaxy at a distance of 7 Mpc miyoshi1995, moran2008 . The VLBI observations show that the galaxy nucleus contains a thin, slightly warped disk of H 2 O masers (viewed almost edge on), in beautiful Keplerian rotation around an unresolved mass of 40 million solar masses, as inferred from the maser motions. The maser motions exceed 1000 km/s at the innermost edge of the disk of about 0.1pc. The inferred density of this mass exceeds a few 10^ 9 solar masses pc ^ 3 and thus cannot be a long-lived cluster of `dark' astrophysical objects of the type mentioned above maoz1995 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as having soft X-rays along anomalous arms, little hard extended emission, and two core point sources with different soft-X-ray brightness.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 Chandra images of NGC 4258 are shown in Figure 8. The soft X-ray emission is extended and trails along the ``anomalous arms'' of the galaxy. The large-scale emission and its relation to the radio jets is discussed in detail by Wilson, Yang Cecil (2001). There is little emission extended at harder energies. There are two point sources in the core, with the one to the SW being brighter in soft X-rays (Fig. \ 8 a ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as a nearby weak-AGN galaxy whose galactic X-ray sources required an added bremsstrahlung component in the BeppoSAX spectral models.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258: NGC 4258 is a nearby galaxy (estimated distance 7 Mpc) hosting a weak AGN. In this object the integrated X-ray emission of galactic sources is not negligible with respect to the AGN contribution. Therefore, in models B and C we added an extra bremmshtralung component to take into account for the galactic contribution. The best fit temperature (kT=7 keV) and normalization are in agreement with those estimated in 5 different measures performed with ASCA (Reynolds et al. 2000). Our results is in agreement with the more detailed analysis of the same data performed by Fiore et al. (2001).

sources.summary: NGC 4258 is used as a geometric benchmark: its maser disk gives 7.2 Mpc, while Cepheid and PNLF distances overestimate it similarly but preserve its relative distance to the LMC.

quotes.quote: The first is NGC 4258, which has a resolved disk of cold gas orbiting its central black hole. The proper motions and radial accelerations of water masers associated with this gas have been detected and measured: the result is an unambiguous geometric distance to the galaxy of 7.2 0.3 Mpc .

According to the table, the Cepheid and PNLF methods both overestimate the distance to NGC 4258 by 0.14 mag, by 1.3 and 1.0 , respectively. In the absence of some systematic error affecting both methods, the probability of this happening is 5 . On the other hand, there is no disagreement concerning NGC 4258's distance relative to that of the LMC: the Cepheids, PNLF, and geometric techniques all agree to within 2 Such a small error is probably fortuitous, but it does suggest the presence of a systematic error in the entire extragalactic distance scale.
There will always be some objects, such as NGC 4258, for which an additional, high-precision distance measurement is useful.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4258 as a key external distance anchor, comparing its maser-based geometric distance with Cepheid measurements that imply a smaller LMC distance modulus.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 is the only other external galaxy besides the LMC with a reliable geometrical distance measurement. The orbital properties of masers around the central black hole in NGC 4258 can be determined to give an absolute geometrical distance of 7.2 0.5 Mpc (Herrnstein et al. 1999). Cepheids have been discovered in NGC 4258, and they provide an independent distance measurement that is approximately 1 Mpc larger (Maoz et al. 1999; Newman et al. 2001); the KP Cepheid calibration (Freedman et al. 2001) is discrepant at the 1- level if the distance to the LMC is 18.50 mag. The two measurements would agree if =18.31 mag. If the maser and Cepheid distances are both reliable, they could be used to rule out a distance of 18.50 to the LMC.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as a nearby barred galaxy with a weak AGN, HII regions, and a water-maser nuclear disk implying a supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: The nearby and almost edge-on barred (SAB(s)bc) galaxy NGC 4258 (Messier 106) hosts a weak AGN. It also forms a pair with NGC 4248 in the Ursa Major Cluster. The optical image (see electronic Fig. ngc4258 ) shows an apparent chain of HII regions at the extremities of its arms. This galaxy is well known for its nuclear thin disk in Keplerian rotation, which is traced by emission from water masers (Miyoshi et al. 1995) and implies a supermassive central black hole. This case is reminiscent of the first object discussed above, both in flux density level and galactocentric distance.

sources.summary: The paper observes two HST fields in M106, describes it as a bright Coma-Sculptor spiral, and uses Cepheids to derive its distance calibration.

quotes.quote: We used the Hubble Space Telescope (GO program 9810) to observe two fields located at widely different galactocentric radii within the disk of \ (M106). This spiral galaxy is one of the brightest members of the Coma-Sculptor Cloud tully87 , and has been classified as SAB(s)bc II-III RC3 and Sb(s) II RSA . We carried out the observations using the Advanced Camera for Surveys / Wide Field Camera ford03 , which consists of 2 back-illuminated SITe 2048 4096 pixel CCDs. The average plate scale of the focal plane is 0 05 pix ^ 1 , making each image 202 on the side. At the nominal \ distance of 7.2 Mpc, this translates to a physical size of 1.7 pc pix ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The article identifies NGC 4258 (M 106) as the strongest massive black-hole candidate, with water maser gas motions revealing a Keplerian disc around a compact object.

quotes.quote: The spiral galaxy NGC 4258 (M 106) is by far the best massive black hole candidate. Gas motions near the centre has been precisely mapped with the 1.3 cm maser emission line of H_ 2 O . The velocities are measured with accuracy of 1 km/s. Their spatial distribution reveals a disc with rotational velocities following an exact Kepler's law around a massive compact object. Also the inner edge of the disc, orbiting at 1080 km/s, cannot comprise a stable stellar cluster with the inferred mass of 3.6 10^ 7 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4258 Cepheids, showing its distance and metallicity results depend on the adopted disk abundance gradient and favor a shallow nebular oxygen gradient.

quotes.quote: Concerning the NGC 4258 Cepheids, the results strongly depend on the adopted metallicity gradient across the galactic disc. The most recent nebular oxygen abundances support a shallower gradient and provide a metallicity dependence that agrees well with current pulsation predictions.

By using this relation for the Cepheids in the LMC and in the outer field of NGC 4258 by neglecting the metallicity correction , we derive _ 0,VI (LMC)=18.45 0.09 mag and _ 0,VI (NGC4258)=29.35 0.12 mag, which are both only slightly larger than _0 (LMC)=18.41 0.09 mag determined by Guinan, Ribas Fitzpatrick (2004) from eclipsing binaries (EB) and _0 (NGC4258)=29.29 0.15 mag based on the maser geometric distance measured by Herrnstein et al. (1999).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC4258 as a strongly misaligned warped-disc case where previous analytical fits become inaccurate, motivating numerical solutions for its maser-observed accretion disc.

quotes.quote: Martin2007 generalized Scheuer1996 's analytical solution to the situation that the s varies as power law, and then, hereafter Martin2008 Martin2008 used this solution to fit the maser observation of NGC4258's disc. We carried on a numerical calculation for a warped disc with power-law varying , and compared the results with Martin2007 's analytical solution. The importance of this work lies in such a fact: Martin2007 's analytical solution (and Scheuer1996 's, as well) are based on first order approximation, under the assumption of a small inclination angle _ out 1 , while the real accretion discs can be strongly misaligned _ out 1 , e.g., the fitting of NGC4258 shows a strong misaligning. A numerical calculation is needed to tell exactly how the error grows. Our calculation shows a prominent deviation between analytical solution and exact solution when the inclination angle are large, suggesting that the analytical solutions not appropriate for study of NGC4258 or other strongly misaligned discs.
As for the situation in NGC4258, M08's fitting suggested a large inclination angle, so that numerical solution is needed for more accurate fitting.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4258 as an AGN disk-jet test case: maser emission shows clockwise disk rotation, jet Faraday rotation is negative, and the PR battery model is consistent.

quotes.quote: For two objects in which the sense of rotation of the underlying accretion disk is known, our model is in agreement with the observed Faraday-rotation signs if these jets are viewed at angles of less than 1/ . HST direct imaging of the M87 nucleus (Tsevtanov et al. 1999) and maser emission in the inner disk of NGC 4258 (Hernstein et al. 2007) reveal that the accretion disks in these systems rotate CW on the sky. FR measurements along the jet spine in M87 (Zavala Taylor 2002) and in NGC 4258 (Krause Lohr 2004) yield large negative FR values ( B_ LOS directed away from the observer). Thus, in the PR battery model, an observer viewing the jet at an angle of less than 1/ would be located in the S hemisphere (Fig. 1b), and these two accretion disks should indeed be rotating CW on the sky.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4258 as the jet source with detected azimuthal motion, describing a braided kiloparsec jet modeled as a triple helix aligned with a nuclear VLBA radio jet.

quotes.quote: In fact, there is one jet source where azimuthal jet motion has been detected. Imaging interferometry of emission lines have detected a braided structure in the jet system of NGC 4258, which could be modeled by a triple helix of jet motion on 5-10 kpc scale ceci92 . This kpc jet is aligned with a nuclear VLBA radio jet herr97 , thereby suggesting a physical connection. However, even if the helix model would explain the physical conditions in the kpc jet, there seems to be no plausible explanation yet about the intrinsic process causing such a complex outflow structure.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4258 as a barred-spiral Seyfert with a warped accretion disk, radio jet, maser emission, and pseudobulge evidence from infrared profiling.

quotes.quote: NGC4258 (M106) A barred-spiral Seyfert galaxy that has been studied extensively over a broad band of wavelengths. The nucleus contains an edge-on warped accretion disk with radio jet ( tex:HM97 ) and strong maser emission ( tex:CH84 ). Both GD07 and H09 used a bulge/disk model and found n_ bulge =2.04 , n_ bulge =2.6 respectively. tex:FD10 , found that n_ bulge =2.8 in mid-infrared and presented evidence of pseudobulge characteristics.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4258 as a modified-gravity distance test, noting its maser, Cepheid, and TRGB distances agree for a Milky-Way-sized galaxy with a supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 is a Milky Way sized galaxy at a distance of 7 Mpc. The water masers in this galaxy provide a rotation velocity of the accretion disk in excess of 1,000 km/s at distances on the order of 0.1-0.3 pc from the inferred super-massive black hole of mass 4 10^7 M_ . The two distance estimates obtained from the maser data are in excellent agreement -- see Herrnstein et al (2005) and Humphreys et al (2008) for recent studies.
We note that the distances agree within estimated errors that are at the few percent level for the maser distances and (allowing for systematics) at the 5-10 level for cepheid and TRGB distances.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as the prototypical water-maser disk galaxy, a revised distance anchor for Cepheid calibration, and a nearby system outside the useful Hubble-flow sample.

quotes.quote: Based on three anchors, (1) the parallax determinations of Galactic Cepheids, (2) Cepheides in the LMC with distances deduced from eclipsing binaries, (3) the distance to NGC 4258 (see Sect. 3) and with new HST (Hubble Space Telescope) data from galaxies with Cepheids and Type Ia supernovae, Riess et al. (2011) derive H_0 = 73.8 2.4 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . This estimate also makes use of Cepheids measured in the near infrared, which helps to reduce both systematic and random errors with respect to optical observations. Note that the distance to NGC 4258 has been slightly revised in the meantime (E.M.L. Humphreys, priv. comm.).

Following the experience gained by studying the prototypical source, NGC 4258 (Miyoshi et al. 1995; Herrnstein et al. 1999), it includes (1) a GBT (Green Bank Telescope) survey to identify suitable circumnuclear 22 GHz H _2 O maser disks (see Fig. 3 for a spectrum), (2) direct imaging of these sub-pc disks, using the VLBA (Very Long Baseline Array), the GBT , and, for northern sources, also the Effelsberg telescope, (3) GBT monitoring to measure accelerations of the spectral components, and (4) model calculations to simulate the maser disk dynamics. So far published articles include Reid et al. (2009), Braatz et al. (2010), Greene et al. (2010), and Kuo et al. (2011).

While NGC 4258 is with V 500 km s ^ 1 not yet in the Hubble flow and therefore not useful for a direct H_0 estimate (its maser lines are nevertheless essential to calibrate the distance scale defined by Cepheids), NGC 6323 has the potential to probe the distance scale with its recesseional velocity of almost 7800 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC4258, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC4258 (M106, Fig. fig:NGC4258 ) is, after M32, the most nearby galaxy in our sample and has one of the largest apparent sizes. The disk profile is divided into an inner disk that dominates within 200 , i.e. the same regions where the spiral arms are present, and an outer disk with apparently larger scale radius, visible not in Figure fig:NGC4258 due to the smaller size of the cutout, which covers mostly the main (inner) part of the disk out to R 800 and showing some warping as well as modest asymmetry.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4258 as the archetypal megamaser galaxy, whose radio astrometry maps an AGN disk, provides accurate geometric distances, and recalibrates Cepheid-based Hubble-constant estimates.

quotes.quote: Observations of the archetypal megamaser galaxy, NGC 4258, demonstrated the power of radio astrometric observations for better understanding of AGN accretion disks ( Herrnstein:05 ) and yielded an accurate distance of 7.2 0.5 Mpc to the galaxy ( Herrnstein:99 ). Since NGC 4258 is nearby and its (unknown) peculiar motion is likely to be a large fraction of its cosmological recessional velocity ( 500 ), one cannot directly measure \ by dividing velocity by distance. However, since Cepheid variables have been observed in NGC 4258, one can use the radio distance to recalibrate the zero-point of the Cepheid period-luminosity relation ( Macri:06 ) and then revise estimates of \ ( Riess:12a,Riess:12b ). Recently, Humphreys:13 analyzed a decade of observations of NGC 4258 and, via more detailed modeling of disk kinematics, obtained an extremely accurate distance of 7.60 0.23 Mpc, which constrains =72.0 3.0 \ Mpc ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as a galaxy with a central water-maser disk whose precisely mapped rotation gives strong dynamical evidence for a compact massive black hole.

quotes.quote: The painstaking measurement of stellar motions near the centers of galaxies has been supplemented by an unexpected gift from the heavens: the otherwise unremarkable galaxy NGC 4258 contains at its center a thin, nearly flat, rotating disk of gas, about a tenth of a parsec in radius. The gas includes water vapor, and the temperature and density in the disk are right for the production of maser (microwave laser) emission in the water, stimulated by a weak active galactic nucleus at the center of the disk. The maser emission consists of tiny, intensely bright sources of radiation in the spectral lines of water, and by measuring the Doppler shift of these sources and their motion across the sky using an intercontinental array of radio telescopes we can map out the rotation of the disk with exquisite precision. The disk is found to rotate around the active nucleus; from the disk kinematics we can deduce that the nucleus is much smaller than the inner radius of the disk,

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 4258 with a partial-covering absorber plus hot gas component, contrasting that with earlier simple absorbed power-law models.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 Only a single summed spectrum of NGC 4258 could be produced from the available Swift data. We find that a partial covering absorber and a hot gas component is required to obtain a good-fit ( ^2_R = 1.03 ), significantly better than a fully-covering absorber ( ^2_R = 1.78 ). Previous studies of NGC 4258 have, however, found the spectrum to be best modelled by a simple absorbed power law and a hot gas component Yamada2009, Terashima2002 . The value we find for the photon index ( = 1.66 ) is consistent with that of Terashima2002 and Yamada2009 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4258 as a geometric distance anchor whose megamaser distance and Cepheid color distribution strongly influence generalized Cepheid calibrations for Hubble-constant inference.

quotes.quote: Finally, line-of-sight and velocity measurements humphreys_toward_2013 of a large megamaser system in NGC 4258 (hosting 150 Cepheid candidates) allow for a 2.6 determination of the distance to the host, after accounting for system inclination and other nuisance effects.

Of particular importance is the value of R_H in the direction of NGC 4258 , which plays an outsized role due to the fact the Cepheid sample there most accurately matches the period-color distribution of Cepheids in supernova host galaxies. With the limited data available, R_H^ NGC 4258 is compatible with the assumed Milky Way value, though more information on dust extinction in this galaxy may shed additional light. It is worth noting, however, that to completely explain the tension between Planck derived and Cepheid-derived Hubble measurements, we would need R_H 0.6 along the line-of-sight to NGC 4258 : an extremely unlikely excursion, and one in no way favored by the local distance data.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4258/M106 as a nearby LINER/Seyfert with anomalous arms, weak AGN jets, first isotopic-line detections, and measured dense-gas optical depths.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 (M106) is a Sab-type LINER/Seyfert galaxy at the distance of 6.8 Mpc 2015yCat..51500043T . Its anomalous spiral arms emerge from the galaxy nucleus and appear to intersect the regular spiral arms of galaxy 2014ApJ...788L..33O . reported that NGC4258 is a weak AGN and a pair of radio jets may be influencing the molecular emission.

Two isotopic lines, H ^ 13 CN 1-0 and H ^ 13 CO ^ + 1-0, were detected for the first time in NGC 4258, while these two lines, as isotopic lines of dense gas tracers, were not detected in NGC 3079, which can only give the upper limits (see Table Table:NGC3079 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports that 4258 has both modeled column densities in the same regime, requires fixed parameters, and agrees well with previous results.

quotes.quote: 4258 According to tab-fit , both \ and \ are \ for this object. A_S needs to be fixed to 1 for \ to be constrained. As before, the lower \ limit tends to zero, so we fix it to 100 . The \ reported by laha2020 for the same obsID is in very good agreement with our \ values.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4258 as a water-megamaser galaxy with precise geometric distance, unsuitable for direct H0 but important as a distance anchor.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 NGC 4258 is a relatively nearby water megamaser host galaxy which has a geometric distance of D = 7.576 0.082 (stat.) 0.076 (sys.) Mpc with 1.4 precision .

Given that the galaxy is quite close, NGC 4258's peculiar motion is likely to contribute significantly to its recessional velocity. Thus, while it is unsuitable for direct H _0 measurements, it has served as an important anchor galaxy for Cepheids, TRGB, and Miras.
Finally, the relative distance between NGC 4258 and the LMC was calculated after applying a Mira-specific color correction to ground-based H band P-L relation.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4258 as a nearby megamaser-BH galaxy with two inner-CGM QSO sightlines, but reports only C IV upper limits for both sightlines.

quotes.quote: Finally, the nearby galaxy NGC 4258 which has a highly accurate BH-mass measurement from megamaser kinematics miyoshi_1995 , is serendipitously intersected by two inner-CGM QSO sightlines at 70 and 130 kpc. We include both QSO targets in our final sample because it offers a rare opportunity to study subtle variations (e.g. column density, kinematics, etc.) within a single halo.

We note that the two sightlines that intersect the halo of NGC 4258, colored in grey, are upper limits; even though the upper-limit observations are consistent with each other, studying the variations within this single halo is not possible with these two sightlines.
for NGC 4258 (QSO: SDSSJ1220 and SDSSJ1222) and NGC 4564 (QSO: SDSSJ1235) we do not detect any C IV absorption, and report only upper limits on C IV column density.

sources.summary: The lecture uses NGC4258 as a geometric distance cross-check, describing its Seyfert nucleus, water-maser clouds orbiting a central black hole, VLBA orbital constraints, and a 7.2 Mpc maser distance.

quotes.quote: NGC4258 (M106) is a Seyfert 2 galaxy with H _2 O maser emission from clouds orbiting around a black hole of mass 4 10^7M_ located at the centre. Precise VLBA measurements of Doppler velocities show that the motion of the clouds is very close to Keplerian and is perturbed very little (Miyoshi et al. 1995). A complete determination is made for the orbital parameters including centripetal acceleration and a bulk proper motion of the emission system. This yields a geometric distance to NGC4258 to be 7.2 0.3 Mpc (Herrnstein et al. 1999).

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4258 with its black-hole growth estimate, using a roughly constant velocity dispersion and finding the predicted mass compatible with the observed central black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: However, in galaxies M 31 and NGC 4258 it seems to be almost constant and equals approximately 200 km/s . Taking the value of f_0 the same as in our Galaxy ( f_0 ) and the time of growth t=3 10^ 17 s = 10^ 10 yr. we obtain the value M_ bh = 1.8 10^7 M_ . The observed black hole masses are (2.0 8.5) 10^7 M_ for M 31 and 3.8 10^7 M_ for NGC 4258 . The comparison shows that dark matter may comprise significant fraction of black hole masses in these galaxies.

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 4258 as a geometric-distance benchmark, noting its maser disk distance and comparing Cepheid and PNLF distance offsets relative to that scale.

quotes.quote: The first is NGC 4258, which has a resolved disk of cold gas orbiting its central black hole. The proper motions and radial accelerations of water masers associated with this gas yield an unambiguous geometric distance of 7.2 0.3 Mpc .

As the table demonstrates, the Cepheid and PNLF methods both overestimate the distance to NGC 4258 by 0.14 mag, by 1.3 and 1.1 , respectively. In the absence of some systematic error affecting both methods, the probability of this happening is 5 . On the other hand, there is no disagreement concerning NGC 4258's distance relative to that of the LMC: the Cepheids, PNLF, and geometric techniques all agree to within 2 Such a small error is probably fortuitous, but it does suggest the presence of a systematic error that affects the entire extragalactic distance ladder.

sources.summary: The paper reports that M106 has a flattened outer UV and 60 micron profile, tying the feature to a visible ring and uncertain outer flux points.

quotes.quote: The profile of M106 shows a flattening in the outer part of the UV and 60 m flux. The feature must be real since we are well above the sky in the UV and it is located in the area of a ring, clearly visible in the image. At 60 m, the flux at the same radius is barely more than one _ sky , and is dominated by a few large peaks, not present in the 100 m image. The few outer points of this profile are quite uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4258 as an unusual source with anomalous radio arms and jet-linked inner emission, while still showing good CO-radio correlation where both are present.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 . The radio emission in this unusual source is associated with two, large-scale ``anomalous arms'' not seen in the optical but detected first in H (Courtes Cruvellier 1961). In the inner galaxy, the radio emission is clearly associated with jets, presumably related to the AGN activity associated with the supermassive black hole; however, at larger distances, the nature of the radio emission is not so clear (e.g. Krause L o hr 2004 and references therein). A detailed discussion of this unusual galaxy is beyond the scope of this paper, and a proper discussion of the RC-FIR-CO correlations in this source would warrant a separate study. However, we include NGC 4258 here and emphasize that, even in this highly unusual source, the CO-RC correlation is very good over those regions where both CO and RC are present.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4258 as a spiral sample galaxy whose outer-field photometry incompletely reaches the red giant branch, making its metal-rich metallicity distribution incomplete.

quotes.quote: For NGC 4258, the photometry does not probe the red giant branch far, and the 50 completeness level limit falls close to the brightest end of the red giant branch sequence. We have limited ourselves to a range of I-band magnitudes where the completeness level is not severely low, i.e., higher than 50 . However, stars with metallicities higher than Fe/H 0.7 lie at fainter magnitudes than the magnitude range chosen to construct the metallicity distribution function of stars at the outskirts of NGC 4258. For this galaxy, the metal-rich end of the metallicity distribution function is suspected to be incomplete, i.e., a fraction of metal-rich stars are missing.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4258 as a Seyfert 2 in the M-sigma sample, with a megamaser black-hole mass and a warped Keplerian maser disk in a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: The galaxies that define the M_ BH - _* relationship are nearly all One Seyfert 2 galaxy, NGC 4258, whose BH mass was measured by megamaser motions (Miyoshi et al. \ 1995), was included in the sample of galaxies that defined the M_ BH - _* relationship. quiescent galaxies whose BH masses were measured by stellar or gas kinematics.

Such a structure is observed in the case of NGC 4258 through the maser spots (Greenhill et al. \ 1995; Miyoshi et al. \ 1995). In this object, the rotation law at the distance of the megamaser sources is Keplerian, but the disk is tipped downward by about 10^ o , which allows more of the disk surface to be directly exposed to the central source.
However, NGC 4258 is a low-luminosity AGN, and it is not clear that this would be the case in more luminous objects, which in particular would be more sensitive to the gravitational instability.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC4258 as the pioneering warped maser disk case, comparing alignment modeling and a possible binary-companion origin for the warp.

quotes.quote: The pioneering example is the maser disk of NGC4258.

Application of this effect to the warped disks of NGC4258 and NGC1068 shows that the alignment radius lies well inside the observed positions of the maser spots, and models can be constructed that fit the observed warps rather well caproni07, martin08 .
They concluded that the warp in the maser disk of NGC4258 might have been excited by a binary companion with a mass comparable to or higher than that of the maser disk.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4258 as a disk galaxy with a pseudobulge and finds it consistent with the classical-bulge relation and within the observed scatter.

quotes.quote: By means of the S e rsic index, we identify nine disk galaxies as hosting pseudobulges: Circinus, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368, NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594.

Figure fg:mbh illustrates that 4 out of 9 pseudobulges (NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594) are consistent with the correlation for classical bulges, while the others (CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368) are outliers, at more than 4 below the LINMIX ERR linear regression.
The other pseudobulges NGC3489, NGC4258, NGC4374, NGC4594 are within the observed scatter.

sources.summary: The paper reports weak dispersion for NGC4258, expects the linear method to recover the dominant mass component, and finds a very weak slope implying a mass ratio of about 0.05.

quotes.quote: For NGC4258 and NGC1068, the dispersion is much weaker with 0.2-0.3 . For these two objects, the linear approach should give the mass of the most massive component quite correctly (i.e. see Tab. tab:synthetic ).
For NGC4258, the slope is very weak and a mass ratio of about 0.05 is expected.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4258's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 is an inclined grand-design spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 7.6 0.8 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a weak Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , a radio jet ford_bubbles_1986 , and a famous water maser (e.g., nakai_extremely-high-velocity_1993,greenhill_detection_1995,herrnstein_vlba_1998 ), allowing for accurate determination of the mass of the SMBH. present a detailed study of the AGN in NGC 4258. The first MIR observations of the nucleus were performed by and and first subarcsecond-resolution N- and Q band images were taken with Keck/MIRLIN in 1998 .

sources.summary: The paper uses a blue supergiant in NGC 4258 to directly estimate disk stellar metallicity, comparing the result to nebular abundances and distance-scale calibration needs.

quotes.quote: We present the first direct determination of a stellar metallicity in the spiral galaxy NGC 4258 ( D=7.6 Mpc) based on the quantitative analysis of a low-resolution ( 5 ) Keck LRIS spectrum of a blue supergiant star located in its disk. A determination of stellar metallicity in this galaxy is important for the absolute calibration of the Cepheid Period-Luminosity relation as an anchor for the extragalactic distance scale and for a better characterization of its dependence as a function of abundance. We find a value 0.2 dex lower than solar metallicity at a galactocentric distance of 8.7 kpc, in agreement with recent  region studies using the weak forbidden auroral oxygen line at 4363  .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4258 as a key geometric maser-distance calibrator, noting its 7.6 Mpc distance and agreement with TRGB and Cepheid distance estimates.

quotes.quote: A notable exception is NGC 4258, whose precise `maser' distance of 7.6 Mpc (see hereafter) is precious for calibrating the other methods.

HRM2013 measured the distance of 7.6 Mpc with 3 uncertainty to the Seyfert II galaxy NGC 4258 using ten years observations of the H _2 O maser. We include a new geometric maser distance, D=7.6 Mpc or =29.40 mag, to NGC 4258 HRM2013 , which agree excellently with the TRGB estimate _ TRGB =29.42 0.04 EDD:CMD and with the HSTKP value of _ Ceph =29.44 0.07 mag.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 4258 as a Seyfert galaxy whose H2O maser disk shows Keplerian rotation requiring a compact central object, making a supermassive black hole viable.

quotes.quote: 2) Additional convincing evidence for the reality of massive black holes had came from the very high mass density required to explain the central object in the Seyfert galaxy NGC 4258 (M106). Using the Very Long Baseline Array in New Mexico, Miyoshi et al. \ (1995) showed that the H _2 O maser emission from this galaxy originates from a thin, rotating nuclear gas disc/annulus displaying a clear Keplerian rotation curve and requiring a mass of 3.6 10^7 M_ within a size of just 0.13 parsec Based on a galaxy distance D = 6.4 Mpc. (see also Haschick et al. \ 1994, Watson Wallin 1994, and Greenhill et al. \ 1995a,b). In their January 12 paper, Miyoshi et al. \ (1995) note that the short collisional timescale ( <10^8 years) for a swarm of solar mass dark stars with such density ( >4 10^9 M_ pc ^ 3 inside of the inner 4.1 milliarcseconds) implies that such a hypothetical star cluster could not survive (see also Maoz 1995, 1998); a single supermassive black hole is the only viable candidate.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as a megamaser Seyfert with a dense edge-on nuclear disk, distance measurements, and a variable relativistic radio jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 (S1.9) NGC 4258 is well-known for its nuclear H _2 O megamaser, which traces a dense, edge-on disk on sub-parcsec scales Watson94,Greenhill95,Miyoshi95 . As such, it has been the target of numerous distance measurements using several different techniques Macri06,Mager08,Humphreys13 . The nucleus of NGC 4258 also contains a variable, relativistic radio jet Doi13 , which may, on larger scales, be interacting with molecular clouds in the galaxy's disk Krause07 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4258 as a hot-flow benchmark with precise AGN parameters, reports a weak gamma-ray excess, and compares X-ray spectra with model predictions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258 The precisely determined parameters of this AGN made it an essential object for the development of the hot flow models e.g., 1996ApJ...462..142L .
At the position of NGC 4258 we found a weak ray excess above the background, with TS = 9.4.

However, the corresponding X-ray spectrum (bottom dashed line in Fig. \ fig:0 ) seems to rule out this (large- ) case if the X-ray radiation observed from NGC 4258 is produced by thermal Comptonization.

sources.summary: Cosmicflows-3 uses NGC 4258 as a distance-calibration anchor, noting that Cepheid, TRGB, and geometric maser methods agree on a 7.57 0.10 Mpc distance.

quotes.quote: Both Cepheid and TRGB methods give distances to NGC 4258 that agree with the maser distance 2013ApJ...775...13H .

The input distances are derived in 25 cases from the Cepheid period-luminosity relation assuming a fiducial distance modulus for the Large Magellanic Cloud of 18.48 2012ApJ...758...24F, 2014AJ....147..122D , in 19 cases from the TRGB method assuming the calibration by 2007ApJ...661..815R , and in the special case of NGC 4258 from the geometric model of the maser emission 2013ApJ...775...13H .
The Cepheid, TRGB, and maser methodologies agree on the distance to NGC 4258 of 7.57 0.10 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4258 as the lone low-mass classical bulge among six classical-bulge systems, with a less massive stellar halo and an uninformative halo-mass limit.

quotes.quote: There are six `classical' bulges in our sample, shown in black, all but one (NGC 4258) with masses in excess of 2 10^ 10 M_ –-- these could plausibly have been expected to have been formed in (minor or major) mergers e.g., kormendy_kennicutt . Three galaxies with classical bulges (M31, NGC 3115, Cen A) indeed have massive metal-rich stellar halos --- carrying >20 of the total galaxy stellar mass --- indicative of a minor or major merger. Three (M81, NGC 4258, NGC 7814) have less massive stellar halos. NGC 4258 has a low mass classical bulge and a relatively uninformative limit on its stellar halo mass.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4258 as kinematically complex, warped, and Seyfert 2, yet with EPG properties similar to the wider HALOGAS sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 4258. This galaxy features a complex kinematical pattern, with streaming motions in the innermost few kpc (as visible from the markedly asymmetric pv slice along the minor axis) and a 15 warp in both PA and INC distributed along its entire disc. It also contains an active nucleus and it is classified as a Seyfert 2 object. Despite this complexity, the properties of its EPG appear to be analogous to those of the rest of the sample.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4258 near the upper radio-loudness sequence, describes it as a spiral hosting an extremely weak AGN, and notes its total radio luminosity is likely not jet-related.

quotes.quote: Close to the upper sequence but still belonging to the 'Seyferts plus LINERs' subsample is located NGC 4258 (M106). This spiral galaxy hosts an extremely weak AGN. Its total radio luminosity is about 100 times larger than the nuclear one, and most likely is not related to the jet activity.

gmw_00000206.name: NGC 4261
gmw_00000206.overview: NGC 4261 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1101.1807, arXiv:1401.3747] hosting the Fanaroff-Riley class I radio source 3C 270 [arXiv:1101.1807, arXiv:2111.02430]. The galaxy features a central gas and dust disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0512527, arXiv:2208.14115] and symmetric, perpendicular radio jets [arXiv:1110.1275, arXiv:2208.14115] that excavate cavities in its surrounding hot gas [arXiv:1106.2449, arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 270 as a nearby elliptical-hosted low-power radio galaxy whose symmetric jet and counterjet show X-ray synchrotron emission, modest beaming, and gas-displacing radio structures.

quotes.quote: 3C 270 is hosted by the nearby elliptical galaxy \ z= 0.00746 , trager . The galaxy's major-axis extent to 25 B mag arcsec ^ 2 is 244 12 arcsec at position angle 160^ , and the ellipticity is 0.11 taken from http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu/ devauc . The radio emission extends roughly 520 arcsec at a position angle 88^ birkd , and is symmetrical on the large scale, as seen in Figure fig:xrbw where the black contours of radio emission show the brightest parts of the twin lobes. The jet radio emission to the west is somewhat brighter than the counterjet to the east, but the overall morphology suggests that the radio emission is not highly affected by relativistic beaming. Indeed, piner have used the apparent speed and the jet-to-counterjet brightness ratio from VLBI images to deduce a jet speed of (0.46 0.02) and an inclination angle of = 63^ 3^ .

Our \ observations of \ have resolved jet emission out to 31.7 and 20.3 arcsec in the jet and counterjet, respectively. The energy spectral index in the jet is = 1.22 0.22 (90 errors), with a consistent, but poorly constrained, value for the counterjet. The jet-to-counterjet intensity ratio of 1.6 0.3 ( 1 errors) is consistent with that found in the radio, and suggests only modest slowing of jet plasma to about 0.25c from the speed previously inferred on VLBI scales.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 270 with giant elliptical NGC 4261, describing its FR I-like lobes, bright core, twin jets, banded RM structure, and high-depolarization rims at its inner radio lobes.

quotes.quote: 3C 270 is a radio source classified as FR I in most of the literature, although in fact, the two lobes have different FR classifications at low resolution LB11a . The optical counterpart is the giant elliptical galaxy NGC 4261, located at the centre of a nearby group. The radio source has a symmetrical structure with a bright core and twin jets, extending E-W and completely surrounded by lobes. The low jet/counter-jet ratio indicates that the jets are close to the plane of the sky, with the Western side approaching LB11a .

We present detailed imaging of Faraday rotation and depolarization for the radio galaxies 0206+35, 3C 270, 3C 353 and M 84, based on Very Large Array observations at multiple frequencies in the range 1365 to 8440 MHz. All of the sources show highly anisotropic banded rotation measure (RM) structures with contours of constant RM perpendicular to the major axes of their radio lobes.

We also report the first detections of rims of high depolarization at the edges of the inner radio lobes of M 84 and 3C 270. These are spatially coincident with shells of enhanced X-ray surface brightness, in which both the field strength and the thermal gas density are likely to be increased by compression.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4261 as a nearby group-central elliptical hosting FR I source 3C 270, with radio lobes excavating cavities, compressing corona gas, and affecting AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: Using observations from the \ and \ X-ray observatories, we examine the interaction between the intra--group medium and central radio source in the nearby NGC 4261 galaxy group. We confirm the presence of cavities associated with the radio lobes and estimate their enthalpy to be 2.4 10^ 58 erg. The mechanical power output of the jets is 10 ^ 43 , at least a factor of 60 greater than the cooling luminosity in the region the lobes inhabit. We identify rims of compressed gas enclosing the lobes, but find no statistically significant temperature difference between them and their surroundings, suggesting that the lobe expansion velocity is approximately sonic ( M 1.05 ).

NGC 4261 is a boxy, cuspy--cored , slowly rotating E2 galaxy Ravindranathetal01 , whose primary axis of rotation is close to the major axis, leading to the conclusion that the galaxy is prolate DaviesBirkinshaw86 . The galaxy hosts a low-ionisation nuclear emission-line region LINER, Hoetal97b and the bright FR I radio source 3C 270 19 Jy at 1.4 GHz, Kuhretal81 , whose twin jets lie close to the plane of the sky. The galaxy has a kiloparsec--scale kinematically decoupled core Cappellarietal07 which contains a 100 pc--scale disk of dust and cool molecular and atomic gas Jaffeetal93,Jaffeetal94 whose rotation axis is closely matched with the axis of the radio jets Ferrareseetal96 . On large scales, deep optical imaging has revealed a faint tidal tail to the northwest and tidal fan extending southeast from the galaxy Taletal09 and there is evidence of anisotropy in the globular cluster distribution Giordanoetal05 .

sources.summary: NGC 4261 is described as non-variable in the X-ray spectral fits, with low soft and hard luminosity changes, no short-timescale variability, and UV-band variations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 We used two Chandra (May 2000 and February 2008) and two XMM Newton observations (December 2001 and December 2007) for this object. Given the different resolutions in both sets of observations we firstly performed the analysis separately (see Table obs ). Variations of 19 and 0 in HR were obtained for Chandra and XMM Newton, respectively (from Column 8 in Table obs ). The best fit for the Chandra spectra is a ME2PL model from the individual analysis. The simultaneous fit without allowing to vary any parameter (i.e. SMF0) resulted in a good fit ( ^2/d.o.f = 1.27 ).

Varying one parameter did not give an improvement in the final fit. Therefore, the source seemed to be non-variable (see Table bestfit and Figure bestfig ). X-ray luminosity variations were below 1 for the soft and the hard bands over a period of 8 years (Table lumincorr and Figure luminfig ).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4261 as an elliptic LLAGN with a supermassive black hole, fitting its SED with RIAF-dominated high-energy emission and radio jet contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 is an elliptic galaxy at 30 Mpc of distance, located at the Virgo cluster. It harbors a SMBH with 5.2 10^8 M_ Tremaine2002 .
The SED data is available in table tab:NGC4261 . presented data points and similar modeling for the same galaxy. We showed the SED data points and our best fit in figure fig:4261-sed .

For NGC 4261 and NGC 315, our models were more focused on the RIAF component, especially for higher frequencies. In our model, the innermost regions of the RIAF produce most of the X-ray emission. We used the jet component to explain mostly the radio region of the SED. We did not consider the jet emission to explain the observed high energy emission (optical, UV, or X-ray bands).

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 4261 as a radio elliptical with a nuclear dust/CO disc, measures its SMBH mass, and identifies likely central non-circular gas motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 is a well-known elliptical galaxy hosting a famous nuclear dust disc (among the first ones detected in an ETG; Jaffe93 ; see Figure fig:NGC4261_optical ). The galaxy is classified as a slow rotator Emsellem11 , with prolate stellar rotation Krajnovic11 .
NGC 4261 is the host of the FRI radio source 3C 270, characterised by a prominent two-sided jet emanating from a bright core, and symmetric lobes on larger scales e.g. Morganti93,Worrall10 .

In NGC 4261, the CO gas is distributed in a small-scale, disc-like structure, extending up to 250 pc along its major axis. The mean line-of-sight velocity and major axis velocity curve clearly indicate that the central gas kinematics is dominated by the SMBH gravitational influence, allowing us to determine an accurate SMBH mass of M_ BH =(1.62 0.04 ) 10^ 9 M _ . This is fully consistent with a previous CO dynamical SMBH mass estimate in this object, and allows us to perform a crucial cross check between the different methodologies developing in this field. Signs of non-circular motions (likely outflow) are also identified in the central CO regions of this object. The derived M_ BH places NGC 4261 slightly above the M_ BH _ relation, but well within its scatter.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBI observations of NGC 4261, locating marginal redshifted H2O maser emission near the receding jet and linking it to the obscuring disk or jet-cloud interaction.

quotes.quote: We report dual-frequency very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) observations at 22 and 43 GHz toward the nucleus of a nearby radio galaxy NGC 4261. In particular, we present a VLBI image of the 22 GHz H _2 O maser line and its location in the circumnuclear region of NGC 4261. H _2 O maser emission is marginally detected above the three times the rms level within a velocity range of approximately 2250--2450 km s ^ 1 , slightly red-shifted with respect to the systemic velocity. H _2 O maser emission is located approximately 1 milliarcsecond (mas) east of the brightest continuum component at 22 GHz, where the continuum spectrum is optically thick, that is at the free--free absorbed receding jet by ionized gas.

We conducted high-sensitivity VLBI observations of the second-nearest radio-loud H _2 O megamaser source NGC 4261 at 22 GHz and 43 GHz. The captured double-frequency VLBI images of the continuum emission have revealed a two-sided jet structure and a central bright component aligned along the east--west direction. The resultant spectral index distribution along the jet structure was found to be consistent with the FFA on the jet bases caused by the dense ionized gas associated with the obscuring disk. H _2 O maser emission was detected at a slightly red-shifted velocity relative to V_ sys with a significance over the 3 level, utilizing the in-beam phase-referencing technique.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4261 as an old E2 elliptical with non-uniform X-ray sources, weak optical merger signatures, a dust lane, radio jets, and possible recent localized star formation.

quotes.quote: images present evidence for a non-uniform spatial distribution of discrete X-ray sources in the elliptical galaxy NGC 4261. This non-uniform distribution is inconsistent with the optical morphology of NGC 4261 at greater than a 99.9 confidence level.

NGC 4261 is a nearby (35.1 Mpc for H_ o =75 km/s ) E2 type galaxy, belonging to a poor group of galaxies (Garcia, 1993) but without any evidence for interaction with other members of the group. Optical observations show a 20 ( 3.4 pc) long dust lane along the North-South axis of the galaxy (Martel 2000 and references therein) and a spectacular nuclear dust disk ( 2 ; Jaffe 1996, Ferrarese 1996). Its optical isophotes in larger scales have a very strong boxy morphology (e.g. Peletier 1990). Optical spectroscopy shows that its dominant stellar population has an age of 15 Gyr (Trager 2000). This galaxy also features two prominent radio jets (e.g. Birkinshaw Davies 1985, Jones 2000) emanating from an active nucleus.
In fact, optical observations indicate stellar populations as old as 9 and 15 Gyrs in the nuclear regions of NGC 4697 and NGC 4261 respectively (Trager 2000).

sources.summary: The paper reports fast-frequency-switching VLBI observations of the FR I radio galaxy NGC 4261, detecting it at 86 GHz with a 59.3 mJy peak, the weakest continuum VLBI source then detected at that frequency.

quotes.quote: We use a new phase-calibration strategy to calibrate the phase of 86 GHz VLBI observations of the FR I radio galaxy NGC 4261. Instead of switching between a calibrator source and the target source, the target was observed while rapidly switching between the target frequency and a lower reference frequency. Self-calibration at the reference frequency yielded phase corrections which were multiplied with the frequency ratio and applied to the target frequency visibilities. The resulting detection of NGC 4261 is, to our knowledge, the first of NGC 4261 with 86 GHz VLBI, and it is also the weakest source so far detected with VLBI at that frequency.

Following the same data reduction path as for the 43 GHz data, we obtained good detections of NGC 4261 at 86 GHz on baselines among the four stations FD, KP, LA, and PT and only weak detections on baselines to NL, OV and MK. The corrected visibility phases are plotted in Fig. fig:86_GHz_corr_vis , and an image is shown in Fig. fig:4261_86GHz_raw . The peak flux density is 59.3 mJy. To our knowledge, this is the first VLBI detection of NGC 4261 at 86 GHz, and is probably the weakest continuum object ever detected with VLBI at this frequency.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4261 as the main galaxy in a Virgo West group, with a nuclear dust disk, black hole, blue nucleus, and double-sided radio jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 (3C 270) NGC 4261 is the main galaxy in a group of 33 galaxies (Nolthenius 1993) in the `Virgo West' cloud. It is a prolate galaxy with stellar rotation around the apparent major axis (Davies Birkenshaw 1986). NGC 4261 has a small nuclear dust disk (diameter 240 pc/ 1.7'' ) discovered by Jaffe (1993).

By modeling the central gas kinematics they detect a black hole with mass 5 10^8 . M o llenhoff Bender (1987) detected in addition to the central dust disk a dust feature at 15'' (2.1 kpc) north of the nucleus which falls outside our image. Jaffe McNamara (1994) detected central HI and CO in absorption. The dust obscured nucleus shows a faint blue point source. NGC 4261 has a double-sided radio-jet (e.g., Birkenshaw Davies (1985); Jones Wehrle 1997).

sources.summary: The paper compares X-ray measurements of NGC 4261's AGN, infers strong UV extinction, and treats optical/UV measurements as poor direct AGN flux tracers.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261. -- The X-ray spectrum of the AGN in NGC 4261 has been measured recently by both \ zezas05 and \ gliozzi03 . Both observations yield the same spectral index and flux but significantly different equivalent hydrogen column densities (the \ spectrum yields N_ H =3.7 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , while the \ spectrum yields N_ H =5.0 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ). A UV observation with the , reported by zirbel98 , yields only an upper limit of < 4.9 10^ 38 at 2300 , which produces a very large dip in the \ see Fig. 3 of ho99 . Neither of the column densities measured from the X-ray spectra produces a reasonable extinction correction of the UV limit; the lower value produces a negligible correction, while the higher value moves the upper limit many orders of magnitude above the monochromatic X-ray luminosity at 0.5 keV. Therefore, we infer that the AGN is indeed completely extinguished in the UV by the large equivalent hydrogen column measured from the \ spectrum.

Similarly, we doubt that the measurements of the nucleus of NGC 4261 in the optical band capture the AGN. Therefore, we do not apply any extinction corrections to these measurements in Table T:indsed nor to the points plotted in Figure F:indsed .

sources.summary: NGC 4261 has Bondi-rate, inclination, and jet-power constraints; the authors prefer ADAF X-rays and argue optical-UV extinction likely hides central-engine emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 Estimates of the Bondi rate, inclination angle and jet power are available Gliozzi03, Merloni07 and there are two independent estimates of the jet power. We adopt i=63^ in our models Gliozzi03 . The accretion rate we obtain from the ADAF fit is somewhat smaller than m _ Bondi . A jet origin for the X-rays is disfavored since it would require p<2 . This AGN is likely to be strongly affected by extinction in the optical-UV (OUV) band (EHF10).

Hence, the OUV measurements probably do not capture the emission of the central engine. For this reason, it is not surprising that the models overpredict the OUV emission by almost one order of magnitude in Fig. fig:seds02 .

sources.summary: NGC 4261 is treated as NGC 4264's nearby interacting neighbor and as a bright contaminant whose SDSS light was modeled and subtracted from imaging.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, the galaxy was classified as a possible member of the Virgo Cluster Kim2014 and it belongs to the rich galaxy group around the early-type galaxy NGC 4261 Garcia1993, Kourkchi2017 . According to , NGC 4264 possibly forms an interacting couple with NGC 4261 (Fig. fig:sdss_image ) which lies at a projected distance of 3.5 arcmin (30 kpc). But, they also pointed out that this interaction is not a necessary condition to trigger the nuclear activity of NGC 4261.

The radial profile of the background surface brightness shows a remarkable gradient in both g and i band images due to the residual light contribution of the bright nearby galaxy NGC 4261. Since NGC 4261 is not fully targeted in the FOV of the NGC 4264 images, we retrieved also the g and i band images of NGC 4261 from SDSS-DR12. Then we performed a photometric decomposition of NGC 4261 by using the Galaxy Surface Photometry 2-Dimensional Decomposition algorithm ( gasp2d , MendezAbreu2008, MendezAbreu2014, deLorenzoCaceres2019 ). We modelled the surface brightness distribution of the galaxy with a S ersic law following the prescriptions given in Sec. sec:gasp2d .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4261 as a key central group galaxy whose jets, entropy profile, cold gas, and kinetic feedback illustrate chaotic accretion and CGM lifting.

quotes.quote: For example, NGC 4261 (represented by purple stars in Figure 1) has radio jets that are narrow out to > 10  kpc and terminate in lobes at 25 -45 kpc, consistent with a power output > 10^ 43 erg s^ 1 OSullivan_2011MNRAS.416.2916O . Meanwhile, that galaxy's profiles of gas density, temperature, and entropy at < 10  kpc remain nearly identical to those of the other galaxies with _v 300 km s^ 1 , even though its jets are unusually powerful. Propagation to such a distance requires the momentum flux in the jets to be at least as great as the ambient pressure, which is 10^ 11 erg cm^ 3 at 10  kpc in NGC 4261 OSullivan_2011MNRAS.416.2916O .

NGC 4261 appears to be a galaxy that is currently experiencing chaotic cold accretion because it has t_ cool / t_ ff 10 at 0.5  kpc, a multiphase medium at 100  pc that includes a cold gaseous disk, and is producing 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 of kinetic feedback power.

NGC 4261 The case of NGC 4261, a central group galaxy, is perhaps the most revealing. Red symbols represent Chandra observations by Werner+2014MNRAS.439.2291W , and a dashed red line shows a fit to observations by Humphrey_2009ApJ...703.1257H . From 0.1 to 10 kpc, the data points are consistent with a steady flow solution determined by _v = 297 km s^ 1 and r_0 = 2.5  kpc.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-frequency VLBI observations of NGC 4261, detecting its FR I AGN at 86 GHz and finding an unresolved point source likely tracing the jet base.

quotes.quote: One of the best targets for resolving details in the jet is NGC 4261 (3C 270). It is an elliptical low-luminosity FR I radio galaxy hosting a 4.9 10^8 M_ black hole ( Ferrarese1996 ) which powers a double-sided radio jet ( Jones1997, Jones2000, Jones2001 ). Given the distance to NGC 4261 of 28.2 Mpc ( H_0= 75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ) and its black hole mass, 86 GHz VLBI observations are expected to resolve 200 R_ s .
This is the first detection of NGC 4261 with VLBI at 86 GHz. It is also probably the weakest continuum object ever detected with VLBI at this frequency.

The image displays a point source only; further self-calibration of the data did not reliably converge on any extended structure. The point source is probably the brightest component seen in the 43 GHz image, which probably corresponds to the jet base.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBI HI absorption in NGC 4261, associating it with the counterjet and a thin circumnuclear disk connected to the HST dust disk and black-hole fueling.

quotes.quote: We report on high sensitivity, spectral line VLBI observations of the H i absorption feature in the radio galaxy 261. Although absorption is only detectable on the most sensitive baseline, it can be unambiguously associated with the counterjet and is interpreted to originate in a thin atomic circumnuclear disk. This structure is probably a continuation of the dusty accretion disk inferred from HST imaging, which could be feeding the massive black hole. H i column densities in front of the counterjet of the order of 10^ 21 (T_ sp /100 K ) cm ^ 2 are derived, consistent with X-ray data and VLBI scale free-free absorption.

Perhaps the most striking feature of the FRI radio galaxy 261 (3C 270) is its approximately 240 pc radius circumnuclear dust disk revealed by HST observations (Jaffe et al. \ ). Further optical observations of the kinematics of emission lines in the inner regions of this disk by Ferrarese et al. \ ( ) have since provided evidence for a central black hole of mass of 4.9 10^8 M _ . In the radio band, H i absorption has been detected toward the core of 261 using the VLA (Jaffe McNamara ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 270 as having unusually steep jet spectra, measurable upstream spectral indices, counter-jet high-frequency emission, and abrupt main-jet steepening near the flaring point.

quotes.quote: High-frequency ( > 10 ^ 14 Hz) emission is seen in many of the main jets and in the counter-jet of 3C 270.

3C 270 A detailed discussion of the radio structure, spectral index and magnetic-field structure of 3C 270 will be given by Laing, Guidetti Bridle (in preparation). The average spectral index of the jets of 3C 270 is unusually high. This is the only source where we can measure the spectral index accurately upstream of the flaring point. The abrupt steepening of the spectrum of the main jet from 0.55 to 0.7 between distances of 9 and 12 arcsec from the nucleus is seen most clearly in the unbinned profile (Fig. fig:profiles g). Given that our model fits give an unusually large range for the flaring-point position (6.5 -- 11.5 arcsec in projection), it is likely that the steepening occurs exactly at the flaring point.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4261 to test a 2D globular-cluster mapping method, finding anisotropic GC structure, spiral-like or shell-like features, and evidence for a complex merging history.

quotes.quote: We apply this method to NGC4261, a ``test galaxy'' where significant 2D anisotropy in the GC distribution has been reported. We confirm that the 2D distribution of GC is not azimuthally isotropic. Moreover, we demonstrate that the 2D distribution departures from the average GC radial distribution results in highly significant spiral-like or broken shell features. Overall, the same perturbations are found in ``red'' and ``blue'' GCs, but with some differences. In particular, we observe a central feature, roughly aligned with the minor axis of NGC4261, composed of red and most luminous GCs.
These results suggest a complex merging history for NGC4261.

sources.summary: The paper detects broad water maser emission in NGC 4261, linking its LINER nucleus, dusty torus, twin jet, and molecular absorption to a likely masing torus.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 The WMCP project lists the giant elliptical galaxy NGC 4261 ( v_ LSR =2240 7 km s ^ 1 2000AJ....119.1645T , D_ L = 35.6 Mpc, z = 0.0075 ) as a 109 mJy rms non-detection observed in 2002. We reached 3.1 mJy rms and detect broad emission that is fitted by a single Gaussian of 10.3 mJy, 154 km s ^ 1 FWHM centered on 2302 km s ^ 1 . Peak emission is redshifted by about +60 km s ^ 1 relative to the systemic velocity and has a total isotropic luminosity of 50 L_ .

NGC 4261 is a LINER galaxy associated with the low-luminosity FR- 1 radio source 3C 270 that launches a highly symmetric kpc-scale twin jet. The galaxy is known for its 240 pc nuclear torus/disk found by the Hubble Space Telescope.

Given the evidence for abundant H 1 and OH molecules and a high X-ray absorbing N_ H column density towards NGC 4261, combined with the radio jet and the maser profile, a masing torus region with gas infalling at +60 km s ^ 1 seems plausible.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC4261 as a giant elliptical in a small group, hosting radio AGN 3C270, with boxy isophotes and supernova placement tied to merger or accretion structures.

quotes.quote: NGC4261 and NGC4649 are giant ellipticals with L_ K = 11.4 \ L_ K, , while NGC4278 is of intermediate stellar mass, with L_ K = 10.8 \ L_ K, devaucouleurs1991 ; all of them have old stellar populations, with ages ranging from 12 to 16.3 Gy see compilation in boroson2011 . While NGC4649 is in Virgo, NGC4261 and NGC4278 are in small groups see garcia1993 . NGC4261 hosts a luminous radio AGN 3C270, birkinshaw1985 , NGC4649 hosts a low-luminosity nuclear radio source dressel1985 and NGC4278 has a LINER nucleus, which is also detected in the radio younes2010,terashima2003 .

These three galaxies all present some sign of merging or accretion of external matter, other than our results: NGC4261 has clear boxy isophotes nieto1989 , usually suggestive of merging kormendy1996 .

Both NGC4261 and NGC4649 are early-type SuperNova (SN) Ia host galaxies. SN 1939B, located close to the outer side of the large-scale arc structure of GCs discussed by dabrusco2013a in the S-W quadrant of NGC4261, was discovered by and was later classified as type Ia SN.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4261 as the exceptional single-phase elliptical whose inner gas reaches the precipitation threshold and whose unusually powerful jets imply cold chaotic accretion.

quotes.quote: However, in one of those single-phase ellipticals (NGC 4261) we find / 10 at < 300 pc. Notably, its jets are 50 times more powerful than in the other nine ellipticals, in agreement with models indicating that precipitation near the black hole should switch its fueling mode from Bondi-like accretion to cold chaotic accretion.

The tenth galaxy is NGC 4261, shown with purple stars in Figure 1. It is the only one that drops below the precipitation threshold within 0.5 kpc (see Figure 2), and its radio luminosity ( 3 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 ) corresponds to a jet power 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 , almost two orders of magnitude greater than in the other galaxies. From the central black-hole mass of this galaxy 5 10^8 M_ , KormendyHo2013ARAA..51..511K and its entropy in the innermost bin, we infer a total Bondi power 3.5 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 , which would imply an implausibly large jet efficiency 0.3 . We therefore suggest that NGC 4261 may be a case in which cold chaotic accretion triggered by precipitation of cold clouds has temporarily boosted the accretion rate far above the Bondi level, as predicted by Gaspari+2013MNRAS.432.3401G,Gaspari+2014arXiv1407.7531G .

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 270 may host a torus or NLR-like MIR component, consistent with polarized broad H emission and previously noted weak warm dust.

quotes.quote: 3C 270 We show our best fit for 3C 270 in Figure fig:SED-3C270-ndoa , the best fit with a forced torus component in Figure fig:SED-3C270-cdoa , and the fractional contributions to the flux in each of the two models in Tables tab:3C270_frac_ndoa and tab:3C270_frac_cdoa respectively. We suspect that the preference for a narrow-line region model in this source is due to a bias in our analysis since BIC favors models with fewer parameters and the NLR model has fewer parameters than a clumpy torus model. Therefore we cannot rule out a torus in this source. The presence of a torus in 3C 270 would be consistent with results by Antonucci (private communication) who detected broad polarized H emission. This is potentially indicative of a Thomson-scattered hidden broad line region such as that expected in a high-excitation AGN, however Antonucci (private communication) caution that it could instead be due to narrow lines in the complex.

Additionally, although ultimately do not conclude the existence of a warm MIR thermal component in any of the eight FR-I radio galaxies in their sample they list 3C 270 as a possible exception due to a weak MIR consistent with 200 K dust. note a 60 pc disk of cold dust in HST images of 3C 270 surrounding its unresolved nucleus, the inner regions of which may be heated by the AGN (or star formation but our SFR estimates are too low for that) to produce the MIR excess detected.

sources.summary: The paper maps dense molecular gas in NGC 4261, finding a Keplerian circumnuclear disk, enclosed central mass, two-sided jet, and dense-gas link to accretion.

quotes.quote: We present the first detection and maps of HCN J =1--0 and HCO ^+ J =1--0 emission lines from the circumnuclear region of a nearby radio galaxy, NGC 4261, using the Northern Extended Millimeter Array. Both molecular lines are detected at a radial velocity of 700 km s ^ 1 relative to the systemic velocity of the galaxy, and they arise from a CND with an outer radius of 100 pc. The velocity fields of HCN and HCO ^+ are fitted with a Keplerian disk rotation. The enclosed mass is (1.6 0.1) 10^ 9 M_ , assuming a disk inclination angle of 64^ . The continuum image at 80 GHz reveals a weak two-sided jet structure extending over 5 kpc along the east--west direction and a bright core at the centre.
The dense gas mass associated with the CND is calculated to be 6.03 10^7 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4261 as a clear minor-axis rotator and finds it most likely prolate, consistent with earlier prolate modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261. DB provide kinematic data on four position angles out to 55 . The major and minor axis data are supplemented with data from Bender et al. \ ( Bender1994 1994), who place their slits 4 from those of DB. The average of the slit positions of the two papers is therefore used when combining the two datasets. Although this does introduce some error into the data it is very minor compared to the uncertainty in the velocity measurements. This galaxy is clearly a minor axis rotator with a turnover radius of the rotation curve at 20 .
The famous minor-axis rotator NGC 4261, not surprisingly, turns out to be most likely prolate, though there are oblate models not excluded at the 2 level.

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 4261/3C 270 has infrared emission dominated by non-thermal nuclear activity, with its observed nuclear disk contributing little to the total infrared flux.

quotes.quote: Infrared emission in radio galaxy NGC 4261 I.M. van Bemmel address= ESA fellow, Space Telescope Science Institute, Baltimore MD, USA Space Telescope Division of ESA C.P. Dullemond address= Max Planck Institute f 'ur Astronomie, Heidelberg, Germany M. Chiaberge address= Istituto di Radioastronomia, Bologna, Italy F.D. Macchetto address= Space Telescope Science Institute, Baltimore MD, USA footnote We have analyzed the total and nuclear SED for \ and find that the dominant process for the mid- and far-infrared emission in this object is non-thermal emission from the active nucleus. Modeling the emission from the optically detected 300 pc dust disk yields no significant disk contribution at any wavelength. To explain the observations, either the disk has an inflated inner region which partly absorbs the core, or the intrinsic core spectrum is curved. The inner 10 pc of the disk can potentially be conceived as an obscuring torus, albeit with optical depth around unity.

We have assessed the contribution of the observed nuclear disk to the total infrared emission in FR I radio galaxy \ (a.k.a. 3C 270). This disk is flatter and has lower optical depth than a typical obscuring torus, but might be responsible for the absence of a distinct UV core.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4261 as a group-central elliptical and radio source with symmetric jets, a nuclear gas/dust disk, possible obscured AGN, and boxy isophotes.

quotes.quote: NGC4261 is the main elliptical galaxy in a group of 33 galaxies located behind the Virgo cluster ( tex:HD83 ). The galaxy corresponds to the radio source (3C 270) which contains a pair of highly symmetric kpc-scale jets ( tex:BD85 ) and an edge-on nuclear disk of gas and dust in the optical ( tex:FF96a,tex:JW00,tex:FC06a ). In X-ray it is possible that the galaxy hosts a heavily obscured AGN ( tex:ZB05 ). The isophotal analysis shows boxy isophotes at large radii both in the optical and near-IR bands ( tex:BF94,tex:FF96a,tex:QB00 ). GD07 derived a S e rsic index fit of n=7.3 .

sources.summary: The paper uses published Bondi, inclination, and jet-power estimates for NGC 4261, comparing two jet-power methods and finding an AD-fit accretion rate below Bondi.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 For this object, estimates of the Bondi rate, inclination angle and jet power are available gliozzi03, merloni07 . There are two independent estimates of the jet power. The first is based on energetic considerations and the VLBA observations of the pc-scale jet, which yield a lower limit to the jet power gliozzi03 . In the second method we use the 5 GHz specific luminosity and the merloni07 correlation obtaining a much higher jet power estimate. Note that the accretion rate that we obtain from the AD fit is somewhat smaller than m _ Bondi .

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4261 as the faintest sample galaxy, with a central gas and dust disk, powerful jets/lobes, and jet power far above its inner cooling demand.

quotes.quote: The faintest galaxy in our sample is NGC 4261, with a bolometric X-ray luminosity of L_ X = 1.63 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 and flux of 1.36 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

NGC 4261 does show a nuclear disk of gas and dust in the innermost 100 pc see jaffe1993,jaffe1994 containing ( 5.4 1.8 ) 10^ 4 M_ of molecular and atomic hydrogen ferrarese1996 ; however, cool gas or dust has not been detected elsewhere in NGC 4261 serra2010,combes2007 .

The jet-powers estimated from the energies and timescales required to inflate the bubbles of relativistic radio-emitting plasma are P_ jet 8 10^ 42 ergs s ^ 1 in NGC 4472 allen2006 ; P_ jet 1.3 10^ 42 ergs s ^ 1 in NGC 4649 and P_ jet 2.2 10^ 42 ergs s ^ 1 in NGC 1399 shurkin2008 ; and P_ jet >10^ 43 ergs s ^ 1 in NGC 4261 osullivan2011 . In NGC 4472, NGC 4649, and NGC 1399 the energy required to keep the hot ISM within 6 kpc from cooling is only 2.5--10 per cent of the estimated jet-power, and in NGC 4261, which is the system with the most powerful radio source in our sample, this energy is less than 1 per cent of the power output of the AGN. In NGC 4261 the jets penetrate through the hot galactic corona and form lobes which deposit most of their energy into the surrounding intergalactic medium osullivan2011 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4261 as borderline MIR class-4/class-2 and a LINER, with M87-like MIR excess, high-ionization [NeV], X-ray cavities, and a dusty torus/synchrotron component suggesting AGN activity.

quotes.quote: Individual notes about MIR classification NGC 4261 (E3) The galaxy (see Figure fig4 ) is borderline between class-4 and class-2. The galaxy displays a MIR spectrum similar to that of NGC 4486 (see Figure fig5 ). At odds with NGC 4486, the spectrum of this galaxy shows the high ionization [ NeV ] 14.32 m emission line and likely a dusty torus is needed to fully account for the observed continuum, in addition to a synchrotron component.

The class-4 NGC 4261, a LINER in the optical, is located in the SB region of Figure fig7 . In the MIR, its AGN nature is suggested by the presence of an excess over the passive stellar continuum, similarly to NGC 4486, and by the high ionization [ NeV ] 14.32 m emission line, as well as, in X-rays, by the detection of a central AGN and cavities.

sources.summary: The paper reports no X-ray spectral changes for NGC 4261 across Chandra and XMM-Newton observations, no short-term variability, and UV filter variations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 : The simultaneous fit without allowing to vary any parameter (i.e., SMF0) results in a good fit both in Chandra and XMM -Newton data over a period of eight and six years, respectively. Simultaneous fit of Chandra and XMM -Newton (the annular region contributes 37 in Chandra data) do not show changes. Short term variations are not detected. Considering the UV range, variations amount to 9 in the UVW1 filter and 34 in the UVM2 filter.
