sources.summary: The radial-acceleration analysis treats NGC 4261 as a slow-rotator X-ray early-type galaxy in a group and limits its mass profile where the outer curve traces group potential.

quotes.quote: Three X ray ETGs are part of Atlas ^ 3D : NGC 4261 and NGC 4472 are classified as ``slow rotators'' (essentially non-rotating galaxies), while NGC 4649 is classified as a ``fast rotator'' but actually lies at the boundary of the classification ( V_ rot / _ 0.12 ). These X ray ETGs are representative of ``classic'' pressure-supported ellipticals, possibly metal rich, anisotropic, and mildy triaxial.

Four of these ETGs (NGC 1407, NGC 4261, NGC 4472, and NGC 4649) belong to groups with 10 to 60 known members, thus the outer mass profile may be tracing the group potential instead of the galaxy potential Humphrey2006 . For these galaxies, we restrict our analysis to R<4 R_ eff . This radius is determined by computing rotation curves from the mass profiles. For example, NGC 4261 has a relatively flat rotation curve within 4 R_ eff 18 kpc but starts to rise at larger radii, reaching velocities of 700 km s ^ 1 at 100 kpc. Clearly, such rotation curve cannot be due to the galaxy potential.

sources.summary: The paper extracts optimized spectra for NGC 4261, finds the lowest Mach-number lower limit among listed systems, reports a probable value, and associates it with weak turbulence.

quotes.quote: For a few objects (NGC 4261, NGC 4374, NGC 4472, and NGC 5044) we extracted RGS spectra from regions maximizing the detection of resonant scattering (Sect. subsec:turnover ). NGC 4261 has the lowest lower limit on Mach number, M>0.054 ( v_ 1D >13 ), while NGC 1404 shows the largest lower limit, M>0.59 ( v_ 1D >130 ). Among these objects, three galaxies also show the most probable value (NGC 1404, NGC 4261, and NGC 5846).

Interestingly, we measured (or have strong hints of) weak turbulence in the most relaxed giant elliptical galaxies, NGC 4261, NGC 4472, and NGC 4649 as identified by e.g. Therefore, this approach also requires additional data for most objects, except for NGC 4261, NGC 4374, NGC 4472, and NGC 5044, where current data are sufficient to maximize the scattering signal. studied spatial the variation of the optically thin to thick line ratio in NGC 4636, suggesting high turbulence in the southern, and low in the northern region of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 4261 as Core-halo, finding nuclear H-alpha emission aligned with more extended soft X-rays and comparing HST and ALMA observations of asymmetric ionized and molecular gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 (see Fig. Figure_AB5 ): The H emission is concentrated near the nucleus, thus we classified it as Core-halo . We find a correlation in the orientation of the soft X-ray emission and the ionised gas, although the first is much more extended ( > 15 from the centre). published H and [ O III ] images obtained with HST, showing a notably less extended H emission (maximum 3 vs our 5 ) and more asymmetric (more emission to the west) than what we find. used high resolution (about 0.2 ) CO observations with ALMA, finding that the molecular gas extends 2 on both sides along the north-south axis. The molecular gas kinematics can be globally reproduced by a rotating disc model.

sources.summary: NGC 4261 contains a compact rotating ionized-gas disk, blueshifted gas near the center, a dusty torus, misaligned prolate stellar rotation, and radio jets from the SMBH.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 -- NGC 4261 reveals a very compact gas distributions. A compact rotating disk is detected in NGC 4261, with a velocity gradient from 150 up to + 70 km s ^ 1 across 0.5 kpc ( 3 ) along the SN axis (minor-axis). Additionally, some blueshifted gas is moving at 80 km s ^ 1 , towards the E and W directions from the galaxy center across 1 kpc. HST images reveal a dusty torus around the inner rotating disk of ionized gas (see Fig.  fig:ngc4261_hst ).

In NGC 4261, the large-scale radio jets are being ejected by the SMBH nearly perpendicular to the plane of the ionized gas disk (see Fig.  fig:ngc4261_hst ) excavating cavities on the IGrM osullivan11 , similar to the BCG in Hydra-A hamer14,rose19 .

sources.summary: The model finds that 3C 270 can help explain the data with Virgo A and Fornax A, and some favored scenarios make it a dominating local source.

quotes.quote: We can conclude that a local source sample consisting of either Hydra A and Pictor A or Virgo A/ 3C 270 and Fornax A is sufficient to explain the data.

A good agreement with the observational data---including the energy spectrum below the ankle as well as the quadrupole strength which where both not included in the fitting procedure---is already obtained by using only Fornax A as well as Virgo A and/or 3C 270 as local sources. However, in this scenario the EGMF strength has to be around B_ rms 1 nG (for a coherence length of 1 Mpc), while the lifetimes of the sources have to be very large, of the order of Gyrs.

In particular, we find that scenarios where a few local sources---among them for instance Fornax A, Virgo A, 3C 270, Centaurus B or Hydra A---dominate the flux above the ankle, while low-luminosity radio galaxies contribute an isotropic background dominating below the ankle, provide a good fit to the data. Moreover, we found that neither the closest (Centaurus A) nor the most powerful source (Cygnus A) in the radio band is likely to give a dominant contribution to the observed UHECR events. Even though our results favor a major contribution by Fornax A as well as Virgo A and/or 3C 270 additional data on medium- and large-scale anisotropies will be necessary in order to draw clear conclusions on the importance of individual local radio sources.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4261 as an elliptical galaxy whose globular-cluster ULX and LMXB populations show long-term variability in Chandra data.

quotes.quote: Utilising archival Chandra X-ray Observatory data and Hubble Space Telescope globular cluster catalogues, we probe the time-domain properties of the low mass X-ray binary population in the elliptical galaxy NGC 4261. Of the 98 unique X-ray sources identified in this study, 62 sources are within the optical field of view and, of those, 33 are aligned with an optical cluster counterpart. We find twenty X-ray sources coincident with globular clusters; two are previously discovered ultra-luminous X-ray sources (ULXs) and eighteen are low mass X-ray binaries (GCLMXBs) with L_X < 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 .

We compare observations of these sources to the twenty previously-studied GCULXs from five other early-type galaxies, and find that GCULXs in NGC 4261 are of similar colour and luminosity and do not significantly deviate from the rest of the sample in terms of distance from the galaxy centre or X-ray luminosity. Both the GCULX and low mass X-ray binary (GCLMXB) populations of NGC 4261 show long term variability; the former may have implications for fast radio bursts originating in globular clusters and the latter will likely introduce additional scatter into the low mass end of GCLMXB X-ray luminosity functions.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4261 with multi-frequency VLBI, measuring its two-sided jet structure, nuclear core, proper motions, viewing angle, intrinsic speed, and sub-parsec jet collimation.

quotes.quote: We report multi-frequency VLBI studies of the sub-parsec scale structure of the two-sided jet in the nearby radio galaxy NGC 4261. Our analyses include new observations using the Source Frequency Phase Referencing technique with the Very Long Baseline Array at 44 and 88 GHz, as well as archival data at 15 and 43 GHz. Our results show an extended double-sided structure at 43/44 GHz and provide a clear image of the nuclear region at 88 GHz, showing a core size of 0.09 mas and a brightness temperature of 1.3 10^ 9 K. Proper motions are measured for the first time in the two-sided jet, with apparent speeds ranging from 0.31 0.14 to 0.59 0.40 in the approaching jet and 0.32 0.14 in the receding jet. The jet-to-counter-jet brightness ratio allows us to constrain the viewing angle to between 54 and 84 and the intrinsic speed to between 0.30 and 0.55 .

sources.summary: The paper finds elongated pressure and entropy structure in NGC 4261, high-temperature low-luminosity gas, and radio-lobe evidence for AGN-related disturbance.

quotes.quote: The spectroscopic analysis confirms the extension in the pressure in the north and south directions. Overall, the very high temperature (1.--1.5 keV) and low luminosity of the system yield very high entropy levels. The differences between the north-south and the east-west directions are clearly seen in the surface brightness reported in Fig. cf:1 as well as in the profile shown in Fig. n4261:pro . The profiles are identical within the central 20^ and outside 3^ . An explanation to the peculiar emission in NGC 4261 is offered through the comparison with the radio data, as is seen in Fig. n4261:radio , the radio lobes fill the space between the X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The atlas identifies 3C 270 with NGC 4261 and describes a nearby FR I boxy elliptical host with circumnuclear dust disk, black-hole mass work, and faint infrared nucleus.

quotes.quote: 3C 270 (NGC 4261); z=0.007465 A well-studied nearby FR I with a boxy elliptical host galaxy and a circumnuclear dust disk. used imaging and spectroscopy of this source to determine its black-hole mass. A faint unresolved nuclear point source is visible in the IR. Data first published by quillen+00 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4261 as a gas-rich early-type galaxy with an absorbed X-ray AGN, masked jet regions, and X-ray SMBH constraints consistent with gas-disk dynamics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 also hosts an X-ray emitting AGN but it is highly absorbed, so that the thermal gas dominates the spectrum below 2 keV, enabling accurate gas temperature determination even at the smallest scales ( sect_spectra ; zezas05a ; humphrey06a ).
We also masked out data around the jet in NGC 4261 zezas05a .

For NGC 4261, the data unambiguously rule out that \ determined from X-rays substantially exceeds the gas disk dynamical measurement. Although the lower limit from our analysis is largely determined by the prior (so we cannot place stringent limits on any bias due to nonthermal pressure support, although see sect_mbh ), there is no evidence from our analysis of a discrepancy between the gas disk dynamics measurement and our X-ray results.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC4261, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC4261 (Fig. fig:NGC4261 ) is a giant elliptical galaxy. The residual image shows at least two peculiarities: a high-ellipticity region immediately surrounding the core (between 3 and 15 ), and strong boxiness between 3 and 45 , while the isophotes are perfectly elliptical at all other radii. Interestingly, the boxiness is most pronounced at the same radius where the ellipticity peaks.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 4261's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261: The galaxy is a core elliptical Rusli2013b . We use their profile and derive R_e=54.2'' , in fair agreement with Beifiori2012 , who measure R_e=45.82'' from a Sersic fit to the i- band SDSS image. In contrast, Sani2011 get R_e=20'' from an improbable bulge plus disk decomposition, given the galaxy type, and Vika2012 derive R_e=21.6'' from a fit with low n_ Sersic . The M/L is scaled to the distance of KormendyHo2013 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4261 as a gamma-ray LLAGN and models its SED, preferring a jet/SSC interpretation over RIAF hadronic emission.

quotes.quote: The results from modeling the SED of NGC 4261 with the jet and RIAF scenarios are displayed in Figure fig:NGC4261 . Similar to the case of NGC 315, observations at E<500 \ MeV are adequately reproduced with an SSC component while the E>500 MeV data are not. The X-ray spectrum is overpredicted by SSC emission, while the radio spectrum is overall adequately fitted by the jet synchrotron emission.
As was found for NGC 315, the RIAF hadronic emission fails to reproduce the Fermi LAT observations, underpredicting the ray luminosity by a factor of four.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4261 as the motivating analog: an early-type galaxy with unusually low central entropy, t_cool/t_ff below 10, and the strongest radio source in its comparison sample.

quotes.quote: Previously, in a similar analysis of eight nearby X-ray and optically-bright elliptical galaxies, Werner2012 , found that NGC 4261 exhibited unusually low central gas entropy compared to the full sample. In the central 0.3 kpc of NGC 4261, the ratio of cooling time to freefall time ( t_ cool /t_ ff ) is less than 10 , indicating that cold clouds may be precipitating out of the hot ambient medium and providing fuel for accretion in the central region. NGC 4261 also hosts the most powerful radio source in the original sample. Because NGC 4261 may represent an important phase during which powerful feedback from a central active galactic nucleus (AGN) is fueled by multiphase condensation in the central kpc, we searched the Chandra archive for analogs to NGC 4261.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4261 in the Virgo outskirts and reports MATLAS shells or umbrella features, major-axis dust lanes, and a nuclear cool dust and gas disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261, Fig. fig:lsma , is a member of the Virgo cluster located in the outskirts of the cluster. The MATLAS image reveals a narrow shell or umbrella feature stretching around 9 (29 kpc) to the north and a probable fainter but wider shell closer to the center (around 7 , 22 kpc) on the other side of the galaxy. The galaxy also has dust lanes situated roughly along the major axis mb87,mah96 and a disk of cool dust and gas surrounding the nucleus jaf93 . In the MATLAS survey, NGC 4261 is classified as having shells and disturbed outer isophotes matlas20 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4261 as an elliptical LINER LLAGN, using archival X-ray and radio jet data and requiring kiloparsec-jet external Compton emission above 0.6 GeV.

quotes.quote: We have shown that SSC emission from the sub-parsec scale jet can well explain the broadband spectral energy distributions of these four LLAGNs, while external Compton emission from the kilo-parsec jet is required to explain the ray emission beyond 1.6 GeV and 0.6 GeV in the case of NGC 315 and NGC 4261, respectively.

NGC 4261 NGC 4261 is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.00738 ( cap11 ), with a nucleus classified as a type 2 Low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) based on high-quality optical spectra by ho97 .

We have analysed the X-ray data recorded by XMM-Newton in 2017 from NGC 4486, XMM-Newton data taken in 2019 and Swift data from 2017 to 2018 for NGC 315, and compiled the archival X-ray data available on NGC 4261 and NGC 1275 (see details in Section sec:xray ). In addition, we analysed 12 years of data, collected over the period of 2008 to 2020. Due to low spatial resolution in gamma-ray energy band, the distinction between the emission from the sub-parsec scale and kilo-parsec scale jet cannot be made. The radio and X-ray data points of the extended jets of NGC 315 and NGC 4261 were obtained from observations by Very Large Array (VLA) and Chandra observatories respectively owing to their high spatial resolution ( worrall315 , worrall4261 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4261 as a group-brightest galaxy with a nuclear optical source, gas and dust disc, perpendicular radio jets, a massive black hole, and LINER properties likely from an obscured low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: For example, NGC 4261 is the brightest galaxy in the NGC 4261 group. Hubble Space Telescope (HST) WFPC2 observations Jaffe1993 and our MUSE data reveals a bright nuclear optical source surrounded by a disc of gas and dust, while radio observations identified two jets perpendicular to the disc BirkinshawDavies1985,Scheneider2006,Kolokythas2015 . Recent kinematical studies using ALMA CO Boizelle2021 find a dynamical mass for the central supermassive black hole (BH) in NGC 4261 of 1.67 10 ^9 M _ . So, the observed LINER properties in this galaxy may be explained by an obscured low-luminosity AGN Zezas2005 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4261 as a Virgo elliptical FR I radio galaxy with bright low-redshift radio flux, jets aligned with a dust/molecular disk, megamaser emission, and absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 Galaxy and environment NGC 4261 is an elliptical in the Virgo cluster with among the brightest radio fluxes of low redshift z <0.01 , Gavazzi2004 FR I radio galaxies ( 19 Jy at 1.4 GHz, 205 mJy at 300 GHz). The axis of its radio jets is closely aligned with a 200 pc wide disky structure of dust and molecular gas traced with CO, HCN and HCO ^ + Jaffe1994, Boizelle2021, Swada-Satoh2022, Ruffa2023 . In the innermost regions, H _ 2 O megamaser emission is reported by Swada-Satoh2022 . Ruffa2023 determine the presence of a (1.62 0.04) 10^ 9 M _ supermassive black hole.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4261 as a low-luminosity radio galaxy whose dusty accretion disk, AGN illumination, broad lines, and jet shadowing constrain the accretion flow.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 (3C 270) is a low-luminosity radio galaxy with two symmetric kiloparsec-scale jets. Earlier Hubble Space Telescope observations indicated the presence of a hundred-parsec scale disk of cool dust and gas surrounding a central, supermassive ( 4.9 10^8 ) black hole. The recent detection of free-free radio absorption by a small, geometrically-thin disk, combined with earlier studies of the disk's large scale properties, provide the strictest constraints to date on the nature of the accretion process in this system. We show here that a supersonic disk, illuminated by the active galactic nucleus (AGN), can not only account for the observed radio shadowing, but can also produce the optical broad lines emitted from this region.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1216+06 as a LINER radio galaxy whose X-rays support radiatively inefficient accretion, with a nuclear Fe line and negligible jet contribution in prior detailed work.

quotes.quote: This idea is supported by the detailed study of 1216+06 (NGC 4261) by Gliozzi et al. (2003)---their argument is summarized by three main points: (1) they found there to be a high Bondi accretion rate along with a low AGN luminosity, suggesting the presence of a radiatively inefficient accretion flow; (2) they observed an Fe line at 7 keV of nuclear origin; (3) they studied the temporal and spectral variability, and combined this with independent information on jet energetics, which lead them to conclude that the bulk of the X--ray emission originates from a radiatively inefficient accretion flow, with negligible jet contribution.

The object 1216+06 has been carefully studied by Gliozzi et al. (2003) and the X--rays of that object are found to be related to accretion. We found that the rest of the objects in our sample behave like 1216+06, and while we can not rule out that a fraction of the X--rays come from the base of the jet, we performed a model independent test and found that the X--rays in this sample do not appear to be beamed.

We tried to add a \ line at 6.4 keV, but this was not statistically required for any of the objects except 1216+06 see sam03 . The spectral results are in good agreement with the findings of the spatial analysis.

sources.summary: NGC 4261 is presented as an unusual radio galaxy whose major-axis stellar rotation may indicate a nearly prolate or triaxial host with a stable dust disk orientation.

quotes.quote: Examining the properties of individual galaxies instead of general sample properties , we note two radio galaxies which could be exceptions to the above scenario: NGC 4261 and 3C 76.1. NGC 4261 is an unusual galaxy because the stellar mean motion is around the major axis (Davies Birkinshaw ). This could indicate that the galaxy is prolate. In that case the dust disk would lie in a plane which is not perpendicular to the long-axis.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBI observations of 3C 270 among four FRI radio galaxies, finding core-region polarization, no polarization beyond the core, and a two-sided jet suggesting a sky-plane orientation.

quotes.quote: We observed the four FRI radio galaxies viz., 3C 66B, 3C 78, 3C 264 and 3C 270, at 8.4 GHz ( ) with the Very Large Baseline Array (VLBA) 4 The NRAO is operated for the National Science Foundation (NSF) by Associated Universities, Inc. (AUI), under a cooperative agreement. The European VLBI Network (EVN) is a joint facility of European, Chinese, South African and other radio astronomy institutes funded by their national research councils. and five stations of the European VLBI Network ^4 viz., Effelsberg, Onsala, Yebes, Medicina, and the phased WSRT, on 2002 March 1. The observations were made in a ``snap-shot'' mode with the observing time per target totalling five to eight hours.
3C 270 has no detected polarization beyond its core (Fig. 1).
The fourth, viz., 3C 270, shows a two-sided jet, suggesting that it is inclined closer to the plane of the sky.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 270 as an X-ray absorbed FR I radio galaxy with a highly inclined dusty disk and optical absorption affecting its nuclear luminosity.

quotes.quote: We obtained a statistically significant estimate for the local column density in four objects (namely 3C 83.1, 3C 270, 3C 272.1 and 3C 296) with values N^z_H 0.2 - 6 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . In the other cases (8/12) we find an upper limit to N^z_H (Table tab_lett2 ) ranging from 0.03 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 to 0.7 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

Conversely, note that all three sources where we measured the largest X-ray absorbing column densities (namely 3C 83.1, 3C 270 and 3C 296) lie significantly above the correlation between L_ O and L_X . This is not surprising since, while the estimated X-ray fluxes are de-absorbed, the optical measurements are not corrected for the presence of local absorption.

All disks are seen at large inclinations, the less inclined being associated to 3C 270 whose axis form with the line of sight an angle 64^ jaffe96 . The fourth X-ray absorbed galaxy is 3C 272.1: its HST image show the presence of a low optical depth ( A_V 0.54 , bower97 ) filamentary dust lane, directly projected onto the nucleus. By no means dust is exclusively found in these 4 sources. The HST images of the remaining radio-galaxies also show the presence of dust, even in the form of disks. For example 3C 66B and 3C 78 have face-on disks sparks00 while disks at higher inclinations (but still substantially less inclined than in the 3 cases discussed above) are found in 3C 31 and 3C 449. Off-nuclear dust, usually distributed in filaments, is seen also in 3C 84, 3C 274, 3C 317 and 3C 338. In most of these sources, the quality of the X-ray spectra is sufficient to set stringent limits to the column density, N_H < 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . Therefore it appears that X-ray absorption is associated with the presence of dust, but only when i) the dust intercepts our line of sight to the nucleus or ii) it takes the form of a highly inclined disk. While the first case is rather obvious, the second situation This behaviour is shared also by the radio-galaxy B2 0055+30, with a large column density N_H 7 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 donato04 associated to a highly inclined disk in the HST image capetti00 . indicates that the gas responsible for the X-ray absorption shares the flattened distribution of the dust, inducing a dependence of N_H on the viewing angle. Note that evidence for a dependence of absorption on orientation in FR I nuclei was already found by chiaberge:uv from the comparison of optical and UV images of radio-galaxies. However, the column densities toward FR I nuclei are much smaller than those of the optical and X-ray absorbers predicted (and required) by the AGN unified models, i.e. the circumnuclear obscuring tori; as a comparison, in the obscured Seyfert 2 galaxies 75 have N_H > 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and about half of them are Compton thick ( N_H > 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 risaliti99 ). Unfortunately, we cannot constrain the location of the X-ray absorbing gas, other that it must be coplanar with the large scale dust. It might be also cospatial with the dust and thus being distributed on a kpc scale, rather than on the characteristic pc scale of tori in Seyfert galaxies. Let's now focus on the three sources for which we found the largest X-ray absorption. In Sect. corr we noted that their location with respect to the L_O vs L_X as well as L_O vs L_r correlations can be interpreted as due to absorption in the optical band This interpretation is supported by the extremely steep UV/optical nuclear spectrum of 3C 270 fr1sed which must be ascribed to absorption.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4261 as a large radio-galaxy system with hot-gas filaments, exclusion from one luminosity-temperature analysis, and possible large-radius energy transfer.

quotes.quote: From the images, there does appear to be evidence of disturbed hot gas in some group cores; groups such as NGC 741 (Fig. chandraimages ) and HCG 62 2004ApJ...607..800B show `ghost' X-ray cavities, and NGC 4261 shows `filaments' of hot gas outlining the radio lobes (shown in Fig.  chandraimages ), which also extend to larger scales see 2005MNRAS.357..279C . This radius was used as we had data for all groups in our sample, apart from NGC 4261 which was excluded from the L_X : T_X analysis since the original X-ray observation did not have data out to 0.05R_ 500 . These three systems (NGC 383, NGC 741, NGC 4261) have jets which transport the AGN energy out to large radii where the gas density may be too low to allow the energy to be radiated in less than a Hubble time.

The difference between the radio loud and radio quiet subsamples is smaller than the difference found by 2005MNRAS.357..279C , suggesting that ongoing large scale outbursts such as those in NGC 383 and NGC 4261 could be transferring energy out to large radii, and may have a stronger effect outside the core, whilst the effects of repeated outbursts are more likely to show up in the core.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 4261 as a literature LINER, but says its observed line ratios resemble normal galaxies and do not support that classification.

quotes.quote: Four of the galaxies listed in Table Xmetal Line Ratios are reported as low ionization nuclear emission-line regions (LINERs) galaxies in the literature: NGC 2768 LINER_Heckman80 , NGC 3115 Luis_LINER_ngc3115_template , and NGC 4125 and NGC 4261 LINER_SPEC_NGC4261 .

Note that NGC 4125 and NGC 4261 show no distinguishing line ratios from the normal galaxies and, the data presented here do not support the LINER classification. These two galaxies were classified as LINERs by LINER_SPEC_NGC4261 which had a similar observational aperture as this study, but only had wavelength coverage down to 4200 , excluding the useful [ O II ] 3727 information.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4261's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 is a radio-loud elliptical galaxy in the Virgo cluster at a distance of D= 31.7 3.2 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a FR I radio morphology and a LINER nucleus with polarized broad emission lines barth_polarized_1999 . It features a compact radio core and a pair of symmetric jets extending east-west (PA 88 ; birkinshaw_orientations_1985,jones_vlba_1997 ), which are also visible in UV and X-ray images chiaberge_what_2003,zezas_chandra_2005 . In addition to a compact NLR, a nearly edge-on nuclear dust disc roughly perpendicular to the jet axis was imaged (diameter 1.7 262 pc; PA 16 ; jaffe_large_1993,jaffe_nuclear_1996 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses Kormendy photometry comparisons and single-component dynamical mass-to-light values for NGC 4261, finding the second-largest mass deficit at 18 black hole masses.

quotes.quote: For six galaxies whose surface brightness profiles are taken from Kormendy-09a (i.e., NGC 4261, NGC 4374, NGC 4472, NGC 4486, NGC 4552, and NGC 4649), the best-fitting \ model of Kormendy-09a is also overplotted on the right-hand panels of Fig. fittingplots .

For the galaxies IC 1459, NGC 3379, NGC 4261, and NGC 4374 and the three objects with dubious black hole masses (see below) the \ values come from single-component dynamical modeling (i.e., without dark matter; see text).
The next-largest mass deficit is 18 times , in NGC 4261, with the one other galaxy having a mass deficit larger than 10 \ being NGC 4486.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4261 as NGC 4264's bright elliptical neighbor, likely gravitationally bound at 30 kpc and associated with the observed warp-like interaction.

quotes.quote: We present surface photometry and stellar kinematics of NGC 4264, a lenticular galaxy in the region of the Virgo Cluster undergoing a tidal interaction with its neighbour, NGC 4261.
In fact, NGC 4264 is located close to the bright elliptical galaxy NGC 4261 at a projected distance of 30 kpc and they are probably gravitationally bound.
We interpreted the twist of the outer isophotes as suggestive of a warp due to the ongoing interaction with NGC 4261.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4261 as the 3C 270 optical counterpart, an FR I radio source with jets, lobes, X-ray halo, and cavities.

quotes.quote: NGC 4261 NGC 4261 is the optical counterpart to the radio source 3C 270 birkinshaw1985 , which is a very well known FR I radio source inside the Virgo cluster W cloud; garcia1993 with well-defined radio jets and large radio lobes piercing through the atmosphere of the host galaxy and extending beyond from the core (Figure fig:chandra_vla_b f). A detailed study, combining data from the GMRT and VLA as well as comparing the radio emission with the X-ray emission of the hot gas, was published by and . \ From the X-ray point of view, NGC 4261 has an extended X-ray halo davis1995 with features showing the interaction with radio source in the form of X-ray cavities croston2008,o'sullivan2011 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4261 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: Non-regular rotation (NRR) is the most common characteristic of the Krajnovic2018 M3G BCGs, whereas we find it for only 8/18 BGEs: ESO 507-G025, NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 1060, NGC 5846, as well as NGC 4261 (which shows non-regular rotation around the major axis, as reported and discussed by Davies1986 ). NGC 193 and NGC 4261 are two of the most radio-luminous AGNs in the CLoGS BGE sample Kolokythas2018, Kolokythas2019 . Studying the thermodynamical properties of the X-ray atmospheres, Olivares2022 find that the filamentary structures and compact disks (the morphology of the ionised gas) are found in systems with small central entropy values, short t_ cool /t_ ff and t_ cool /t_ eddy ratios (NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 978, NGC 1060, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4261, NGC 5846, NGC 7619).

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4261 as an FR-I with Doppler factor near unity, ray properties like Fornax A, and potential future lobe-dominated Fermi LAT emission.

quotes.quote: Besides the four FR-Is mentioned above, the other two FR-Is (NGC 3894, NGC 4261) are also possessing the Doppler factors 1 , first reported in 4FGL. There is one FR-I, NGC 4261, whose ray properties ( L_ =41.25 erg/s, _ ph =2.08 , =0.88 ) are similar to those ( L_ =41.39 erg/s, _ ph =2.07 , =0.97 ) of Fornax A, and the redshifts between NGC 4261 (0.007) and Fornax A (0.006) are similar. We hoped that NGC 4261 is a potential target whose dominant ray emissions in the lobe component can be detected by Fermi LAT in the future just like the observations of Fornax A.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 270 as exceptional in the FR I sample: it alone shows significant 2-10 keV variability, has the largest intrinsic absorption, and has excess X-rays attributed mainly to accretion flow.

quotes.quote: According to a ^ 2 test for constancy, no significant variability of the 2--10 keV flux is detected in the sources of our sample, except for 3C 270.
The source 3C 270 stands out for the largest intrinsic column density, N _H^z 10^ 22 , in agreement with previous findings Glio03 .

On the other hands, 3C 270 appears to have an excess of X-ray flux for its given inclination, supporting our previous claims that the bulk of the X-ray emission originates from the accretion flow Glio03 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC4261 as strongly underpredicted in the basic model, notes a central gas and dust disk, and says it remains underpredicted by about two orders.

quotes.quote: Sources that were under-predicted 4-7 orders of magnitude are: NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC1553, NGC4261, NGC4438, NGC4594, NGC4697 and IC4296.

NGC1291 is an early type spiral, NGC1316 is disturbed early type elliptical, NGC 1553 has a spiral feature passing threw a center, NGC 4261 core is surrounded by a disk of gas and dust, NGC4438 has an outflow bubbles which also may indicate angular momentum to dominate the accretion flow, NGC4594 is a spiral galaxy, the last two sourced NGC 4697 and IC4296 are elliptical radio galaxies.
Two sources: NGC1553 and NGC 4261 are still under-predicted of about 2 orders of magnitude.

gmw_00000207.name: NGC 4278
gmw_00000207.overview: NGC 4278 is a nearby elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0902.1652] hosting a low-luminosity LINER active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2405.07691] with symmetric, S-shaped radio jets [arXiv:1907.05879]. It features a large, regularly rotating H I disk connected to faint gas tails [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:1007.2059] and exhibits multi-year X-ray variability [arXiv:1004.5134].

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra monitoring of elliptical galaxy NGC 4278, cataloging its X-ray sources, LMXB population, variability, globular-cluster associations, and suitability for studying old stellar populations.

quotes.quote: We present the properties of the discrete X-ray sources detected in our monitoring program of the globular cluster (GC) rich elliptical galaxy, NGC 4278, observed with Chandra ACIS-S in six separate pointings, resulting in a co-added exposure of 458-ks. From this deep observation, 236 sources have been detected within the region overlapped by all observations, 180 of which lie within the D_ 25 ellipse of the galaxy. These 236 sources range in \ from 3.5 10^ 36 \ (with 3 upper limit 1 10^ 37 ) to 2  10^ 40 , including the central nuclear source which has been classified as a LINER. From optical data, 39 X-ray sources have been determined to be coincident with a globular cluster, these sources tend to have high X-ray luminosity, with ten of these sources exhibiting > 1 10^ 38 . From X-ray source photometry, it has been determined that the majority of the 236 point sources that have well constrained colors, have values that are consistent with typical LMXB spectra, with 29 of these sources expected to be background objects from the logN - logS relation.

The characteristics of NGC 4278 are similar to those of NGC 3379 in all but the GC population, where NGC 4278 has a rich globular cluster system ( S_ GC =6.1 1.5 , Harris 1991), making it an ideal galaxy to explore the evolution of LMXB from primordial field binaries in a rich environment. Like NGC 3379, NGC 4278 is a nearby (D=16.1 Mpc; Tonry \ 2001) relatively isolated, unperturbed `typical' elliptical galaxy with an old stellar population (age of 10.7 Gyr, Terlevich Forbes 2002). Observationally, NGC 4278 is an ideal target for LMXB population studies, because of its proximity, resulting in a resolution of 36  pc with Chandra , and its lack of a prominent hot gaseous halo.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4278 variable on long timescales, with normalization and intrinsic luminosity changes, including two-month X-ray flux variations, but no short-timescale variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 We only used three of the nine observations taken by Chandra , in March 2006, February 2007 and April 2007, and the XMM Newton observation in May 2004 (Table obs ). HR variations amounted to 4 for the runs with useful spectroscopic Chandra data. Taking into account all the observations from Chandra , 40 HR variations were found between 2000 and 2010 (from Column 8 in Table obs ). Our best fit for Chandra data was a MEPL model with Norm_2 varying (i.e. SMF1, see Figure bestfig ). This parameter varied 30 between the first and the last observation ( 1 year apart) (Table bestfit ).

It is remarkable that flux variations were 11 (13 ) between the second and the third observations (two months apart), with a 13 variation in Norm_2 for the same period (Tables bestfit and lumincorr ). We compared the XMM Newton observation in 2004 with the Chandra observation in 2006, which is the closest in time (see Figue images ). We applied the procedure explained in section apertures .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4278 as a variable LINER with symmetric S-shaped jets; its nonthermal-electron ADAF model may be hard for EHT but possibly observable with GMVA.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 is a LINER source displaying variability both in short and long timescales in X-ray and radio bands Younes2010 . It displays a two-sided structure, with symmetric S-shaped jets emerging from a flat-spectral core Giroletti2005 . Its nucleus consists of a black hole of mass M_ BH 4 10^8 M_ and the galaxy is at a distance of 14.9 Mpc Giroletti2005, Nemmen14 . The data in Table - [ tab:NGC4278 ] displays variability which can also be seen in the left panel of Fig. [ fig:NGC4278spec ] . The XMM-Newton data points are taken from the observation of August 2013.

With all the uncertainties taken into account, we fit the data with an ADAF which consists of emission also by the non-thermal electrons as we have seen for M87 and Cen A. It can be seen in the right panel of Fig. [ fig:NGC4278spec ] that the presence of power-law electrons results in emission through the entire ADAF at the radio frequencies although displaying a gradual increase towards the core. Given such an emission profile, it may be difficult to observe the source with the EHT due to the low flux at 230 GHz but could possibly be observed by the GMVA.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4278 as a nearby FRI-like CSO/LINER gamma-ray emitter with a bright core, SED, low-accreting AGN classification, and possible weak beaming.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 1FLT J1219 + 2907 was detected in TBIN 7 (2009 March 5 to 2009 April 5) of our analysis and, using the positional association procedure, we found that NGC 4278 lies within its error ellipse. The SED of NGC 4278 is shown in Figure figure:NGC4278 . NGC 4278 (z = 0.0021) is a nearby galaxy identified as a likely radio compact symmetric object (CSO) with a FRI morphology. It shows a dominant, bright, flat-spectrum component identified as the core (see Figures 2M and 3M in tremblay2016compact ).

giroletti2005low reported that the source is oriented at a very small viewing angle ( 2^ < <4^ ) with respect to the line of sight, although they did not find compelling evidence for strong beaming. They also proposed an alternative scenario in which the source could be oriented at a larger angle, with asymmetries related to the jet's interaction with the surrounding medium. NGC 4278 is optically classified as a low-luminosity and low-accreting AGN with a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER). All of these characteristics make NGC 4278 a very intriguing new type of ray emitter.

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 4278 as a nearby LLAGN, finds variability and increasing flux, and measures a damped-random-walk timescale with substantial uncertainty from long-term behavior.

quotes.quote: Six LLAGNs, Centaurus A (hereafter Cen A), NGC 4374, NGC 4552, NGC 4579, NGC 4278, and NGC 5077, were selected. These sources are predicted to have variability timescales ranging from a few days to ten days, due to their black hole mass being situated between Sgr A* and M87. This is expected to provide clarity on the sub-millimeter mass scaling relationship.

We accumulate four years of observations using Submillimeter Array for Centaurus A, NGC 4374, NGC 4278, and NGC 5077 and one year of observations for NGC 4552 and NGC 4579. All sources are variable. We measure the characteristic timescale at which the variability is saturated by modeling these sources' light curve as a damped random walk. We detect a timescale for all the sources except NGC 4552. The detected timescales are comparable to the orbital timescale at the event horizon scale for most sources.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4278 as a nearby LINER/LLAGN elliptical with S-shaped jets, radio components, and high-energy emission requiring an additional jet component.

quotes.quote: The Large High Altitude Airshower Array (LHAASO) has detected very high energy gamma rays from the LINER galaxy NGC 4278, which has a low luminosity active galactic nucleus, and symmetric mildly relativistic S-shaped twin jets detected by radio observations. Earlier, several radio-emitting components were detected in the jets of NGC 4278. We model their radio emission with synchrotron emission of ultra-relativistic electrons to estimate the strength of the magnetic field inside these components within a time-dependent framework after including the ages of the different components.

We show that the synchrotron and synchrotron self-Compton emission by these components cannot explain the Swift X-ray data detected from NGC 4278 and the LHAASO gamma-ray data associated with NGC 4278. We suggest that a separate component in one of the jets is responsible for the high energy emission whose age, size, magnetic field and the spectrum of the ultra-relativistic electrons inside it have been estimated after fitting the multi-wavelength data of NGC 4278 with the sum of the spectral energy distributions from the radio components and the high energy component.

NGC 4278 is an elliptical galaxy located at a distance of 16.7 Mpc Tonry_2001 . Hence, they concluded that NGC 4278 may appear as a LINER or a Seyfert depending on the intensity of its X-ray emission. reported a detailed study of the radio-emitting components in the jets of NGC 4278 using the observational data from Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) at 5 GHz and 8.4 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 1217+29 as a compact, weak radio source in an elliptical host with a regular but misaligned H I disc, faint H I tails, dust lane, and old stars.

quotes.quote: B2 1217+29 (NGC 4278): \ observations of this sources have been published by rai81 , lee94 and mor06b . They all detect an \ disc with regular rotation, although the gas is not co-planar with the rotation of the stars in the inner part of the galaxy and shows indications for non-circular motions. Deep \ imaging by mor06b shows that the \ disc is somewhat asymmetric and slightly elongated eastward (roughly in the direction of a close companion) and contains two faint tails of \ gas on either side.

Interestingly, B2 1217+29 shows no evidence for \ absorption against the central continuum. In our deep optical image, both B2 1217+29 and the smaller, close companion towards the north-east have the appearance of a typical elliptical galaxy, although B2 1217+29 contains a faint dust-lane stretching from north-east to west of the nucleus. Typical for early-type galaxies, B2 1217+29 contains a relatively old stellar population e.g. san06 . The radio source B2 1217+29 is compact and the second weakest source in our sample (see Table tab:sourceproperties ).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4278 as a low-accretion LINER, finding multi-year X-ray variability and SED changes that can resemble either LINER or low-luminosity Seyfert activity.

quotes.quote: We investigate the LINER--Seyfert connection by studying the unabsorbed LINER galaxy NGC 4278 that accretes at a low rate (L _ bol/Edd 7 10 ^ 6 ) but exhibits a broad H line, and a point-like nucleus in radio, optical, UV and X-rays. We analyzed one XMM-Newton and seven \ X-ray observations of NGC 4278 spread over a three year period, allowing the study of the X--ray variability at different time-scales (hours, months, years). We also examined the radio to X-ray spectral energy distribution to constrain the accretion mode in the nucleus of . Long time-scale (months) variability is observed where the flux increased by a factor of 3 on a time-scale of a few months and by a factor of 5 between the faintest and the brightest observation separated by 3 years.

We constructed SEDs based on simultaneous or quasi simultaneous observations and compared them to LINER, radio--loud, and radio--quiet quasar SEDs. We find that at a low X--ray flux the \ SED resembles that of typical LINER sources where the radio to X--ray emission can be considered as originating from a jet and/or RIAF, whereas at a high X--ray flux, \ SED is more like a low luminosity Seyfert SED. Consequently, \ could exhibit both LINER and Seyfert nuclear activity depending on the strength of its X--ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4278's central kiloparsec, finding multi-phase dusty gas, molecular hydrogen, PAH changes, and shock heating likely tied to cold-gas accretion.

quotes.quote: We present the Spitzer spectroscopic mapping observations toward the central kpc region of the nearby elliptical LINER galaxy, NGC 4278, by using the Infrared Spectrograph (IRS). These observations reveal rich mid-IR emission features of extended ionized gas, warm molecular hydrogen and dust. Different phases of gas and dust are closely related and belong to a same elongated feature.

H _ 2 S(0)-S(7) pure rotational lines of molecular hydrogen show excessive intensity and moderately high excitation temperature comparing with photon dissociation region (PDR). A strong and extended [ SiII ] emission line is detected, which could be a sign of reduced depletion of silicon in interstellar dust. We also discover an extended high ionization region associated with enhanced H _ 2 S(1) emission. We conclude that a shock-heating component is required to account for observed emission characteristics, which could be triggered by cloud-cloud interactions during accretion of cold gas from the large HI disk.

sources.summary: Deep Chandra observations of NGC4278 reveal hot gas out to 5 kpc, misaligned NE-SW emission, a dimmed nucleus, low-efficiency accretion, likely jet heating, and unusual multiphase gas.

quotes.quote: The analysis of a deep (579 ks) Chandra ACIS pointing of the elliptical galaxy NGC4278, which hosts a low luminosity AGN and compact radio emission, allowed us to detect extended emission from hot gas out to a radius of 5 kpc, with 0.5--8 keV luminosity of 2.4 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . The emission is elongated in the NE-SW direction, misaligned with respect to the stellar body, and aligned with the ionized gas, and with the Spitzer IRAC 8 m non-stellar emission. The nuclear X-ray luminosity decreased by a factor of 18 since the first Chandra observation in 2005, a dimming that enabled the detection of hot gas even at the position of the nucleus. Both in the projected and deprojected profiles, the gas shows a significantly larger temperature ( kT=0.75 keV) in the inner 300 pc than in the surrounding region, where it stays at 0.3 keV, a value lower than expected from standard gas heating assumptions. The nuclear X-ray emission is consistent with that of a low radiative efficiency accretion flow, accreting mass at a rate close to the Bondi one; estimates of the power of the nuclear jets require that the accretion rate is not largely reduced with respect to the Bondi rate.

Among possibile origins for the central large hot gas temperature, such as gravitational heating from the central massive black hole and a recent AGN outburst, the interaction with the nuclear jets seems more likely, especially if the latter remain confined, and heat the nuclear region frequently. The unusual hot gas distribution on the galactic scale could be due to the accreting cold gas triggering the cooling of the hot phase, a process also contributing to the observed line emission from ionized gas, and to the hot gas temperature being lower than expected; alternatively, the latter could be due to an efficiency of the type Ia supernova energy mixing lower than usually adopted.

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 4278 as a Coma I elliptical with radio jets, X-ray variability, a large H I disk, significant GC bimodality, and 256 confirmed GCs.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 is an elliptical (E 1 - 2 ) member of the Coma I cloud. It has been extensively studied at different wavelengths: in radio, Nagar detected two sub-parsec jets; in X-rays, a long (months) time scale variability Ho and a dominant nuclear source Younes were detected. It has long been known for its massive ( 10^8 M _ ) H I disc extending beyond 10 \ Gallagher and used to infer the dark matter content at large radii Bertola93 . The bimodality of the GC system of NGC 4278 has been under debate.

In this work we present new wide field BVI Suprime-Cam imaging (with the B band in poor seeing conditions), combined with four g and z HST /ACS pointings downloaded from the Hubble Legacy Archive (see Section sec:subarudata ). We find statistically significant bimodality both in our ground-based and space-based observations, with the colour separation occurring at (B-I) 1.9 and (g - z) = 1.1 , respectively. We spectroscopically confirm 256 GCs over four DEIMOS masks observed in good seeing conditions.

sources.summary: NGC 4278 is modeled with a mildly relativistic near-line-of-sight radio jet, small transition-radius thin disk, and possible old stellar optical contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 Di-Matteo01 also estimated that the Bondi rate is m _ Bondi 0.001-0.01 . Giroletti05 found a two-sided radio structure for the jet using VLA data, and estimated that the jet is oriented close to the line of sight ( 2^ i 4^ ) and mildly relativistic ( _j 1.5 ). In our jet models we therefore adopt i=3^ and _j = 1.5 . The optical data point requires a truncated thin disk with the somewhat small transition radii r_ tr 30-40 . If we allow s to vary, we can also reproduce the SED with a larger transition radius, r_ tr =100 , m _ o =4 10^ 3 , =0.1 and s=0.77 .
Alternatively, the optical observation can be fitted with a 10^8 M_ stellar population. A JD model is disfavored for this source.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4278 as a deep early-type X-ray luminosity function comparison, noting possible faint-end flattening and completeness to low luminosities.

quotes.quote: We plot this function, scaled to fit NGC 4278's XLF, in Figure fig:xlf . It can be seen that Equation equ:bpl represents the high completeness regions of the different galaxies XLFs relatively well. For the two deepest XLF's, those of NGC 3379 and NGC 4278, there are suggestions that the XLF flattens slightly at around 1-2 10^ 37 . While a flatter power-law at these faintest luminosities may be genuine, consideration of this is beyond the scope of this paper.

For NGC 3379 and NGC 4278, the XLFs should be complete to this limit. For the other galaxies we extrapolate their LMXB population by assuming the universal broken power-law XLF discussed in Section sec:xlf and fitting it to the galaxies XLF above its detection limit.
Additionally, NGC 4278 and NGC 4697 have quite similar masses to NGC 3379, so a mass dependent IMF would be expected to effect all of these galaxies in a similar way.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4278 as the massive elliptical anchoring the Coma I cloud, whose loose satellite system has unusually large negative peculiar velocity.

quotes.quote: The most cryptic case is a flock of nearly 30 galaxies around NGC 4278 (Coma I cloud), moving to us with the mean peculiar velocity of 840 km s ^ 1 . This cloud (or filament?) resides at a distance of 16.1 Mpc from us and approximately 5 Mpc away from the Virgo center.

We have considered the wide outskirts of NGC 4278 and distinguished 28 distant galaxies with small radial velocities as possible members of the Coma I cloud. We have enlarged this sample with other ten galaxies which have not yet distance estimates but can still belong to this cloud, by reference to their texture. The whole sample is presented in the Table  table03:Kashibadze_n_en . Its columns contain (1) galaxy name; (2) equatorial coordinates; (3) de Vaucouleurs numerical morphological type; (4) radial velocity (km s ^ 1 ); (5), (6) galaxy distance (Mpc) and the method applied to determine it; (7) apparent B magnitude ; (8) peculiar velocity (km s ^ 1 ).

Pro ba bly, NGC 4278 with its satellites forms a part of an elongated fi la ment or sheet with the mean peculiar velocity of 840 km s ^ 1 , which is comparable with virial velocities in rich galaxy clusters.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4278 as a low-luminosity LINER/RL-AGN associated with VHE rays, finding X-ray brightening and BL Lac-like but lower-luminosity jet behavior.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278, a Low-luminosity active galactic nucleus (AGN), is generally classified as a low-ionization nuclear emission line region (LINER) type AGN. Recently, it is reported to be associated with a very high energy (VHE) ray source 1LHAASO J1219+2915 in the first Large High Altitude Air Shower Observatory (LHAASO) source catalog. However, no associated counterpart has been detected by Fermi-LAT. By analyzing its X-ray observation data from Swift-XRT, we find it is in a high-flux state on MJD 59546, with the X-ray flux more than one order of magnitude higher than that observed 11.7 year earlier by Chandra. We propose that the detection of VHE rays from NGC 4278 may be attributed to the presence of an active nucleus displaying behavior similar to that of a BL Lac.

Furthermore, NGC 4278 exhibits significantly lower luminosity in both radio and TeV bands when compared with typical TeV BL Lacs. In the radio-luminosity vs. Eddington-ratio plane, NGC 4278 shows greater similarity to Seyfert galaxies and LINERs rather than BL Lacs; however, it still roughly follows the extension towards lower luminosity seen in BL Lacs.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4278 has a bright soft-band nucleus plus an elongated feature, little absorption in its X-ray spectrum, and evidence for X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: The soft band image of NGC 4278 consists of a bright nucleus and a faint elongated feature with a length of 50^ and a position angle of 70^ . The hard band image is unresolved. In the two objects NGC 3147 and NGC 4278, the pileup is mild and we corrected for the effect by applying the pileup model implemented in XSPEC, where the grade morphing parameter was fixed at 0.5 (after initially treating it as a free parameter [ Davis 2001 ] since is not well constrained).

NGC 4278 (L1.9) . --- A result on the same data set is presented by Ho et al. (2001). We analyzed archival data observed on 1998 May 24. The effective exposure times after standard data screening were 19.6 ksec for each SIS and 23.9 ksec for each GIS. The spectrum is well fitted with a power law with a photon index 1.85 (1.77 1.94).

sources.summary: NGC 4278 has a large extended H I disk connected to gas tails and regular kinematics, implying it has remained gas rich for several Gyr without recent young stars.

quotes.quote: The final category (denoted D, for disc/ring) refers to galaxies where most of the \ is found in a fairly regularly rotating disc or ring. This category contains the galaxies NGC 2685, NGC 3032, NGC 3414, NGC 3489, NGC 4150 and NGC 4278, while also the only cluster galaxy detected (NGC 4262) falls in this category. In NGC 2685 and in NGC 4278 the \ disc is large, i.e. extending beyond the bright optical body, while in NGC 3032, NGC 3489 and in NGC 4150 the \ forms a small, inner gas disc.
Similarly, near NGC 4150 a small \ cloud is detected, while even the outer regions of the large, and presumably older, \ disc in NGC 4278 are connected to two large gas tails.

One example of such a galaxy is NGC 4278 where the fact that the large \ disc shows very regular kinematics implies that this galaxy has been gas rich for at least a few Gyr. Despite this, there is no evidence for a young stellar population in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4278 as a low-luminosity AGN with radio and X-ray jet emission, parsec-scale relativistic jets, and possible CTA-detectable gamma rays if jet domination holds.

quotes.quote: We focus on NGC 4278 as an example of LLAGNs and demonstrate expected ray flux based on a SSC model on the assumption that non-thermal radiation from jets is dominated. We also survey parameter space in the model and constrain physical parameters required in jets. NGC 4278 is a LLAGN with z = 0.002 and the distance of 16.7 Mpc Tonry2001ApJ546p681 for H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , which is Hubble constant. This source is sometimes categorized into LINER or radio-loud LLAGNs. Recent observations in radio bands have revealed a two-sided relativistic parsec scale jet ( 0.75 ) closely aligned to the line of sight ( 2^ 4^ ), where and are the velocity in the unit of speed of light and the viewing angle of the jet, respectively Giroletti2005ApJ622p178 .
The integral flux for R = 10^ 16 cm is above the CTA sensitivity at 100 GeV. Thus, CTA can test the jet domination of the total emission in this source in the case of R 10^ 16 cm.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4278 as an L* elliptical with a rich globular cluster system showing color bimodality, a blue tilt, radial color gradients, and size trends with color and radius.

quotes.quote: We use multi-pointing HST ACS and wide field Subaru Suprime-Cam imaging to study the globular cluster system of the L* elliptical galaxy NGC 4278. We have also obtained a handful of new globular cluster spectra with Keck/DEIMOS. We determine the globular cluster surface density profile and use it to calculate the total number of globular clusters, finding the system to be slightly more populous than average for galaxies of its luminosity.

We find clear evidence for bimodality in the globular cluster colour distribution and for a colour--magnitude relation in the blue subpopulation (a `blue tilt'). We also find negative radial colour gradients in both colour subpopulations of equal strength which are similar in strength to those reported in other galaxies. The sizes of NGC 4278's globular clusters decrease with redder colours and increase with galactocentric radius.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC4278, an intermediate-mass elliptical with LMXB and globular-cluster inhomogeneities, including arc-like features and possible streamers suggesting past accretion.

quotes.quote: Here we report the case of a third galaxy, NGC4278, where we have been able to detect significant inhomogeneity in both the LMXB and the GC 2D distributions. NGC4278 is an elliptical galaxy at a distance of 16 Mpc tonry2001 , which was the subject of deep Chandra observations, resulting in a well-characterized catalog of LMXBs hereinafter B09 brassington2009 . This galaxy is an intermediate-size elliptical, with stellar mass smaller than the giant elliptical NGC4261 and NGC4649 see for a compilation of properties boroson2011 , which were the subjects of companion studies dabrusco2013a,dabrusco2013b . The spatial 2D distribution of LMXBs in NGC4278 (see Figure 1 in B09) suggests an arc-like feature and possible ``streamers'', motivating the present investigation.

In order to get a comprehensive picture of the properties of the GCs population in NGC4278, in this paper we investigate the projected spatial distribution of both candidate U13 GCs and the GC catalog from B09. We discuss the datasets in Section sec:data ; we present the results of our analysis of the spatial distribution of LMXBs and GCs in the central region of the galaxy NGC4278 and at larger radii in Section sec:results , and examine their astrophysical implications in Section sec:discussion .

sources.summary: The paper uses deep optical, globular-cluster, and X-ray data to show NGC 4278 is relaxed while red and blue clusters trace stellar and total mass differently.

quotes.quote: We present new, deep optical observations of the early-type galaxy NGC 4278, which is located in a small loose group. We find that the galaxy lacks fine substructure, i.e., it appears relaxed, out to a radius of 70 kpc. Our g and i band surface brightness profiles are uniform down to our deepest levels of 28 mag arcsec ^ 2 . This spans an extremely large radial range of more than 14 half-mass radii.

Combined with archival globular cluster (GC) number density maps and a new analysis of the total mass distribution obtained from archival Chandra X-ray data, we find that the red GC subpopulation traces well the stellar mass density profile from 2.4 out to even 14 half-mass radii, while the blue GC subpopulation traces the total mass density profile of the galaxy over a large radial range. Our results reinforce the scenario that red GCs form mostly in-situ along with the stellar component of the galaxy, while the blue GCs are more closely aligned with the total mass distribution in the halo and were accreted along with halo matter.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4278 as a LHAASO-detected LINER-type AGN, discusses its possible parsec-scale jet acceleration site, and finds its hard X-ray spectrum favors a jet origin.

quotes.quote: Recently, the Large High Altitude Air Shower Observatory (LHAASO) collaboration present the first LHAASO catalog of VHE ray sources, five of which are AGNs 2023arXiv230517030C . These five AGNs include four high-synchrotron-peaked HSP; 2010ApJ...710.1271A blazars, which are Mrk 421, Mrk 501, 1ES 1727+502 and 1ES 2344+514, and a Liner-type AGN named NGC 4278.

Although Fermi telescope does not detect GeV photons from NGC 4278 The space science data center (http://tools.ssdc.asi.it/SED/) shows that the GeV emission of NGC 4278 was detected by EGRET and AGILE, but no reference is provided. , the parsec-scale jet discoved by radio observation is still a possible site for the acceleration of relativistic particles 2004AJ....127..119L, 2005ApJ...622..178G . The newly LHAASO observation sheds light on the VHE radiation mechanism of AGNs.

For the Liner-type AGN NGC 4278, the jet/RIAF and the thin accretion disk may alternately dominate as the origin of the radiation, depending on the strength of its X-ray emission 2010A&A...517A..33Y . In our data analysis, the discovered hard X-ray power-law spectrum favours the jet origin.

sources.summary: The paper reports TeV gamma-ray emission associated with the low-luminosity LINER AGN NGC 4278, finding variability, an active-period spectrum, and evidence that its weak jet accelerates particles efficiently.

quotes.quote: The best-fit position of the TeV source ( RA =185.05^ 0.04^ , Dec =29.25^ 0.03^ ) is compatible with NGC 4278 within 0.03 degree. Variation analysis shows an indication of the variability at a few months level in the TeV band, which is consistent with low frequency observations. Based on these observations, we report the detection of TeV ray emissions from this low-luminosity AGN NGC 4278.

The observations by LHAASO-WCDA during active period has a significance level of 8.8 with best-fit photon spectral index =2.56 0.14 and a flux f_ 1-10 TeV =(7.0 1.1_ sta 0.35_ syst ) 10^ 13 photons cm^ 2 s^ 1 , or approximately 5 of the Crab Nebula. The discovery of VHE from NGC 4278 indicates that the compact, weak radio jet can efficiently accelerate particles and emit TeV photons.

NGC 4278, a typical low-luminosity AGN classified as a LINER due to its weak H line, has a central black hole mass of 3 10^ 8 M_ at distance D_ L =16.4 Mpc 2001ApJ...546..681T , with an Eddington ratio of 5 10^ 6 2014A&A...569A..26H , indicative of RIAF.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC4278 as a strongly misaligned regular rotator with peculiar kinematics, declining mean velocity, incomplete radial coverage, and a central velocity-dispersion drop suggesting possible KDC classification.

quotes.quote: Of these only NGC4278 was not previously mentioned. Although this galaxy is classified as a RR, its kinematics show some peculiar signatures 1979ApJ...229..472S,1988ApJS...68..409D,1993ApJ...407..525V,2004MNRAS.352..721E : the mean velocity is decreasing towards the edge of the SAURON FoV and we do not cover the full effective radius. In that respect the rotation that we are seeing could also belong to a large KDC covering the FoV and it could change significantly outside the covered area (see discussion in Appendix A:cav ). This galaxy also shows a drop in the central velocity dispersion. All these suggest it is a special case and could be classified as a KDC.

sources.summary: The paper reports short-timescale and longer-term X-ray variability in NGC 4278, including a 10 percent flux rise over 1.5 hours and a fivefold long-term flux increase.

quotes.quote: Two sources in our sample exhibit significant short time-scale variability, both already reported in the literature (NGC 4278: Y10 and NGC 3226: binder09apj:ngc3226 ). NGC 4278 exhibited a short time-scale variability ( t 1.5 h) during the \ observation where the X-ray flux level was highest (compared to the other 6 \ observations, where no short time-scale variability was detected). During this 1.5 h time-scale, the flux of NGC 4278 increased by 10 . Y10 proposed that this variability could be the result of a more compact X-ray emission region, e.g. an accretion disk truncated at a lower radius during the \ observation compared to the \ observations.
with a 5 times flux increase in the most variable source, NGC 4278, and a 1.4 times flux increase in the least variable source, NGC 4143.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4278 using prior radio, Bondi-rate, and jet-orientation constraints, requiring a small transition radius and noting ADAF emission is needed even in the jet case.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 The radio emission of NGC 4278 was previously studied in the context of ADAF models by dmt01 , who found that s > 0 is required in order for the radio emission predicted by ADAF models not to overestimate the radio flux. dmt01 also estimated that the Bondi rate is m _ Bondi 0.001-0.01 . giro05 found a two-sided radio structure for the jet using VLA data, and estimated that the jet is oriented close to the line of sight ( 2^ i 4^ ) and mildly relativistic ( _j 1.5 ). In our jet models we therefore adopt i=3^ and _j = 1.5 . Both the AD and JD models require a truncated thin disk with a small transition radius ( r_ tr 30-40 ) in order to account for the near-IR data.

sources.summary: NGC 4278 is treated as a small round elliptical with a distorted H I disk, inner-only stellar rotation, rapidly falling dispersion, and mildly rotating metal-poor GCs.

quotes.quote: This well-studied small round elliptical (E1--2) contains a distorted H I disk Knapp:1978a . Our combined kinematic maps indicate that the stars are rotating only within 0.5 , with very weak to no rotation farther out. The velocity dispersion falls rapidly with radius. Pota:2013en did not find evidence that the metal-rich GCs rotate but concluded that the metal-poor GCs rotate mildly in the outer regions along a direction between the major and minor axes. The velocity dispersion of both GC subpopulations agrees broadly with the stellar kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4278 has variable Chandra normalization, soft and hard X-ray luminosity changes, a cross-instrument normalization variation, and no short-term variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 : The best fit for Chandra data is SMF1 with Norm_2 varying (30 ) in a one year period. An X-ray intrinsic luminosity variation at soft (hard) energy of 26 (29 ) is found. The contribution of the annular region in Chandra data amounts to 38 . When comparing XMM -Newton and Chandra data, a variation in the normalization of the PL (15 ) along two years is found. Short term variations are not detected.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4278 among six gas-rich ETGs with enormous H I ring or disc structures, red dusty inner regions, blue outer H I rings, and is the only one in this group not described as CO-poor.

quotes.quote: We note that six rich galaxies (NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 3945, NGC 4036, NGC 4278, and NGC 5582) in our sample have enormous \ ring or disc structures, while they have no \ in their very central regions (red circles in Fig. fig:derivedH ). While these galaxies show very red colours in the inner regions due to dust, they show blue colours in the outer regions where the highest \ column density rings are located 2017MNRAS.464..329Y . We would like to point out that these galaxies are also CO-poor with the exception of NGC 4278. Therefore, we conclude that the presence of a large \ disc or ring in an ETG indicates a dust structure in the centre, even if there is no \ in central regions. It would be interesting to continue to study these objects in future studies.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 4278 as a Virgo elliptical with dust, a blue nucleus, unusual stellar rotation, a large HI disk, and non-circular gas motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 is an E1-2 galaxy ( M_B = --19.9) located in the Virgo cluster. It contains large-scale dust, as well as a blue central point source (Carollo et al. \ 1997). Long-slit spectroscopy reveals a peculiar stellar rotation curve, rising rapidly at small distances from the nucleus and dropping to nearly zero beyond 30 (Davies Birkinshaw 1988; van der Marel Franx 1993). NGC 4278 also contains a massive HI disk extending beyond 10 R_e . The HI velocity field is regular but has non-perpendicular kinematic axes, indicating non-circular motions (Raimond et al \ 1981; Lees 1992).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4278 as having an integral-sign ionised-gas pattern, twisting misaligned stellar and gas kinematics, moderately high line ratios, and dust broadly aligned with the gas.

quotes.quote: The gas distribution in NGC 4278 displays a peculiar integral-sign pattern that is closely followed by the gas velocity field. The stellar and gas kinematics appear to be misaligned by increasingly wider angles, as they twist in opposite directions towards the outer parts of the field-of-view.

The ratio is moderately high in NGC 2768, NGC 2974, and NGC 4278, while it is very low in NGC 4526. In this last galaxy, the location of the low / \ values corresponds to the location of a prominent dust disk (as shown in the unsharp-masked image), and suggests ongoing star formation. In this respect, there seems to be a clear link between the ionised structures found in the / \ maps with those seen in the \ equivalent width and kinematic maps (i.e. ring in NGC 2974, or integral-sign pattern in NGC 4278).
The dust morphology is less defined in NGC 2768 and NGC 4278, but still suggests an orientation for the dust that is similar to that of the gas distribution.

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 4278 as the closest radio galaxy in the sample, with stronger CO(2-1), broader emission, and irregular dust suggesting recent gas accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278: Also detected by . The line emission is stronger in CO(2-1) than in CO(1-0), with a broader width. Note that this is the closest radio galaxy of all (9 Mpc). According to the dust morphology across the disk consist in irregular patches or lanes; this suggests that this galaxy has recently accreted its gas Sarzi06 .

sources.summary: NGC 4278 is included through two SED states; the paper compares its radio emission to radio-loud quasars and identifies its lowest X-ray fluxes as fundamental-plane exceptions.

quotes.quote: Two SEDs were reported in the NGC 4278 case, one corresponding to the high state simultaneous X-ray and UV \ fluxes and one representing the contemporary optical and X-ray (obs. ID: 7081) fluxes (see younes10aa:ngc4278 ).

Two of the sources, NGC 315 and NGC 4278, exhibit radio emission comparable to radio-loud quasars. The radio-loudness parameter R_ X indicates that all of the six LINER 1s could be considered as radio-loud sources having R_ X >-4.5 (see rad-prop-sec ).

At the 1 level, our sample follow well the authors fundamental plane, with the only exception being NGC 2787 and the lowest NGC 4278 X-ray fluxes (note, however, that these exceptions disappear at the 2 level).

sources.summary: The paper detects hot X-ray gas in NGC4278, measuring its central mass fraction and interpreting its bipolar morphology as evidence for an outflow powered by evolved stars and supernovae.

quotes.quote: We detect hot X-ray gas with a temperature of kT 0.35 keV in NGC4278, the component of which has a steeper surface brightness distribution than the stellar light. Within the central 50 ( 3.9 kpc) the estimated gas mass is 3 10^ 7 \ M_ , implying a gas mass fraction of 0.06 . We demonstrate that the X-ray gas exhibits a bipolar morphology in the northeast-southwest direction, indicating that it may be outflowing from the galaxy. The mass and energy budget of the outflow can be maintained by evolved stars and SNe Ia, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper links B2 1217+29/NGC 4278 to low-redshift photometric scaling issues, a YSP UV decline, and multi-temperature dust emission.

quotes.quote: B2 1217+29 (NGC 4278). The fit with a YSP has a rather high ^2 value that is due to the systematically higher 2MASS points, as compared to the GALEX and SDSS data. We note that NGC 4278 is at very low redshift (z=0.0021), which probably causes this discrepancy. The decrease in luminosity from SDSS via NUV to FUV can be reproduced well by a YSP, but not by a power law. MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mixture of dust with temperatures of 20 K ( M_d = 1.5 10^5 M_ ), 40 K ( M_d = 7.0 10^3 M_ ) and 180 K ( M_d = 1.0 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4278 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This a rather round elliptical galaxy (E1-2), member of the Coma I group. The ATLAS^ 3D kinematic map 2011MNRAS.414.2923K shows that stars are regularly rotating only within 0.5 along a direction slightly offset from the photometric major axis, while there is very weak or no rotation farther out. The kinemetry of shows that the galaxy does not significantly rotate beyond 1 . The PN velocity field does not show any significant rotation, hence also the kinematic position angle is not very well defined. 2013MNRAS.428..389P do not find any evidence of rotation for the metal rich GCs, while the blue metal poor GCs display a weak rotation in the outer regions, along a direction between the major and the minor axes.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4278 has a low-significance colour/CaT metallicity mismatch, evidence for a blue tilt with redder metal-poor GCs, and CaT metallicity bimodality.

quotes.quote: In five of our eleven galaxies (NGC 1400, NGC 1407, NGC 2768, NGC 3377 and NGC 4494) the KS test (Figure fig:zhist ) provides evidence that the CaT-based metallicities are not drawn from the same distribution as the colour-based metallicities while in a sixth (NGC 4278) the KS test says there is a low but not significant probability that the two metallicities are not drawn from the same distribution.

NGC 4278 is possibly another example of this effect as it also possesses a blue tilt (Usher et al. in prep.) and, like NGC 1407, shows weak evidence that the metal poor GCs are redder than in other galaxies (Figures fig:zhist g , fig:metalmetal g ).

Using GMM (Section GMM , Table tab:gmm-metal , Figure fig:metalmetal ), we detected CaT metallicity bimodality with a probability of greater than 0.95 in NGC 3115, NGC 4278 and NGC 4365 and greater than 0.90 in NGC 1407, NGC 5846 and NGC 3377.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4278's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4278 is a radio-loud dusty elliptical galaxy at a distance of D= 16.1 3.3 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a broad-line LINER nucleus (see pellegrini_agn_2012 for a recent detailed study). It features a compact flat-spectrum radio core with a parsec-scale two-sided jet (PA 40 ; giroletti_two-sided_2005,nagar_radio_2005 ). The emission extends to kiloparsec-scales (PA 70 ; sarzi_sauron_2006 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports a broad velocity-dispersion bump in NGC 4278 and notes that this early-type galaxy has a large HI disk and a dusty patch.

quotes.quote: For NGC 4278 (GCs data from pota:2013MNRAS.428..389P , stellar data from marel:1993ApJ...407..525V ) we observe a bump in a large range: from 1.5 to 3.8 ( = 2.58 kpc). This galaxy has a large HI disk and a dusty patch goudfrooij:1994A&AS..104..179G .
Observations of some ETGs such as NGC 821, NGC 1407, NGC 3115, NGC 4278 and NGC 4697 show a positive deviation of the velocity dispersion in the same range as our galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 4278 with brighter SLUGGS hosts and finds its globular clusters have CaT strengths weaker than color-based predictions at both blue and red colors.

quotes.quote: At blue colours the GCs of NGC 1407 and NGC 4278 have weaker CaT values than predicted by Equation eq:CaT_colour . For red colours, NGC 1407, NGC 4278, NGC 4365 and NGC 4649 GCs have weaker CaT strengths than predicted from their colours while NGC 3115, NGC 3377, NGC 4473 and NGC 4494 have stronger than predicted CaT values. The shapes of the colour--CaT relations of NGC 1407 and NGC 4494 are radically different as first noticed by 2011MNRAS.415.3393F . As seen in Figure fig:stacking_CaT1 , at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show bluer colours compared to the other galaxies while at high CaT values NGC 3377 and NGC 4494 also show bluer colours than the other galaxies. Additionally, at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show higher Na82 strengths than the other galaxies.
We included NGC 3115, NGC 3377, NGC 4473 and NGC 4494 in one group and NGC 1407, NGC 4278, NGC 4365 and NGC 4649 in another.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4278 as having declining angular momentum, movement across the fast/slow-rotator dividing plane, and noisy slow-rotation-driven position-angle variation.

quotes.quote: Arnold14 reported cases of declining angular momentum with radius in the galaxies NGC 821, NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4278.
In fact, NGC 4278, moves back and forth through the dividing plane.

Other galaxies show strong variations in PA_ kin (NGC 3607, NGC 4278, NGC 4473) either because the kinematic solution is noisy due to slow rotation (NGC 4278) or as a result of multiple components such as kinematically distinct haloes (KDH, e.g. NGC 3607 and NGC 4473) as can be seen in Fig. fig:kinemetry .
