quotes.quote: NGC 4388 NGC 4388 is also a highly inclined galaxy hosting a Seyfert type 2 nucleus philips1982 . X-ray studies of NGC 4388 show the presence of an ample amount of extended soft X-ray emission, which is generated as a result of photoionization by the nuclear activity iwasawa2003,risaliti2002,cappi2006,beckmann2004 . Ionized gas studies also show similar extended features, for instance, find a conical shaped ionized region. NGC 4388 is well studied in radio wavelengths, all of which show a double source slightly away from the center of the galaxy stone1988, hummel1991, irwin2000, condon1987 .

sources.summary: The paper detects lower-significance extended mid-IR emission in NGC 4388, finds its orientation consistent with polar dust, and cites larger-scale molecular outflow evidence.

quotes.quote: The PSF-subtracted images of six out of the twelve galaxies present bright extended mid-IR emission (Fig. fig:midIRimages ). These are NGC 1365, NGC 3227, NGC 5506, NGC 5643, NGC 6300, and NGC 7582. The contours show that the extended emission is detected up to at least a 20 _ bk level. For NGC 4388 we also detected extended mid-IR emission at a lower significance but in a similar orientation as that derived by using a different dataset. The sizes of the extended mid-IR emission range from approximately 50 pc in NGC 6300 to 190 pc in NGC 5506.

The extended mid-IR emission in NGC 3227, NGC 4388, and NGC 7582 is more prominent on the optical bright side of the ionization cone. In NGC 1365, NGC 5506, and NGC 5643 the mid-IR extended emission is more symmetric around the AGN position and approximately along the direction of the ionization cone.

NGC 3227, NGC 4388 and NGC 7582 as well as NGC 1068 show evidence of molecular outflows on physical scales larger than the torus up to a few hundred parsecs, see and GB21 Davies2014, GarciaBurillo2019, DominguezFernandez2020 , which are more likely due to the interaction between the AGN wind and/or a radio jet if present and molecular gas in the disk of the host galaxy. NGC 5643 and NGC 5506 appear to have extended both polar and equatorial mid-IR emission. In NGC 6300 the mid-IR emission might be due to dust in the equatorial direction of the torus as well as in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4388 as one of two X-ray/Hα scale-height outliers, known for enormous Hα and X-ray filaments whose differing structures can bias scale-height comparisons.

quotes.quote: Although there are only six galaxies with reliable measurements in both H and X-ray, it seems that most galaxies tend to have an X-ray scale height smaller than the H scale height, except for two outliers: NGC 4388 and NGC 3079 (Fig. fig:hvshX ). These two galaxies are well known for their extremely large H and X-ray filaments extending to a few tens of kpc above the disk (e.g., Cecil01, Yoshida02, Iwasawa03, Strickland04a, Kenney08, Li19, HodgesKluck20 ). However, the H filaments always appear much thinner than the coherent X-ray filaments, because they are intrinsically denser and less volume filling, and also because the H images typically have higher angular resolutions than the X-ray images. Therefore, these large scale filaments may not contribute significantly in our H scale height measurements, but they could largely affect the X-ray scale height.

sources.summary: NGC 4388 is treated as a Seyfert spiral whose AGN, outflows, and ram-pressure stripping shape its LOFAR radio morphology and trailing emission.

quotes.quote: VCC 836 (NGC 4388) This object hosts a Seyfert 2-type nucleus and is the only spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster where an AGN contributes a large fraction of the total radio emission. The sub-parsec nuclear jets are oriented perpendicular to the disk Giroletti2009THEVLBI and give rise to a bi-conical outflow Damas-Segovia2016CHANG-ES4388 that is barely resolved even in our high-resolution maps. Furthermore, the galaxy, which is believed to be post-core passage and moving to the southwest, is undergoing a ram-pressure stripping event Murphy2009 which generates a spectacular tail Oosterloo2005ACluster that can also be partly traced in H Yoshida2002 . In the LOFAR maps, this imprints in a strong intensity gradient on the leading-side and an extension of the emission beyond the stellar component towards the trailing-side of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4388 as a bright Sy2 hard-X-ray source, notes previous SIGMA and BATSE detections, and attributes its ROSAT non-detection to high intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: An iterative removal procedure was used to eliminate ``ghosts'' associated with the brightest sources (NGC 4151, NGC 4388 and Coma). No iterative removal of sources was performed for weak sources.

NGC 4388 was detected by SIGMA 1992A&A...264...22L and BATSE 1996A&AS..120C.559B . Most of positively identified INTEGRAL sources are also detected in the soft X-ray band in the ROSAT All-Sky Survey. The only exception is the Sy2 galaxy NGC 4388. This second-brightest INTEGRAL source is undetectable in the ROSAT energy band, most likely because of a high intrinsic absorption 2004ApJ...614..641B .

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 4388's western and northeastern tidal plumes, possible bridge connection, an extended HI cloud from ram-pressure stripping, and only faint optical counterparts.

quotes.quote: Other notable features include NGC 4388's tidal plumes to the west and northeast (E), a southern tail in NGC 4425 (F; both noted by Malin 1994) which connects to VCC 987, another tail extending from IC 3349 (G) and passing through VCC 942, and a loop to the east of IC 3475 (H).

We also see a common envelope of light (M) surrounding the complex of galaxies to the south of M86, including NGC 4413, IC 3363, and IC 3349, along with a tidal bridge connecting NGC 4413 and IC 3363, and perhaps extending to NGC 4388 to the east.

Finally, Oosterloo van Gorkom (2005) have recently identified an extended (110x25 kpc) HI cloud streaming from NGC 4388 which they attribute to ram-pressure stripping due to the lack of any stellar counterpart in previous photographic imaging. We also find no extended counterpart on our image down to a surface brightness limit of =28.5, save for faint streamers close to the main body of NGC 4388 coincident with the extended emission line region noted by Yoshida (2002).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4388 as a nearly edge-on Virgo spiral with a southeastern polarized-emission ridge, reduced polarized intensity elsewhere, and beam depolarization from a nuclear outflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 4388 shows a ridge of polarized radio continuum emission in the south eastern part of the nearly edge-on disk. We observe a decreased polarized intensity to the south west and north east of the galaxy center. This is due to beam depolarization caused by the nuclear outflow which is visible in H emission (Veilleux et al. 1999). Thus, it is not excluded that the south eastern ridge of polarized intensity continues towards the west and then turns to the north.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 4388, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 4388 This source varied between the XMM-Newton observations from July to December 2002, increasing in flux and decreasing in Fe K EW. Both observations were best fit by a double absorbed power law (allowing the normalization of the second power law component to vary between the two observations), a thermal component (necessary to fit the soft emission), and a Gaussian component to accommodate the Fe K line. The spectral shape of the local continuum (3 - 8 keV, to constrain the Fe K EW) for the December observation and Chandra observation required a base model of a double power law with an absorption component attenuating the second power law; a single power law base model was sufficient to fit the local continuum for the July observation.

sources.summary: NGC 4388 is cited as a Virgo galaxy with a large H I cloud whose stripping can be reproduced by ram-pressure simulations, despite tail-morphology discrepancies.

quotes.quote: Motivated by he discovery of a large ( M 3 10^8 ) HI cloud in the Virgo cluster that appears to originate from the galaxy NGC 4388 Osterloo.vanGorkom:2005 , Roediger.etal:2006 used hydrodynamic simulations to confirm that ram pressure is sufficient to strip this galaxy's gas mass. They report a distinctive flow pattern that they ascribe to von Karman oscillation, typically produced by the unsteady flow of a fluid past a blunt body, and suggest that the Osterloo.vanGorkom:2005 discovery is evidence of this. The authors do remark that the observed HI tail morphology does not match the simulation and suggest that this discrepancy is due to missing physics and ICM interaction.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4388's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4388 is an almost edge-on spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster phillips_ngc_1982 at a distance of D= 19.2 6.6 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a Sy 2 nucleus trippe_multi-wavelength_2010 with broad emission lines in polarized light shields_reality_1996 . The AGN is one of the X-ray brightest objects of its class in terms of flux. It is an X-ray ``buried" AGN candidate noguchi_new_2009 and belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4388 as a dusty edge-on Seyfert with a conical narrow-line region, megamaser, chaotic small-scale structure, and heavily absorbed continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4388 (S1.9) Classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy by Phillips82 and Seyfert 1.9 by Ho97 , NGC 4388 (Fig. emlines , f7 ) is one of the first galaxies in which a conically shaped NLR was detected Pogge88 . This AGN, which is host to an H _2 O megamaser Kuo11 , lies in an SB()a galaxy close to edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4388 as having a >2 sigma Ni-K detection, but describes the line as marginal and the Ni-K measurement as mild with low significance.

quotes.quote: Ni-K line was detected with >2 level for the Circinus galaxy, Cen A, MRK 3, NGC 4388, and NGC 4151.
the Circinus Galaxy, which has the highest Fe-K line flux Fuka11b , shows a clear Ni-K line, while some other AGNs show a marginal Ni-K line as NGC 4388.
Several other AGNs also provide a mild measurement for Cen A, MRK 3, NGC 4388, and NGC 4151, but the Ni-K line significance is not so high.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4388 as one of five objects with very large iron-K equivalent width because the reflection continuum is weak, interpreting its torus as Compton-thin and reproducible with a Monte-Carlo torus model.

quotes.quote: In the rest of objects (NGC 4388, NGC 5252, LEDA 170194, Mrk 520, and Mrk 915), the EW is very large ( >3 keV) because the reflection continuum (i.e., R ) is apparently very weak. We interpret that the tori in these objects are Compton-thin, thus producing a much weaker hump at 30 keV and absorption iron-K edge features than what the pexrav model predicts. In fact, we confirm that a Monte-Carlo based torus model by can well reproduce the spectra of these objects including the continuum and iron-K emission line, as done in , , and .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4388 to show how a dust band obscures the match before continuum subtraction, while pure [O III] morphology completely resembles the soft X-ray morphology.

quotes.quote: NGC 4388 : The continuum emission shows a dust band in the galaxy plane with a cone-shaped [ O iii ] morphology. If we compare [ O iii ] +continuum with the soft X-ray image, the good match is not clear as the dust band is still present. When we subtract the continuum to produce a pure [ O iii ] emission line image, the resemblance with the soft X-ray morphology is complete, as shown in Fig.  fig:subs_ngc4151_ngc4388 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4388's cutoff constraint agrees with a detailed study and a multiepoch result, while still allowing models above earlier lower limits.

quotes.quote: NGC 4388 -- For this target our constraint agrees very well with a more detailed study by ursini+2019-ngc2110+4388 , including \ and \ hard X-ray data. We also confirm 200 keV from a dedicated multiepoch study with a different spectral model (M. Balokovi et al., in prep.). The data used in our analysis do not strongly reject \ above lower limits found by deRosa+2012 and vasudevan+2013 as an acceptable model with > 5 can be found as long as >160 keV.

sources.summary: The paper reports far-off atomic gas around Virgo spiral NGC 4388, interpreting its H I observations and cluster passage as evidence for displaced stripped material.

quotes.quote: In addition, NGC 4388 represents one of the nearest Seyfert 2 galaxies and the first AGN to be found in the Virgo cluster (Phillips Malin 1982).
In this article we report the detection of atomic gas at a projected distance of more than 20 kpc northeastwards from the center of NGC 4388.

We have observed the Virgo cluster core galaxy NGC 4388 in the 21 cm H i line with the Effelsberg 100-m telescope at 5 different positions: one in the center and 4 located one 100-m beamsize to the NE, NW, SW, and SE from the galaxy center.
